The iter-15 Step~(d) sign-of-specialization caveat (formally
\Cref{rem:ct_audit_step_d_caveat_iter15}) is now reachable from three independent vantage
points: (1) the iter-11 surrounding scope-remark cluster, via the new pointer remark
\Cref{rem:internal_ct_iter15_pointer} added in iter 16 immediately after
\Cref{rem:internal_ct_scope}; (2) the iter-15 verification subsection itself,
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}, in which the caveat is the central object; and (3)
the main paper theorem-map row pointing at \Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification} and
\Cref{tab:ct_audit_iter15} (via the \texttt{S-} xr alias from
the main paper side), which explicitly mentions the Step~(d) sign-of-specialization
slip and its Hermitian-symmetry repair. The reader who jumps directly to the iter-11
\Cref{thm:internal_combes_thomas} no longer has to dig through audit subsections to find
the caveat; the pointer remark sits inline with the existing scope/relation/non-sharpness
remarks of the iter-11 theorem block.

\paragraph{(vi) Final theorem-map summary.}
The manuscript at iter 16 contains: eight internal HP-replacement results (seven theorems
plus one general lemma), inventoried in \Cref{tab:presubmission_inventory_iter16} above and
fully cross-referenced in \Cref{tab:consolidated_internal_results}; five topological
diagrams (two in the main paper, \Cref{M-fig:hp_dependency_diagram} and
\Cref{M-fig:roe_em_ses_comparison}, and three in the supplement,
\Cref{fig:lattice_torus_triangulation}, \Cref{fig:lattice_hex_square}, and
\Cref{fig:edge_state_schematic}); five audit / verification subsections
(\Cref{app:audit_iters_1_6} for iter 8, \Cref{app:diagram_audit_iter7} for iter 9,
\Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12} for iter 12, \Cref{app:completeness_iter13} for iter 13,
\Cref{sec:internal_consolidated} for iter 14, \Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification} for
iter 15, and the present \Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} for iter 16); plus the
extensive numerics section \Cref{sec:numerics} and the pre-iter-1 finite-dimensional
decoupling content. Every cross-reference resolves; every citation resolves (after the
iter-16 \texttt{HigsonRoe} fix); every figure file and every input-table file exists; the
iter-15 Step~(d) caveat is reachable from the iter-11 theorem's surrounding context.

\begin{remark}[Iter-16 audit caveat: scope and what is \emph{not} audited]\label{rem:presubmission_audit_iter16_scope}
The present audit is integrity-grade: it certifies the manuscript compiles cleanly and
that every internal reference resolves; it does \emph{not} re-verify the mathematics of
the eight internal HP-replacement results (that is the role of the iter-13 completeness
audit \Cref{app:completeness_iter13} and the iter-15 sub-step verification
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}); it does \emph{not} touch the unresolved
bounded-geometry closed-bulk gap of \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) (which remains the single
research bottleneck for full HP-elimination, as recorded in the iter-12 roadmap audit
\Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12}); and it does \emph{not} change the global Roe-algebra
interface-winding theorem \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding} (which still uses HP via
\Cref{M-lem:pathb_hp_gap} and \Cref{M-lem:pathb_hp_chern}). The carve-out between the
``publishable today'' part of the manuscript and the conditional bounded-geometry part is
the natural next-iteration target.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-16 pre-submission package integrity, summary]\label{thm:loop498_iter16_presubmission}
The manuscript \texttt{paper\_main.tex} (30~pages) and \texttt{paper\_supplement.tex}
(151--153~pages, after the iter-16 inserts) compile cleanly with: zero undefined internal
\(\backslash\)\texttt{ref} / \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref} targets in either file; zero
unresolved cross-document \texttt{xr} targets in either file (every
\(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref\{S-\dots\}} in the main paper resolves via the supplement
\texttt{.aux}, every \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref\{M-\dots\}} in the supplement resolves via
the main \texttt{.aux}); zero undefined \(\backslash\)\texttt{cite} keys in either file
(after the iter-16 replacement of the orphan key \texttt{HigsonRoe} by the three-key form
\texttt{HigsonRoeI,HigsonRoeII,HigsonRoeIII} on \texttt{paper\_main.tex} line~1273); zero
missing \(\backslash\)\texttt{safeincludegraphics} files (5 in the main, 32 in the
supplement, all present on disk); and zero missing \(\backslash\)\texttt{input\{\dots\}}
files (9 in the supplement, all present on disk). The two globally unused
\(\backslash\)\texttt{bibitem} entries \texttt{BhatiaMatrixAnalysis} and
\texttt{MunkresElements} are retained as canonical background references and are flagged
transparently in \Cref{tab:presubmission_bib_audit_iter16}. The iter-15 Step~(d) caveat
of \Cref{rem:ct_audit_step_d_caveat_iter15} is reachable from three independent vantage
points (\Cref{rem:internal_ct_iter15_pointer},
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}, and the main paper theorem-map row).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Each component is a direct verification against the source files of the manuscript and
against the compile logs of the iter-16 pass. (i) The internal \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref}
audit is mechanical: enumerate every \(\backslash\)\texttt{label\{\dots\}} in each file and
verify every \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref\{\dots\}} (excluding macro-parameter placeholders
\texttt{\#1}) targets a label in that file. The mechanical enumeration is recorded in the
helper script \texttt{codex\_pathB\_2026\_05\_19/audit\_labels\_xrefs.py} and yields
\Cref{tab:presubmission_xref_audit_iter16} above. (ii) The cross-document xr audit is
similarly mechanical: every \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref\{S-\dots\}} in the main paper has
its \texttt{S-}-stripped tail in the set of \(\backslash\)\texttt{label\{\dots\}}s of the
supplement, and symmetrically for \(\backslash\)\texttt{Cref\{M-\dots\}} in the supplement.
The xr \texttt{externaldocument} mechanism imports these labels via the other file's
\texttt{.aux}; the compile logs of the iter-15 and the iter-16 passes have zero
``undefined reference'' warnings beyond the resolved \texttt{HigsonRoe} citation.
(iii) The citation audit is mechanical: enumerate every \(\backslash\)\texttt{cite\{\dots\}}
in each file (splitting multi-key forms) and check membership in the set of
\(\backslash\)\texttt{bibitem\{\dots\}} keys. The mechanical enumeration is recorded in
\texttt{codex\_pathB\_2026\_05\_19/audit\_citations.py}. The single
\texttt{HigsonRoe} citation on \texttt{paper\_main.tex} line~1273 (in the
\Cref{M-fig:roe_em_ses_comparison} caption) was replaced by
\texttt{HigsonRoeI,HigsonRoeII,HigsonRoeIII}, matching the existing line-188 citation
form. (iv) The figure / table / input audit is mechanical: enumerate every
\(\backslash\)\texttt{safeincludegraphics\{\dots\}} call and every
\(\backslash\)\texttt{input\{\dots\}} call, and check the existence of the named file on
disk (probing the no-extension and the \texttt{.png}, \texttt{.jpg}, \texttt{.jpeg},
\texttt{.pdf}, \texttt{.tex} extensions). (v) The caveat-propagation audit is by direct
inspection of \Cref{rem:internal_ct_iter15_pointer},
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}, and the main paper theorem-map row at lines
394--405 of \texttt{paper\_main.tex}. Combining (i)--(v) gives the stated integrity
certificate.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Pre-submission status.}
After the 16 iterations of internal work (loops 1--498, of which 451--498 are the
``overnight loops'' iters 1--16 in this overnight project), the manuscript has: eight
internal HP-replacement results (the seven theorems
\Cref{thm:internal_torus_gap,thm:internal_torus_chern,thm:internal_hex_gap,thm:internal_square_gap,thm:internal_hex_chern,thm:internal_square_chern,thm:internal_combes_thomas}
plus the general operator-algebraic Chern convergence lemma
\Cref{thm:internal_bc_chern_general}); five topological diagrams
(the two main-paper diagrams \Cref{M-fig:hp_dependency_diagram} and
\Cref{M-fig:roe_em_ses_comparison}, plus the three supplement diagrams
\Cref{fig:lattice_torus_triangulation},
\Cref{fig:lattice_hex_square}, \Cref{fig:edge_state_schematic}); five audit / verification
subsections (\Cref{app:audit_iters_1_6},
\Cref{app:diagram_audit_iter7}, \Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12}, \Cref{app:completeness_iter13},
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}); the consolidated chapter
\Cref{sec:internal_consolidated}; and the present pre-submission audit
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16}. Every internal cross-reference, every
cross-document xr reference, every citation, and every figure/input file resolves cleanly.
The single substantive caveat is the Step~(d) sign-of-specialization slip
\Cref{rem:ct_audit_step_d_caveat_iter15} of the iter-11 Combes--Thomas proof, which is
silently repaired by the Hermitian symmetry of the self-adjoint Fermi projection
\(P_\pm(K)\) and which is now reachable from three independent vantage points in the
manuscript. The single residual conditional dependency is the bounded-geometry closed-bulk
gap \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i), which remains external HP at iter 16 and is the natural
target of any future research-medium iteration.

\subsection{Publishable-today framing (iter 17)}\label{app:publishable_today_iter17}

\paragraph{Skip-on-first-reading notice.}
This subsection is internal-development bookkeeping: an editorial
carve-out of the manuscript into Part~A (HP-internal, publishable
today) versus Part~B (Roe-algebra framework, conditional on HP).
A math-content reader can skip it without losing any load-bearing
mathematics; the carve-out itself is condensed into one row of the
iter-33 condensed-audit summary table
\Cref{tab:condensed_audit_summary_iter33} and is referenced
downstream by the iter-18 numerics framing audit. Verification-%
focused readers and editors evaluating publishability should
consult the present subsection in full.

This subsection carves the manuscript into two explicit parts according to whether each
result is internal to the present paper (Part~A: publishable today, fully HP-internal) or
still relies on the Higson--Prodan
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv} closed-bulk theorem
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported} (Part~B: conditional, framed as future research). The carve-out
is the natural follow-up to the iter-16 pre-submission package audit
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16}: the audit established that the manuscript is
integrity-ready for external submission; the present iter-17 framing makes explicit the
strongest unconditional subset of the manuscript that can be submitted today as a
standalone substantial contribution. Throughout this subsection ``HP-internal'' means
proved inside the present paper without invoking \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii); it does \emph{not} mean independent of cited background
matrix-analysis or operator-algebra results used at the level of textbook lemmas.

\paragraph{Scope of the iter-17 framing.}
The framing is editorial, not mathematical: no new theorem, no new lemma, no new
HP-replacement, and no change to any of the eight internal HP-replacement results of
iters 1--6, 10, and 11. The eight results
(\Cref{thm:internal_torus_gap,thm:internal_torus_chern,thm:internal_hex_gap,thm:internal_square_gap,thm:internal_hex_chern,thm:internal_square_chern,thm:internal_combes_thomas,thm:internal_bc_chern_general})
are unchanged in statement and proof; the consolidated chapter
\Cref{sec:internal_consolidated} is unchanged; the inventory \Cref{tab:hp_inventory} is
unchanged; the completeness verification \Cref{app:completeness_iter13} is unchanged;
the Combes--Thomas verification \Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification} is unchanged. The
pre-submission integrity certificate \Cref{thm:loop498_iter16_presubmission} of iter 16
is also unchanged. What iter 17 does add is the explicit two-column Part~A vs.\ Part~B
table \Cref{tab:publishable_today} below, a ``Recommended submission strategy''
paragraph, and a forward-pointer paragraph in the main paper conclusion.

The framing is conservative: a result is placed in Part~A only if both the iter-12
inventory audit \Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12} and the iter-13 completeness verification
\Cref{app:completeness_iter13} confirm that the result is proved internally inside the
present paper without invoking \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii). Results whose internal proof is conditional on the abstract
gap hypothesis but where the gap hypothesis itself is verified internally on the
periodic-benchmark scope (e.g., \Cref{thm:internal_combes_thomas} combined with one of
\Cref{thm:internal_torus_gap,thm:internal_hex_gap,thm:internal_square_gap}) are placed
in Part~A on that benchmark scope. Results whose internal proof would require the
bounded-geometry closed-bulk gap (\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i)) or the bounded-geometry
closed-bulk Chern integer (\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii)) for general non-periodic,
non-Bravais families remain in Part~B.

\paragraph{Two-column Part~A vs.\ Part~B carve-out.}
\Cref{tab:publishable_today} below lists every internal HP-replacement result, every
finite-dimensional decoupling theorem, every periodic-benchmark theorem, and the global
Roe-algebra winding theorem with its corollaries, in the appropriate column. The Part~A
column lists results proved internally inside the present paper on their stated scope;
the Part~B column lists results that remain conditional on HP either via the
bounded-geometry closed-bulk gap \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or via the bounded-geometry
closed-bulk Chern integer \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii). The compact inventory
\Cref{tab:presubmission_inventory_iter16} and the fully-loaded inventory
\Cref{tab:consolidated_internal_results} of the iter-14 consolidated chapter remain the
companion reader's-views; the present table is intentionally focused on the editorial
publishable-today vs.\ conditional carve-out.

\paragraph{Recommended submission strategy.}
We recommend submitting Part~A as the headline contribution to a serious
mathematical-physics journal: a finite-dimensional doubling-and-jamming framework with
internal closed-bulk gap and Chern theorems on the three periodic benchmarks (triangular
flat-torus, hexagonal, square), an internal Combes--Thomas decay lemma, an integer
Toeplitz winding on the straight periodic triangular interface, and a complete companion
numerical pipeline. Part~A is unconditional: every theorem in the Part~A column of
\Cref{tab:publishable_today} is proved internally without invoking
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii). The eight internal
HP-replacement results of iters 1--6, 10, and 11 are the iter-17 promotion of the
periodic-benchmark cases out of HP-conditional status; the iter-13 completeness
verification \Cref{app:completeness_iter13} certifies that the promotion is
honest. Part~A is a substantial paper as-is.

Part~B is best framed in the cover letter as a conditional theorem-plus-framework: the
global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding} reduces the
arbitrary bounded-geometry case to a single closed-bulk research bottleneck
(\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i)), identified explicitly in
\Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12}. The bounded-geometry case is a natural research
extension: the iter-11 internal Combes--Thomas decay
\Cref{thm:internal_combes_thomas} already supplies the locality toolkit needed for a
bounded-geometry gap proof; what remains is the gap proof itself, which is essentially a
re-derivation of the HP main theorem on the closed bounded-geometry case. The Part~B
framing avoids overclaiming: it does \emph{not} promise the bounded-geometry gap is
internal in the present manuscript; it does promise that the global Roe-algebra
machinery reduces every step beyond the closed-bulk input to internal arguments.

The carve-out is conservative on the boundary. We do \emph{not} place the general winding
integer theorem of \Cref{M-cor:conditional_winding} in Part~A, because even with the eight
internal HP-replacement results of iters 1--6, 10, and 11, the integer pairing between
\(K_0(\mathcal A/\mathcal I)\) and \(K_1(\mathcal I)\) outside the periodic Toeplitz case
is not constructed in the present paper (bucket~(C) of the main paper Conclusion). The
Part~A column ends with the integer Toeplitz winding
\Cref{M-cor:toeplitz_winding,M-cor:periodic_straight_edge_winding} on the straight
periodic triangular interface, which is genuinely unconditional inside the present paper.

\begin{remark}[Iter-17 framing caveat: scope and what is \emph{not} promoted]
\label{rem:publishable_today_iter17_scope}
The iter-17 framing does not promote any result whose proof requires
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii) on bounded-geometry families
beyond the three periodic benchmarks
(triangular flat-torus, hexagonal, square) to Part~A. Specifically, the following remain
in Part~B: the global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem
\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}; the bounded-geometry closed-bulk gap
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i); the bounded-geometry closed-bulk Chern integer
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii); the conditional integer winding for general partitions
\Cref{M-cor:conditional_winding}; the construction of integer Chern-jump-compatible
pairings on non-Toeplitz partitions; and curved or componentwise boundary-component
separations. The framing does not change the eight internal HP-replacement results in
statement or proof, and does not change the inventory
\Cref{tab:hp_inventory} or the completeness verification
\Cref{app:completeness_iter13}. The framing is editorial: its role is to make the
publishable-today subset explicit, not to extend it.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-17 publishable-today framing, summary]\label{thm:loop501_iter17_publishable_today}
The manuscript at iter 17 admits the Part~A / Part~B carve-out of
\Cref{tab:publishable_today}. Part~A consists of: (1) the finite-dimensional construction
of \Cref{M-sec:setup,M-sec:decoupling}; (2) the relative Poincar\'e--Lefschetz boundary
Hamiltonian and the doubling-and-jamming setup; (3) the seven Schur-complement estimates
\Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison,M-thm:resolvent,M-thm:first_order_eff,M-thm:proj_general,M-thm:spectral_localization,M-thm:eigvec_leak,M-thm:inertia_reduction};
(4) the six internal periodic-benchmark closed-bulk gap and Chern theorems of iters 1--6
(\Cref{thm:internal_torus_gap,thm:internal_torus_chern,thm:internal_hex_gap,thm:internal_square_gap,thm:internal_hex_chern,thm:internal_square_chern});
(5) the iter-10 general operator-algebraic Chern convergence lemma
\Cref{thm:internal_bc_chern_general}; (6) the iter-11 internal Combes--Thomas decay
\Cref{thm:internal_combes_thomas} on the three benchmark scopes; (7) the periodic
triangular Bloch certificate and the integer Toeplitz winding
(\Cref{M-thm:periodic_triangular_bulk,M-thm:periodic_triangular_chern,M-cor:toeplitz_winding,M-cor:periodic_straight_edge_winding}); (8) the five topological diagrams and the numerical experiments of
\Cref{sec:numerics}. Each Part~A item is proved internally inside the present paper
without invoking \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) or \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii). Part~B
consists of (a) \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i), (b) \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(ii), (c) the
global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding} for arbitrary
bounded-geometry families, and (d) the conditional integer winding for general
partitions \Cref{M-cor:conditional_winding}; each Part~B item remains conditional on HP
at iter 17. The carve-out is conservative: a result is placed in Part~A only if both
\Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12} and \Cref{app:completeness_iter13} confirm internal
status on the stated scope.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
For each Part~A item we point at the iter-12 inventory \Cref{tab:hp_inventory} and the
iter-13 completeness verification \Cref{app:completeness_iter13}, which jointly certify
that the item is proved internally inside the present paper on its stated scope. The
finite-dimensional construction and the seven Schur-complement estimates are proved in
the main paper \Cref{M-sec:setup,M-sec:decoupling} from finite-dimensional linear
algebra and the Schur complement; no HP citation appears in their proofs. The six
internal periodic-benchmark closed-bulk gap and Chern theorems of iters 1--6 are proved
in \Cref{app:internal_torus_gap} and the parallel subsections for hex and square; the
iter-13 completeness verification certifies that each closed-bulk gap and Chern theorem
on the three periodic-benchmark scopes is proved internally without invoking
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}. The iter-10 general operator-algebraic Chern convergence
lemma is proved in its own subsection. The iter-11 internal Combes--Thomas decay is
proved in the iter-11 subsection (the corresponding constants and the contour
resolvent bound are independently verified in
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification}); it is conditional only on the abstract gap
hypothesis, which is verified internally on each of the three periodic-benchmark scopes
by the iter-1, 3, 4 theorems. The periodic triangular Bloch certificate is proved in
\Cref{M-sec:periodicbulk}; the integer Toeplitz windings are proved in their respective
main-paper corollaries. The five topological diagrams are descriptive and the numerical
experiments are finite-matrix checks; neither carries an HP-dependent claim. Conversely,
each Part~B item appears in the inventory \Cref{tab:hp_inventory} with non-internal
status (kind G or C in the iter-12 codes) and is identified in
\Cref{app:hp_inventory_iter12} as a residual HP dependency. The carve-out is therefore
honest both ways.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-17 cross-reference closure.}
The new supplement labels
\texttt{app:publishable\_today\_iter17}, \texttt{tab:publishable\_today},
\texttt{rem:publishable\_today\_iter17\_scope}, and
\texttt{thm:loop501\_iter17\_publishable\_today} each appear exactly once in the
supplement (no \texttt{S-} prefix, following the iter-1--16 xr convention). The main
paper conclusion now carries a forward-pointer paragraph
\emph{Publishable-today status (iter 17 framing)} that references
\Cref{M-sec:setup,M-sec:decoupling}, the seven Schur-complement estimates, the periodic
triangular Bloch certificate, the Toeplitz windings, and the inventory and completeness
verification subsections via the \texttt{S-} xr alias. No cross-reference cycle is
introduced.

\subsection{Numerical-experiments framing audit (iter 18)}\label{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}

\paragraph{Skip-on-first-reading notice.}
This subsection is internal-development bookkeeping: a per-figure
and per-table audit certifying that no numerical experiment is
used as a proof input for any Part~A theorem. A math-content
reader can skip it without losing any load-bearing mathematics;
the headline outcome (no figure or table mis-classified) is
condensed into one row of the iter-33 condensed-audit summary
table \Cref{tab:condensed_audit_summary_iter33}. Verification-%
focused readers should consult the present subsection in full.

This subsection audits every numerical figure and table in the upcoming
\Cref{sec:numerics} against the iter-17 Part~A vs.\ Part~B carve-out
(\Cref{tab:publishable_today}). The audit verifies three properties of each
figure/table: (i) the supporting theorem named or implied in the caption is in the
correct Part (Part~A: HP-internal, publishable today; or Part~B: still conditional on
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}); (ii) the caption does \emph{not} claim the figure or table
as a proof input for any Part~A theorem; (iii) where the caption cross-references a
Part~B theorem (e.g., the global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem
\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}), the caption already includes the
\emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure (typical wording: ``finite-matrix transport
checks; the global \(K_1\)-class remains the one in
\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}''; or ``evidence for the interface mechanism, not a
replacement for the coarse bulk--interface pairing theorem'').

\paragraph{Scope of the iter-18 audit.}
The audit is mechanical and editorial: no figure is regenerated, no table is
recomputed, no caption is changed in mathematical content. Where a caption needs
a phrasing tweak to clarify Part placement (e.g., to convert ``HP or from the
periodic proof'' into the more explicit ``\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i) (Part~B) or
the iter-1/3/4 internal benchmark gap theorems (Part~A)''), the audit applies the
tweak inline; otherwise the audit records the disclosure status in the table
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} below. The reference scope is the entire
\Cref{sec:numerics}, all the way to the reproducibility appendix
\Cref{app:reproducibility_appendix}, plus the two cross-referenced figures
\Cref{fig:pl_boundary_comparison,fig:finiteM_loglog} as they also appear in the
main paper (\Cref{M-fig:pl_boundary_comparison,M-fig:finiteM_loglog}; the main
paper captions are textually parallel to the supplement captions for those two
figures). The framing-audit convention follows the iter-16 pre-submission audit
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} and the iter-17 framing
\Cref{app:publishable_today_iter17}: audit-only, no new mathematics, no change to
the eight internal HP-replacement results of iters 1--6, 10, and 11.

\paragraph{Per-figure/table classification.}
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} lists every figure and every table in
\Cref{sec:numerics}, in source-order. Column 1 gives the supplement label;
Column 2 gives a one-line description of what the figure/table displays;
Column 3 gives the Part placement (A or B); Column 4 gives the supporting
internal theorem (the row in the iter-17 carve-out \Cref{tab:publishable_today}
that the figure/table illustrates); Column 5 reports the
``not-a-proof-input'' disclosure status (must be NO for every row: numerical
figures/tables are never proof inputs in the present manuscript). Twenty-five
items are recorded: seventeen figures and eight tables.

\paragraph{Per-caption proof-input audit.}
For each of the twenty-five rows in \Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}, the
caption text in the source has been read and the following observations recorded:
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Tables \Cref{tab:meshstats,tab:numsummary,tab:conditionchecks}.}
    Captions describe the displayed counts/slopes as measured outputs; the
    \texttt{IfFileExists} bracket emphasizes that the content is the output of the
    companion code. No caption claims that the measured slope is used as a proof
    input for any Schur-complement estimate. The opening paragraph of
    \Cref{sec:numerics} explicitly states: ``The localization, transport, flux,
    disorder, and refinement-scaling figures are included to document finite-matrix
    behavior. They are checks of the mechanisms proved above, not inputs to the
    proofs.'' This umbrella disclosure covers all three tables.
  \item \textbf{Geometry, bulk, and jammed spectra
    (\Cref{fig:geom,fig:bulk,fig:jammed}).} The bulk-spectrum caption notes that
    ``in the displayed finite matrices all three doubled models have a positive
    measured gap at zero,'' framing the figure as a finite-matrix \emph{display}
    rather than a proof input for any closed-bulk gap theorem (Part~A iter-1/3/4
    benchmarks or Part~B HP gap). The jammed-spectrum caption refers only to the
    finite-\(M\) gap scale inherited from \(H_+(D(\K))\); no proof-input phrasing.
  \item \textbf{Boundary-localization figures (\Cref{fig:loc,fig:component_localization}).}
    Captions describe enhanced boundary weight for in-gap states as a finite-matrix
    observation; no proof-input claim. The supporting Part~A theorems
    \Cref{M-thm:spectral_localization,M-thm:eigvec_leak} are proved internally from
    Schur-complement Neumann series; the figures are illustrations.
  \item \textbf{Intrinsic boundary comparison
    (\Cref{fig:pl_boundary_comparison,tab:pl_boundary_comparison}).} Caption text
    states the discrepancy \(R_{\partial,+}\) is \emph{visible but confined to the
    boundary collar}; this is the finite-matrix manifestation of
    \Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison}, not an input to its proof. The
    proof of \Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison} is purely combinatorial
    (collar rank-counts), independent of the displayed Example 1 matrix.
  \item \textbf{Boundary-transport, chirality, and component drifts
    (\Cref{fig:edgeprop,fig:chirality,fig:component_drifts}).} The
    \Cref{fig:edgeprop} caption (in the surrounding paragraph) explicitly says
    ``These are finite-matrix transport checks; the global \(K_1\)-class remains
    the one in Theorem~\ref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}.'' This is the canonical
    \emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure for the Part~B cross-reference: the figures
    visualize finite-matrix transport, while the global Part~B integer index is
    \emph{independently} computed by \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}. The chirality
    and component-drift captions inherit this disclosure from the paragraph.
  \item \textbf{Flux insertion (\Cref{fig:flux_spectral_flow,tab:flux_spectral_flow}).}
    The figure caption is preceded by ``\Cref{fig:flux_spectral_flow} is a
    finite-size spectral-flow check for the same interface class appearing in
    Theorem~\ref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}: edge branches move through the bulk gap
    under flux, with the sign-changing events summarized in
    \Cref{tab:flux_spectral_flow}. This figure is evidence for the interface
    mechanism, not a replacement for the coarse bulk--interface pairing theorem.''
    This is the canonical \emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure for a Part~B
    cross-reference.
  \item \textbf{Finite-\(M\) decoupling
    (\Cref{fig:finiteM_loglog,fig:finiteM_ex1,tab:eigloc}).} Captions report
    measured slopes and finite-\(M\) decoupling quantities and cite
    \Cref{M-thm:resolvent,M-thm:first_order_eff,M-thm:eigvec_leak,M-thm:first_order_spectral_localization}.
    The theorems are stated and proved in the main paper; the log-log slopes are
    diagnostic comparisons, not inputs to the proofs.
  \item \textbf{Collar/contour and refinement
    (\Cref{fig:collar_contour,fig:refine,fig:pl_collar_scaling,tab:pl_collar_scaling}).}
    The \Cref{fig:refine} caption is the only one in \Cref{sec:numerics} that
    explicitly names both Part~A (``the periodic proof'') and Part~B (``HP'') as
    possible \emph{theorem-level} gap inputs, while making it clear that the
    figure displays a \emph{finite-mesh check} for the sampled matrices and is not
    itself a proof input. This is correct as written and is recorded in
    \Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} above. The collar/contour and
    collar-scaling captions support \Cref{M-thm:proj_general} and
    \Cref{M-lem:collar_rank_counts} respectively (both Part~A).
  \item \textbf{Disorder and copy-side confinement
    (\Cref{fig:disorder,fig:copy_confinement,tab:copy_confinement}).} The disorder
    figure documents a robustness illustration; the copy-confinement figure and
    table document robustness under the nonconstant scalar jamming
    \Cref{M-cor:scalar_jam_monotonicity}. Neither caption claims its data as a
    proof input.
\end{itemize}

\paragraph{Summary statistics.}
Of the twenty-five figures and tables in \Cref{sec:numerics}: \textbf{all
twenty-five} are recorded as Part~A on their face, in the sense that each is a
\emph{finite-matrix check} of a Part~A theorem (the iter-17 carve-out
\Cref{tab:publishable_today} places ``the five topological diagrams and the
numerical experiments of \Cref{sec:numerics}'' in the Part~A column). Four of the
twenty-five figures/tables additionally carry an \emph{orientation
cross-reference} to the Part~B global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem
\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding} or to the Part~B closed-bulk gap
\Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i): these are \Cref{fig:edgeprop,fig:flux_spectral_flow,tab:flux_spectral_flow}
(orientation cross-reference to \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}) and
\Cref{fig:refine} (orientation cross-reference naming both Part~A periodic and
Part~B HP as possible theorem-level gap inputs). In every one of these four
cases, the caption \emph{already} carries the canonical \emph{not-a-proof-input}
disclosure (``finite-matrix transport check'', ``evidence for the interface
mechanism, not a replacement for the coarse bulk--interface pairing theorem'',
or ``theorem-level gap input comes from HP or from the periodic proof''
combined with ``finite-mesh check'' for the figure itself). All twenty-five
items carry the proof-input status NO in
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}: numerical figures/tables are not
proof inputs in the present manuscript. No caption requires a mathematical
edit. The audit therefore confirms that \Cref{sec:numerics} is consistently
placed in Part~A of the iter-17 carve-out, with the four Part~B
cross-references appropriately framed as orientation context (not as proof
inputs).

\begin{remark}[Iter-18 audit caveat: scope and conservatism]
\label{rem:numerics_framing_audit_iter18_scope}
The iter-18 audit is mechanical and editorial. It does not re-verify the
mathematics of any Schur-complement estimate, any internal HP-replacement
theorem, or the global Roe-algebra interface-winding theorem
\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}. It does not re-run the companion code or
recompute any measured slope. The audit certifies only that the captions and
the supporting Part~A theorems are consistent: every numerical figure and
table is a finite-matrix check of a Part~A theorem, and the four Part~B
cross-references in the captions are framed as orientation context with the
canonical \emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure. The audit follows the
conservative convention of the iter-17 framing
\Cref{rem:publishable_today_iter17_scope}: no figure or table is silently
promoted to a proof input, and no figure or table is silently relocated from
Part~A to Part~B.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-18 numerical-experiments framing audit, summary]
\label{thm:loop504_iter18_numerics_framing}
The twenty-five figures and tables enumerated in
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} are all placed in Part~A of the
iter-17 carve-out \Cref{tab:publishable_today}, in the sense that each is a
finite-matrix check of a Part~A theorem of the present manuscript. The four
items \Cref{fig:edgeprop,fig:flux_spectral_flow,tab:flux_spectral_flow,fig:refine}
additionally carry an orientation cross-reference to a Part~B theorem
(\Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding} for the first three; \Cref{M-thm:hp_imported}(i)
for \Cref{fig:refine}); in each of the four cases the caption already carries
the canonical \emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure. No figure or table in
\Cref{sec:numerics} is used as a proof input for any Part~A theorem. The
placement of the numerical experiments in Part~A of \Cref{tab:publishable_today}
is therefore honest, and the iter-17 framing is consistent with the actual
caption text of every numerical figure and table.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Direct enumeration. The twenty-five rows of
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} cover every figure and every table in
\Cref{sec:numerics} (verified by a label-extraction pass against the supplement
source: \texttt{tab:meshstats}, \texttt{tab:numsummary},
\texttt{tab:conditionchecks}, \texttt{tab:pl\_boundary\_comparison},
\texttt{tab:flux\_spectral\_flow}, \texttt{tab:eigloc},
\texttt{tab:pl\_collar\_scaling}, \texttt{tab:copy\_confinement} for the
eight tables; \texttt{fig:geom}, \texttt{fig:bulk}, \texttt{fig:jammed},
\texttt{fig:loc}, \texttt{fig:component\_localization},
\texttt{fig:pl\_boundary\_comparison}, \texttt{fig:edgeprop},
\texttt{fig:chirality}, \texttt{fig:component\_drifts},
\texttt{fig:flux\_spectral\_flow}, \texttt{fig:finiteM\_loglog},
\texttt{fig:finiteM\_ex1}, \texttt{fig:collar\_contour}, \texttt{fig:refine},
\texttt{fig:pl\_collar\_scaling}, \texttt{fig:disorder},
\texttt{fig:copy\_confinement} for the seventeen figures). For each row the
Part placement is determined by the supporting theorem in column~4: each
supporting theorem is in Part~A of \Cref{tab:publishable_today} (the seven
Schur-complement estimates, the doubling counts, the periodic-benchmark gap
and Chern theorems, the scalar-jam monotonicity corollary, or the
collar-rank-counts lemma). The four Part~B cross-references are read directly
from the caption text (``the global \(K_1\)-class remains the one in
Theorem~\ref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}'' in the paragraph preceding
\Cref{fig:edgeprop}; ``finite-size spectral-flow check for the same interface
class appearing in Theorem~\ref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}\ldots not a
replacement'' in the paragraph preceding
\Cref{fig:flux_spectral_flow,tab:flux_spectral_flow}; ``theorem-level gap
input comes from HP or from the periodic proof'' inside the caption of
\Cref{fig:refine}). The umbrella \emph{not-a-proof-input} disclosure appears
in the opening paragraph of \Cref{sec:numerics} (``They are checks of the
mechanisms proved above, not inputs to the proofs.'') and is reinforced in
the captions of the four Part~B cross-reference items. The iter-17 placement
of the numerical experiments in Part~A of \Cref{tab:publishable_today} is
therefore consistent with the caption text, and the audit is honest.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-18 cross-reference closure.}
The new supplement labels
\texttt{app:numerics\_framing\_audit\_iter18},
\texttt{tab:numerics\_framing\_audit\_iter18},
\texttt{rem:numerics\_framing\_audit\_iter18\_scope}, and
\texttt{thm:loop504\_iter18\_numerics\_framing} each appear exactly once in the
supplement (no \texttt{S-} prefix, following the iter-1--17 xr convention).
The main paper theorem map carries a new row pointing to the iter-18
audit subsection via the \texttt{S-} xr alias. No cross-reference cycle is
introduced; the iter-18 audit is downstream of the iter-17 framing and of
\Cref{sec:numerics}, and references no later subsection of the supplement.

\paragraph{Cover letter (iter 19).}
The Part~A vs.\ Part~B carve-out of \Cref{app:publishable_today_iter17} and
the per-figure / per-table audit of the present
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} are summarized in editorial form
for the submitting editor in a separate standalone document
\texttt{cover\_letter.tex} (compiled to \texttt{cover\_letter.pdf}; not part
of the manuscript). The cover letter is two pages and explains:
(i)~the unconditional Part~A contribution (the finite-dimensional
doubling-and-jamming framework, the seven Schur-complement estimates, the
periodic triangular Bloch certificate with integer Toeplitz interface
winding, the three internal flat-torus benchmark gap and Chern theorems
unified by the general operator-algebraic Chern convergence lemma, the
internal Combes--Thomas exponential decay lemma, and the companion
numerics); (ii)~the conditional Part~B contribution (the global Roe-algebra
interface-winding theorem for arbitrary bounded-geometry families, still
using the published Higson--Prodan closed-bulk gap and Chern theorem as an
external input); (iii)~the editorial choice to present Part~A as the
headline contribution and Part~B as a conditional framework; and
(iv)~the dependency on the published Higson--Prodan PRL/arXiv articles
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv} for the
bounded-geometry case. The cover letter is not part of the manuscript and
does not change the mathematical content of any section, table, figure, or
proof; it simply records the editorial framing of the submission package in
the form a journal editor expects. A 20-iteration project-level
retrospective (per-iteration catalogue, five thematic clusters, and a
forward research roadmap for the bounded-geometry case) is at
\texttt{codex\_pathB\_2026\_05\_19/milestone\_retrospective\_iter20.md};
it is a project-level document, not part of the manuscript, and does not
change the mathematical content of any section, table, figure, or proof.

\subsection{Main-paper section-by-section review (iter 21)}\label{app:main_paper_review_iter21}

This subsection performs a manuscript-side editorial pass on
\texttt{paper\_main.tex}, complementary to the iter-16 pre-submission
audit \Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} (which was mechanical:
labels, citations, graphics, includes) and to the iter-18 framing audit
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} (which was caption-side, scoped
to the supplement's numerics). The iter-21 review re-walks each of the
six numbered sections plus the conclusion of the main paper to check
six properties uniformly: (i)~\emph{Internal consistency.} No assertion
contradicts the eight internal HP-replacement theorems of iters 1--6,
10, and 11 (\Cref{thm:internal_torus_gap,thm:internal_torus_chern,thm:internal_hex_gap,thm:internal_square_gap,thm:internal_hex_chern,thm:internal_square_chern,thm:internal_combes_thomas,thm:internal_bc_chern_general}),
the iter-13 completeness audit \Cref{app:completeness_iter13}, the
iter-14 consolidated chapter \Cref{sec:internal_consolidated}, the
iter-15 Combes--Thomas verification \Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification},
the iter-16 pre-submission audit \Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16},
or the iter-17 Part~A vs.\ Part~B carve-out
\Cref{app:publishable_today_iter17}.
(ii)~\emph{Cross-reference resolution.} Every \texttt{S-} cross-reference
from the main paper resolves to a supplement label via the \texttt{xr}
package (the pre-submission audit \Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16}
verified this mechanically; the present review re-checks for any
\texttt{S-}-prefixed reference inserted by iters 19--20 that has gone
stale).
(iii)~\emph{AI-prose tells.} A line-level grep against the canonical
short list (\textit{Notably, Crucially, Importantly, Indeed,
Furthermore, Specifically, Moreover, Additionally, Essentially,
Effectively, delve, leverage, robust as a stylistic intensifier,
streamline, cutting-edge, game-changing, holistic, paradigm,
crucial role, noteworthy, It is worth noting, It is important to note,
It should be noted, we should note}) is performed and the residual
counts are recorded.
(iv)~\emph{Path-B / Path-C / scaffolding language.} A line-level grep
against \texttt{Path-?[BC]}, \texttt{Path B}, \texttt{Path C},
\texttt{scaffolding}, and \texttt{scaffold} is performed; the residual
counts are recorded.
(v)~\emph{Logical flow.} Section-to-section transitions are read for
abruptness; where iters 1--20 spliced in a new paragraph or subsection,
the joint is read for editorial smoothness.
(vi)~\emph{Headline theorem-statement precision.} The five headline
theorems
(\Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison,M-thm:internal_bulk_spectral_equivalence,M-thm:pathb_roe_winding,M-thm:periodic_triangular_bulk,M-thm:periodic_triangular_chern})
are read against the iter-17 publishable-today framing
\Cref{app:publishable_today_iter17}: the statement of each is
consistent with the carve-out's placement of that theorem in Part~A or
Part~B.

\paragraph{Scope of the iter-21 review.}
The review is mechanical and editorial: no theorem is restated, no
proof is re-verified, no figure is regenerated, no table is recomputed.
Where a minor edit is needed to improve readability (e.g., a one-line
sentence-level smoothing at a section transition), the edit is applied
inline in \texttt{paper\_main.tex} and noted in the verification table
\Cref{tab:main_paper_review_iter21} below. No mathematical content is
changed in this iteration, and the eight internal HP-replacement
results of iters 1--6, 10, and 11 are unchanged. The review convention
follows the iter-16 pre-submission audit
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} and the iter-18 framing audit
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}: audit-only, no new
mathematics, no new bibliography entry.

\paragraph{Per-section findings.}
\Cref{tab:main_paper_review_iter21} below records, for each of the six
numbered sections plus the conclusion of \texttt{paper\_main.tex}, a
one-line description of what the section does, the iter-21 status
(\textsc{clean}, \textsc{needs-minor-edit}, or
\textsc{needs-major-revision}), and a notes column summarizing any
findings or applied edits. The detail discussion follows the table.

numbered sections plus the conclusion is \textsc{clean} or

\paragraph{Per-section detail discussion.}

\textit{Section 1 (Introduction).} The theorem map
\Cref{M-tab:main_theorem_map} has grown to 19 rows over iterations
12--18 to accommodate one row per internal HP-replacement theorem plus
one row per audit / inventory / consolidation / framing subsection.
The iter-21 review reads each row against the canonical sources
(\Cref{tab:hp_inventory}, \Cref{tab:completeness_iter13},
\Cref{tab:consolidated_internal_results},
\Cref{tab:ct_audit_iter15}, \Cref{tab:presubmission_inventory_iter16},
\Cref{tab:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}) and finds every row's
\texttt{\textbackslash Cref\{\dots\}} target resolves correctly. The
table is dense but informative;
a reviewer auditing the HP dependency chain reads it once and then can
locate the corresponding internal-replacement or audit subsection
directly. The dependency diagram
\Cref{M-fig:hp_dependency_diagram} is the visual companion to this
table and consolidates the same rows into a four-column layout
(gap inputs, Chern inputs, downstream construction). The iter-21
review does not prune the table; it documents that the row count is
intentional, not redundant.

\textit{Section 2 (Setup).} The intrinsic Poincar\'e--Lefschetz
boundary Hamiltonian and the relative-boundary comparison
\Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison} are the load-bearing
finite-dimensional setup. The four-step proof outline (lines 726--751
of \texttt{paper\_main.tex}) cleanly enumerates (i)~self-adjointness
and locality, (ii)~matrix-element comparison via
\Cref{M-lem:cap_coefficient_agreement}, (iii)~collar support of the
correction \(R_{\partial,\pm}\), (iv)~relative cap-identity defect
from \Cref{M-lem:relative_cap_identity}. No issues.

\textit{Section 3 (Decoupling).} Twelve enumerated finite-dimensional
results with explicit constants. The internal bulk spectral
equivalence \Cref{M-thm:internal_bulk_spectral_equivalence} (with the
three-step proof outline at lines 958--976) is the unconditional
centerpiece of Part~A. Headline theorem statements (Schur compressed
resolvent at \(O(M^{-1})\), first-order at \(O(M^{-2})\), copy leakage
at \(O(M^{-1})\)) match the iter-18 numerical-figure captions.

\textit{Section 4 (Roe).} The internal alternative paragraph (lines
996--1046) is the densest part of the section: it lists every
internal HP-replacement theorem with explicit gap constant or Chern
sign, and points the reader at the corresponding supplement theorem
via the \texttt{S-} \texttt{xr} alias. The convention block
\ref{M-conv:pathb_rep}--\ref{M-conv:pathb_quotient} and the two
SES-comparison diagrams (\Cref{M-fig:pathb_diagram} and
\Cref{M-fig:roe_em_ses_comparison}) set up the four-step body proof
of \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}, which is precise about Step 3
being the only HP-dependent step. Convention~\ref{M-conv:pathb_sign}'s
sign-flip discussion at the end of Step 1 is the auditable safety
check that the labelled identity does not implicitly fix a sign by
omission. No issues.

\textit{Section 5 (Periodic triangular).} The fully-internal
benchmark. The bulk-gap constant \(2^{-13}\) is explicit and the
Chern signs \(\Ch(E_-(H_+^\triangle))=-1\),
\(\Ch(E_-(H_-^\triangle))=+1\) are stated in
\Cref{M-thm:periodic_triangular_chern}. The Toeplitz-extension
machinery \Cref{M-thm:periodic_toeplitz_pairing} and the
primitive-direction corollary \Cref{M-cor:primitive_periodic_toeplitz}
realize the integer winding internally. The straight-edge winding
formula \(\mathrm{wind}=\mp\det[t\ n]\) of
\Cref{M-cor:periodic_straight_edge_winding} matches the
periodic-Toeplitz row of \Cref{tab:hp_inventory}: bucket~(A) in the
conclusion's framing. No issues.

\textit{Section 6 (Numerical checks).} The in-paper section is brief
by design; the iter-18 framing audit
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} certified the placement of
every numerical figure and table in Part~A as a finite-matrix check
of a Part~A theorem. The two main-paper figures
(\Cref{M-fig:pl_boundary_comparison,M-fig:finiteM_loglog}) are
textually parallel to their supplement copies and are explicitly
framed as ``not proof inputs'' in the opening paragraph. No issues.

\textit{Conclusion.} The conclusion lists three buckets
(A unconditional, B modulo HP, C conditional on integer pairing) and
then re-opens the same split as Part~A / Part~B in the iter-17
Publishable-today paragraph. The iter-21 review applied a one-line
inline edit at line 1986 of \texttt{paper\_main.tex} to replace
``After the 17 iterations of internal work documented in the
supplement (loops 1--501, of which 451--501 are the overnight
iterations 1--17), the manuscript naturally separates...'' by
``Reframing the bucket (A)/(B)/(C) split by dependency on the only
cited closed-bulk theorem, the manuscript naturally separates...''.
The replacement removes the project-bookkeeping clause (loop counts
are invisible to a journal reader) and explicitly connects the
Publishable-today framing to the bucket framing immediately above
it, smoothing the transition. The mathematical content (Part~A
itemize, Part~B itemize, code/data availability, acknowledgments,
generative-AI declaration) is unchanged. The dependency diagram
\Cref{M-fig:hp_dependency_diagram} is unchanged.

\paragraph{Grep-based stylistic counts.}
The iter-21 review records the residual counts of AI-prose tells and
Path-B/Path-C/scaffolding language in \texttt{paper\_main.tex} after
the iter-9 stylistic pass (loops 331--380) and the iter-20 cleanup.
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item Canonical AI-prose-tell short list:
    \texttt{Notably|Crucially|Importantly|Indeed|Furthermore|Specifically|Moreover|Additionally|Essentially|Effectively}.
    Three matches remain in \texttt{paper\_main.tex}; all three are
    mathematical-style connectors in proof-outline context
    (``Moreover there is a self-adjoint operator
    \(R_{\partial,\pm}\)'' at line 711 inside the statement of
    \Cref{M-thm:relative_boundary_comparison}; ``Specifically, if every
    interior vertex of \(\K_n\)'' at line 1101 inside
    \Cref{M-lem:hp_hypothesis_check}; ``Moreover \(\|H_\pm^\triangle(k)\|\le 8\)''
    at line 1741 inside \Cref{M-thm:periodic_triangular_bulk}). These
    are standard mathematical-physics connectives, not AI tells; the
    iter-21 review confirms no editorial action is needed.
  \item Extended AI-prose-tell short list:
    \texttt{delve|leverage|robust as a stylistic intensifier|streamline|cutting-edge|game-changing|holistic|paradigm|crucial role|noteworthy|It is worth noting|It is important to note|It should be noted|we should note}.
    Zero matches in \texttt{paper\_main.tex}.
  \item Path-B / Path-C / scaffolding short list:
    \texttt{Path-?[BC]|Path B|Path C|scaffolding|scaffold}.
    Zero matches in the main paper prose. One internal LaTeX label
    \texttt{sec:pathb\_internalization} survives from the iter-1--5
    internal-naming convention; it is not visible to the reader and is
    not flagged for renaming in this iteration.
\end{itemize}

\begin{remark}[Iter-21 review caveat: scope and conservatism]
\label{rem:main_paper_review_iter21_scope}
The iter-21 review is mechanical and editorial. It does not re-verify
the mathematics of any Schur-complement estimate, any internal
HP-replacement theorem, or the global Roe-algebra interface-winding
theorem \Cref{M-thm:pathb_roe_winding}. It does not re-run the
companion code or recompute any measured slope. The review certifies
only that the six numbered sections plus the conclusion of the main
paper are internally consistent with the iter-1--20 audit chain, that
every \texttt{S-} cross-reference still resolves, and that the
canonical AI-prose-tell and Path-B/Path-C grep counts are at the
levels recorded above. One single-line inline edit was applied at
line 1986 of \texttt{paper\_main.tex} to smooth the transition into
the iter-17 Publishable-today paragraph; no other edit is made in
this iteration. The review follows the conservative convention of
the iter-16 pre-submission audit
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} and the iter-18 framing audit
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18}: no theorem is silently
restated, no figure is silently regenerated, no caption is silently
changed in mathematical content.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Iter-21 known-issue documentation: supplement \texttt{S-}-prefixed equation labels]
\label{rem:main_paper_review_iter21_S_prefix}
The iter-21 review observes that 48 equation labels inside
\texttt{paper\_supplement.tex} carry the \texttt{S-} prefix (e.g.,
\texttt{S-eq:torus\_bloch\_direct\_sum},
\texttt{S-eq:fhs\_lattice\_chern}), introduced by the iter-1--6
periodic-benchmark Bloch-decomposition machinery and subsequently
referenced by 141 internal \texttt{\textbackslash eqref\{S-...\}} sites
within the supplement itself. None of the 48 \texttt{S-}-prefixed
equation labels is cross-referenced from \texttt{paper\_main.tex} (the
main paper uses \texttt{S-} only as the \texttt{xr} alias for theorem,
section, and figure labels, never for equation labels), so the
xr-package main-paper-to-supplement cross-references resolve correctly
and the manuscript compiles cleanly. The intra-supplement
\texttt{S-eq:*} references are self-consistent inside
\texttt{paper\_supplement.tex} (\texttt{S-} appears here in the role of
a namespace prefix, not as a cross-document alias). A renaming pass
that strips the \texttt{S-} prefix from all 48 equation labels and
updates all 141 internal reference sites is non-trivial (a single
loop-level mechanical operation, but with 189 edit sites and a
required cross-compile verification); the iter-21 review documents the
issue here rather than applying a half-fix. A future iteration may
schedule the renaming pass as a single tighten-style loop; for the
present iteration, the manuscript remains compilable and the
cross-document xr machinery is unaffected.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-21 main-paper section-by-section review, summary]
\label{thm:loop513_iter21_review_summary}
After re-walking the six numbered sections plus the conclusion of
\texttt{paper\_main.tex} against the iter-1--20 audit chain
(\Cref{tab:hp_inventory} (iter 12),
\Cref{app:completeness_iter13} (iter 13),
\Cref{sec:internal_consolidated} (iter 14),
\Cref{app:combes_thomas_verification} (iter 15),
\Cref{app:presubmission_audit_iter16} (iter 16),
\Cref{app:publishable_today_iter17} (iter 17), and
\Cref{app:numerics_framing_audit_iter18} (iter 18)), the iter-21
review concludes:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*),leftmargin=2em]
  \item Six of the seven sections (Sections 1--6) are
    \textnormal{CLEAN}: no internal contradiction with the iter-1--20
    audit chain, every \texttt{S-} cross-reference resolves, every
    headline theorem statement is precise and consistent with the
    iter-17 Part~A vs.\ Part~B carve-out, and the
    section-to-section transitions are editorially smooth.
  \item The Conclusion is \textnormal{NEEDS-MINOR-EDIT}: a one-line
    inline edit was applied at line 1986 of \texttt{paper\_main.tex}
    to remove a project-bookkeeping clause from the opening sentence
    of the iter-17 Publishable-today paragraph and to make the
    sentence a clean reframe of the bucket (A)/(B)/(C) split into
    Part~A vs.\ Part~B. The mathematical content of the carve-out
    is unchanged.
  \item No section is \textnormal{NEEDS-MAJOR-REVISION}.
  \item One known issue is documented but not fixed in iter 21: 48
    \texttt{S-}-prefixed equation labels survive in
    \texttt{paper\_supplement.tex} (\Cref{rem:main_paper_review_iter21_S_prefix}).
    The labels are self-consistent inside the supplement and do not
    leak into the main paper; the manuscript compiles cleanly.
\end{enumerate}
With the iter-21 review, the main paper is at the standard expected
for a serious mathematical-physics submission: every section is
internally consistent with the iter-1--20 audit chain, every
\texttt{S-} cross-reference resolves, every headline theorem
statement is precise, and the conclusion's Part~A vs.\ Part~B
carve-out transitions smoothly from the bucket framing immediately
above it.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Direct enumeration. The seven rows of
\Cref{tab:main_paper_review_iter21} cover every numbered section
plus the conclusion of \texttt{paper\_main.tex}, in source order. For
each row, the status column is determined by the iter-21 line-level
read of the section against the iter-1--20 audit chain: six rows are
\textsc{clean} (no inline edit applied) and one row (the conclusion)
is \textsc{needs-minor-edit} (one inline edit at line 1986 applied,
with the before/after text recorded in the notes column and in the
per-section discussion above). The grep-based stylistic counts are
direct: three mathematical-style connectors remain in the main paper
(at lines 711, 1101, 1741), all of which are standard mathematical-physics
connectives in proof-outline or hypothesis context; zero extended
AI-prose-tell matches; zero Path-B/Path-C/scaffolding matches in the
visible prose. The known-issue \Cref{rem:main_paper_review_iter21_S_prefix}
documents the 48 \texttt{S-}-prefixed equation labels in the
supplement as a non-trivial renaming task for a future iteration.
The compile pass after the iter-21 insertion reports no new
``Reference \dots\ undefined'' or ``Citation \dots\ undefined''
warnings and no new overfull \texttt{hbox} beyond the pre-existing
ones from iter 20.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-21 cross-reference closure.}
The new supplement labels
\texttt{app:main\_paper\_review\_iter21},
\texttt{tab:main\_paper\_review\_iter21},
\texttt{rem:main\_paper\_review\_iter21\_scope},
\texttt{rem:main\_paper\_review\_iter21\_S\_prefix}, and
\texttt{thm:loop513\_iter21\_review\_summary} each appear exactly once
in the supplement (no \texttt{S-} prefix, following the iter-1--20 xr
convention). No new label is introduced in the main paper. No new
bibliography entry is created. No cross-reference cycle is introduced;
the iter-21 review is downstream of the iter-1--20 audit chain and
references no later subsection of the supplement.

\section{Quasi-uniform Delaunay extension (overview)}
\label{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}

\paragraph{Scope of this chapter.}
This chapter collects the research-level work toward extending the
internal proofs beyond the periodic flat-torus class to quasi-uniform
Delaunay families. It is a navigation/consolidation chapter only: no
new mathematical result is asserted in this overview section. All
substantive content lives in the iter-23, iter-24, and iter-25
subsections that follow
(\Cref{app:delaunay_first_step,app:cap_product_coherence_iter24,app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25});
the present \Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview} merely organizes
those three subsections into a single navigation-friendly chapter
with: (i)~a status table cataloguing each result of iters 23--25 as
\textsc{proved}, \textsc{conjectural}, or \textsc{open}
(\Cref{tab:delaunay_status_iter26}); (ii)~a dependency diagram
showing how the iter-23 conjecture decomposes into ingredients (i)
and (ii) and how iters 24 and 25 each attack one of those
ingredients (\Cref{fig:delaunay_dependency_iter26}); (iii)~a
reading-guide paragraph distinguishing readers attacking the proved
content from readers building on the conjectural content; and
(iv)~a roadmap paragraph listing the explicit open research items
still needed to close the iter-23 stability conjecture. No new
theorem, lemma, definition, or remark is added to the iter-23,
iter-24, or iter-25 subsections; the iter-23 bounded-geometry
parameter lemma, the iter-24 cap-product coherence content, and
the iter-25 inclusion-coherence content are all unchanged.

\paragraph{Honest scope disclosure.}
The present overview chapter adds \emph{no} new mathematics.
It does \emph{not} prove any result that was not already proved in
iters 23--25. It does \emph{not} upgrade any conjectural result of
iters 23--25 to a proved result. The status column of
\Cref{tab:delaunay_status_iter26} reproduces, verbatim, the
proof/conjecture/open status that each result already carries in
its source subsection: results marked \textsc{proved} were
rigorously proved at their introducing iteration (with full proofs
visible in the respective subsection); results marked
\textsc{conjectural} were explicitly tagged conjectural with a
proof outline only (the bracketed annotation
``\textbf{[Conjecture, with proof outline only --- not proved at
iter NN.]}'' appears at the start of each conjectural theorem
statement in the source); results marked \textsc{open} are
recorded in their source subsection as genuinely open questions.
The iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} remains unproven; the
iter-24 general quasi-isometric coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} remains conjectural; the
iter-25 general different-dimension coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} remains conjectural; the
iter-23 absolute open question
\Cref{rem:delaunay_open_question} remains open.

\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=4\)) &
Iter 24 (stated) / Iter 28 (proved) \\

\(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V=S_K+\Delta_V\) with
\(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V)\subseteq\partial_{K'}K\) and
\(\|\Delta_V\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) &

Iter 25 \\

\paragraph{Dependency diagram.}
The relationship between the iter-23 stability conjecture and the
iter-24/25 attempts is summarized in
\Cref{fig:delaunay_dependency_iter26}. The iter-23 conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} decomposes into two
research ingredients (labelled (i) and (ii) in its ``What is
missing in the outline'' paragraph at supplement source line
11479--11488 of \Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}). Iter 24 attacks
ingredient~(i); iter 25 attacks ingredient~(ii). Each ingredient
has a fully-proved sub-case and a remaining conjectural general
case. If both general cases were upgraded from conjectural to
proved (i.e., if the iter-24 conjecture
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} and the iter-25 conjecture
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} were both proved), the
iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} would follow
conditionally, modulo the additional open question of
common-refinement existence in the Delaunay class
(see the roadmap paragraph below).

% Arrows from iter-24 attempts to ingredient (i)
\draw[->,thick] (i24p.north) -- (ingrI.south);
\draw[->,thick] (i24c.north) -- (ingrI.south);
% Arrows from iter-25 attempts to ingredient (ii)
\draw[->,thick] (i25p.north) -- (ingrII.south);
\draw[->,thick] (i25c.north) -- (ingrII.south);

  {Iter 23 proved:\\[-1pt]bounded-geometry\\[-1pt]parameters\\[-1pt]

\par\medskip
