vertices \(\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\}\) are \emph{common to \(K_0\) and
\(K'\)} (the edit changes only the interior diagonal and the two
incident triangles). The local Hilbert space of the quadrilateral
edit is
\(\Hil_Q(K_0)=\ell^2(Q_0\sqcup Q_1(K_0)\sqcup Q_2(K_0))\) where
\(Q_0=\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\}\),
\(Q_1(K_0)=\{[v_1,v_2],[v_2,v_3],[v_3,v_4],[v_4,v_1],[v_1,v_3]\}\),
\(Q_2(K_0)=\{\tau_1,\tau_2\}\), so
\(\dim\Hil_Q(K_0)=4+5+2=11\), and similarly
\(\dim\Hil_Q(K')=11\) with \(Q_1(K')\) and \(Q_2(K')\) using the
post-flip diagonal and triangles. The \emph{natural local bijection}
\(\phi:Q_0\sqcup Q_1(K_0)\sqcup Q_2(K_0)\to
Q_0\sqcup Q_1(K')\sqcup Q_2(K')\) is the identity on the four
vertices and the four common boundary edges, and on the differing
simplices is given by
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:single_flip_bijection_iter27}
\phi([v_1,v_3])=[v_2,v_4],\quad
\phi(\tau_1)=\tau_1',\quad
\phi(\tau_2)=\tau_2'.
\end{equation}
The bijection \(\phi\) induces a local unitary
\(V_\phi:\Hil_Q(K_0)\to\Hil_Q(K')\) by
\(V_\phi\,\delta_\sigma=\delta_{\phi(\sigma)}\).
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Why the natural bijection breaks simpliciality]
\label{rem:single_flip_breaks_simpliciality_iter27}
The natural bijection \(\phi\) of
\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27} is \emph{not} a simplicial
vertex-bijection in the sense of
\Cref{def:cap_product_coherence_iter24}: although the vertex map
\(\phi_0=\mathrm{id}\) is a bijection of \(Q_0\), the map
\(\phi_1\) sends the diagonal \([v_1,v_3]\) to the differently
oriented \([v_2,v_4]\), and the standard simplicial incidence
\([v_1,v_3]\subset[v_1,v_2,v_3]\) is replaced under \(\phi\) by the
incorrect incidence
``\([v_2,v_4]\subset[v_1,v_2,v_4]\)'' (which is true as an
incidence, but with no compatibility of vertex orderings under
\(\phi_0=\mathrm{id}\)). This is exactly why
\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24} does not apply directly to
the flip: the iter-27 contribution is to identify the closed-form
correction \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) that
\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24} would produce as a remainder.
\end{remark}

\begin{lemma}[Closed-form cap-product on the flip quadrilateral]
\label{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}
Let \(K_0,K'\) be related by a single-flip Delaunay edit
(\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) and let
\(V_\phi:\Hil_Q(K_0)\to\Hil_Q(K')\) be the natural local unitary
\eqref{S-eq:single_flip_bijection_iter27}. Let
\(S_{K_0,Q}\) and \(S_{K',Q}\) denote the cap-product operators
restricted to \(\Hil_Q(K_0)\) and \(\Hil_Q(K')\) respectively
(supplement lines 482--487 convention,
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}). The
matrix elements
\(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\) and
\(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_{K',Q}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\) for
\(\sigma,\sigma'\in Q_0\sqcup Q_1\sqcup Q_2\) satisfy the
following closed-form identities.
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\arabic*),leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item \textbf{Vertex--edge entries.} For each vertex
    \(v\in Q_0\) and each edge \(e''\) of the quadrilateral,
    \(\langle\delta_v,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{e''}\rangle\) is
    \(\pm\tfrac12\) if \(v\) is an endpoint of \(e''\) and 0
    otherwise; the sign is the simplicial incidence
    \([e'':v]\in\{+1,-1\}\) from the orientation of
    \Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}. Identical entries hold for
    \(S_{K',Q}\) on the post-flip basis. The matrix block
    \(\langle\delta_v,(V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0,Q})
    \delta_{e''}\rangle\) is zero for every common boundary edge
    \(e''\in\{[v_1,v_2],[v_2,v_3],[v_3,v_4],[v_4,v_1]\}\); the
    only nonzero entries are on the differing diagonal pair
    \((v,e)\) vs.\ \((v,\phi(e))\) with \(v\in Q_0\).
  \item \textbf{Edge--triangle entries.} For each edge
    \(e''\) and each triangle \(\tau''\) of \(K_0\) or \(K'\),
    \(\langle\delta_{e''},S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\tau''}\rangle
    =\tfrac12[\tau'':e'']\) if \(e''\) is a face of \(\tau''\), and 0
    otherwise. The factor of \(\tfrac12\) is the Hermitization
    coefficient (closed-case cap-product convention, supplement
    lines 482--487; the raw cap product produces \(\pm1\), and
    self-adjointness divides by 2). Identical entries hold for
    \(S_{K',Q}\) on the post-flip basis.
  \item \textbf{Vertex--triangle entries.} For each vertex \(v\) and
    each triangle \(\tau''\),
    \(\langle\delta_v,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\tau''}\rangle
    =\tfrac12[\tau'':v_*\dots v_*]\), where the sign is the
    incidence sign of \(v\) in \(\partial\tau''\) summed over
    composite cap-product paths (zero unless \(v\) is a vertex of
    \(\tau''\), in which case the entry is \(\pm\tfrac14\) by the
    closed-case cap-product convention). Identical entries hold for
    \(S_{K',Q}\) on the post-flip basis.
  \item \textbf{Same-dimensional entries (vertex--vertex,
    edge--edge, triangle--triangle).} For \(\sigma,\sigma'\) both of
    the same dimension \(p\), the cap-product matrix element
    \(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_K\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\) vanishes by
    the dimension-shifting nature of the cap product:
    \(S_K\) lowers dimension by 0 only via Hermitization through
    composite \(\delta_\sigma\to|X\rangle\frown\delta_\sigma^*\to
    [v_0,\dots,v_{2-p}]\to\delta\), and the same-dimensional matrix
    elements vanish unless \(\sigma=\sigma'\) and the
    self-loop coefficient is zero from the Hermitization. In
    particular, the four common boundary edges contribute zero
    same-dimensional same-edge entries, and the two pre/post-flip
    diagonals each contribute zero (the self-loop term is fully
    cancelled by Hermitization).
  \item \textbf{Off-quadrilateral entries.} For \(\sigma\) or
    \(\sigma'\) outside the closed quadrilateral
    \(\overline Q\subseteq\Sigma\), the matrix element
    \(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\) is
    identical to
    \(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_{K',Q}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\),
    because the cap-product is local with locality radius 1 on the
    simplex graph
    \cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv} and
    both triangulations agree on
    \(\Sigma\setminus\mathrm{int}(Q)\).
\end{enumerate}
Combining (1)--(5), the difference
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}=V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0,Q}\) is
supported on the set
\(\Omega_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}=
\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\}\cup\{[v_1,v_3]\}\cup\{\tau_1,\tau_2\}\),
and the only nonzero entries of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) on the basis
\(\{\delta_\sigma\}_{\sigma\in Q_0\sqcup Q_1(K_0)\sqcup Q_2(K_0)}\)
are the \(\le 4+4+4\le 12\) entries of the form
\((\text{vertex},\text{diagonal})\),
\((\text{diagonal},\text{triangle})\), and
\((\text{vertex},\text{triangle})\) corresponding to the differing
diagonal and the two differing triangles. Each such entry has
absolute value at most \(\tfrac12\) (the maximum value of a
single cap-product matrix element on a simplex of dimension
\(\le 2\) in the Hermitized closed-case convention).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}]
The proof is a direct computation in five steps, one per
enumerated entry type.

\emph{Step (1) [vertex--edge entries].}
The closed-case cap-product convention (supplement lines 482--487)
defines, for an oriented edge \(e''=[w_0,w_1]\) and a vertex
\(v=w_i\) of \(e''\),
\(\delta_v^*\frown[w_0,w_1]=
\delta_v^*([w_1])\,[w_0]\) if \(v=w_1\), and zero otherwise; this
gives the raw cap-product matrix element
\(\langle\delta_{w_0},(|X\rangle\frown\delta_{w_1}^*)
\delta_{e''}\rangle=1\) up to incidence signs. After Hermitization
\(S_K=\tfrac12(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}}+(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}})^\dagger)\)
the matrix element
\(\langle\delta_v,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{e''}\rangle\) becomes
\(\tfrac12\,[e'':v]\) when \(v\) is an endpoint of \(e''\)
(the \(\pm1\) sign is the simplicial incidence
\([e'':v]\in\{+1,-1\}\)), and zero otherwise. The same computation
applies to \(S_{K',Q}\) on the post-flip basis: the four common
boundary edges give identical matrix elements in \(K_0\) and \(K'\)
because they are common simplices with identical orientations. The
only difference is between the pre-flip diagonal \([v_1,v_3]\)
(with endpoints \(v_1,v_3\)) and the post-flip diagonal
\([v_2,v_4]\) (with endpoints \(v_2,v_4\)). Under the natural local
bijection \(\phi\) (\(\phi_0=\mathrm{id}\), \(\phi([v_1,v_3])=[v_2,v_4]\)),
the conjugated entry is
\(\langle\delta_v,V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi\,
\delta_{[v_1,v_3]}\rangle=
\langle\delta_v,S_{K',Q}\,\delta_{[v_2,v_4]}\rangle\), which is
\(\tfrac12[\,[v_2,v_4]\!:\!v]\) if \(v\in\{v_2,v_4\}\) and zero if
\(v\in\{v_1,v_3\}\). The pre-flip entry is
\(\tfrac12[\,[v_1,v_3]\!:\!v]\) if \(v\in\{v_1,v_3\}\) and zero
if \(v\in\{v_2,v_4\}\). Hence the difference
\(\langle\delta_v,R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\delta_{[v_1,v_3]}\rangle\)
takes the value \(+\tfrac12\) at \(v=v_2\), \(-\tfrac12\) at
\(v=v_4\), \(-\tfrac12\) at \(v=v_1\), and \(-\tfrac12\) at
\(v=v_3\) (the signs follow from the orientation convention
\([\tau_1:e]=+1\), \([\tau_2:e]=-1\) of
\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}, and the requirement that the
endpoints \(v_1,v_3\) of the pre-flip diagonal carry incidence
signs opposite to the endpoints \(v_2,v_4\) of the post-flip
diagonal under the natural orientation of the quadrilateral
boundary). In total, the vertex--edge block of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) restricted to the diagonal column has
at most four nonzero entries, each of absolute value
\(\le\tfrac12\). The vertex--edge block on the four common
boundary edges is identically zero.

\emph{Step (2) [edge--triangle entries].}
The cap-product matrix element
\(\langle\delta_{e''},S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\tau''}\rangle\) is
computed by the convention
\(\delta_{e''}^*\frown[w_0,w_1,w_2]=
\delta_{e''}^*([w_1,w_2])\,[w_0]\) followed by Hermitization. When
\(e''\) is a face of \(\tau''\) with \(\tau''=[w_0,w_1,w_2]\) and
\(e''=[w_a,w_b]\) for \(a<b\), the raw cap-product gives
\(\pm 1\), and Hermitization gives \(\tfrac12[\tau'':e'']\). For
\(K_0\) the four boundary-edge--triangle pairs \((e'',\tau_i)\) with
\(i\in\{1,2\}\) and \(e''\) a boundary edge of \(\tau_i\)
contribute \(\pm\tfrac12\); the diagonal--triangle pair
\(([v_1,v_3],\tau_i)\) contributes
\([\tau_i:[v_1,v_3]]\cdot\tfrac12\). For \(K'\) the analogous
diagonal--triangle pair is
\(([v_2,v_4],\tau'_i)\) for \(i\in\{1,2\}\). Under the bijection
\(\phi\), \(\phi([v_1,v_3])=[v_2,v_4]\) and
\(\phi(\tau_i)=\tau'_i\), so the conjugated pre-flip entry is
\(\langle\delta_{[v_1,v_3]},V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi\,
\delta_{\tau_i}\rangle=
\langle\delta_{[v_2,v_4]},S_{K',Q}\,\delta_{\tau'_i}\rangle=
\tfrac12[\tau'_i:[v_2,v_4]]\). For boundary-edge--triangle
pairs \((e'',\tau_i)\) with \(e''\) a common boundary edge of
\(\tau_i\) and \(\tau_i'\) the corresponding post-flip triangle
containing \(e''\), the difference
\(\langle\delta_{e''},R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\delta_{\tau_i}\rangle=
\tfrac12([\tau'_i:e'']-[\tau_i:e''])\), which is zero when
the simplex \(e''\) appears as a face of \(\tau_i\) and \(\tau_i'\)
with the same incidence sign and nonzero (of magnitude \(\le 1\))
when the signs differ. Each common boundary edge appears as a face
of exactly one pre-flip triangle and exactly one post-flip
triangle, and the natural orientation choice
(\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) makes these incidence signs
agree. Hence the only nonzero edge--triangle entries of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) are the four entries between the
pre-flip diagonal \([v_1,v_3]\) and the two pre-flip triangles
\(\tau_1,\tau_2\), each of magnitude
\(\tfrac12\), and the four ``apparent'' entries between the four
common boundary edges and the two pre-flip triangles, which all
vanish by the matched orientation choice.

\emph{Step (3) [vertex--triangle entries].}
The closed-case cap-product convention applied iteratively gives
the vertex--triangle matrix element via two successive cap-products
of dimension 1, divided by the Hermitization factor 2 for each
step:
\(\langle\delta_v,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\tau''}\rangle=
\tfrac14[\tau'':v]\) when \(v\) is a vertex of \(\tau''\), with the
sign \([\tau'':v]\) from the composite cap-product convention. The
factor of \(\tfrac14\) is \((\tfrac12)^2\) from two Hermitization
steps (vertex--edge then edge--triangle, composed). The pre-flip
matrix has eight such vertex--triangle pairs: \(\tau_1\) contains
\(v_1,v_2,v_3\) and \(\tau_2\) contains \(v_1,v_3,v_4\). The
post-flip matrix has eight pairs: \(\tau'_1\) contains
\(v_1,v_2,v_4\) and \(\tau'_2\) contains \(v_2,v_3,v_4\). Under
the bijection \(\phi\) (\(\phi_0=\mathrm{id}\),
\(\phi(\tau_i)=\tau'_i\)), the conjugated post-flip entry at
\((v,\tau_i)\) becomes
\(\tfrac14[\tau'_i:v]\) for \(v\in\{v_1,v_2,v_4\}\) when
\(i=1\) and \(v\in\{v_2,v_3,v_4\}\) when \(i=2\); it is zero
otherwise. The pre-flip entry at \((v,\tau_i)\) is
\(\tfrac14[\tau_i:v]\) for \(v\in\{v_1,v_2,v_3\}\) when
\(i=1\) and \(v\in\{v_1,v_3,v_4\}\) when \(i=2\); it is zero
otherwise. The difference \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) at
\((v,\tau_i)\) takes value at most \(\tfrac14\) in absolute
magnitude (it can take the values \(0,\pm\tfrac14,\pm\tfrac12\)
when both pre- and post-flip entries are nonzero and the signs add
constructively or destructively; the worst case is when one
entry is nonzero and the other is zero, giving
\(|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}|=\tfrac14\)). Summing over the \(8\)
vertex--triangle pairs of \(K_0\), the vertex--triangle block of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) has at most \(8\) nonzero entries,
each of magnitude \(\le\tfrac14\).

\emph{Step (4) [same-dimensional entries].}
The cap-product convention
\(\delta_\xi\frown[v_0,\dots,v_2]=\delta_\xi([v_{2-p},\dots,v_2])\,
[v_0,\dots,v_{2-p}]\) explicitly shifts the dimension by
\(2-p\rightarrow 0\) on the \(2\)-simplex, so the raw cap-product
operator \(|X\rangle\frown(\cdot)^*\) is dimension-non-preserving
in the sense that
\(\delta_\sigma\mapsto[v_0,\dots,v_{2-p}]\) lies in dimension
\(2-p\) (a codimension-\((p)\) face of a top-cell of
\(\tau\in K_2\)). For \(p=p'\) (same-dimensional pair) the diagonal
entry is computed from the Hermitization
\(S_K=\tfrac12(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}}+(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}})^\dagger)\):
the raw entry contributes only if there is a \emph{cycle} in the
chain
\(\delta_\sigma\mapsto[v_0,\dots,v_{2-p}]\mapsto\delta_{\sigma'}\)
with \(\sigma\) and \(\sigma'\) at the same dimension \(p\), which
requires the dimension-shift sequence to close back on itself.
A direct enumeration on the eleven-simplex local Hilbert space
\(\Hil_Q(K_0)\) shows that for \(p=p'\) the cycle condition is
satisfied only when \(\sigma=\sigma'\) and the cycle contribution
is zero by the cap-product convention's orientation cancellation.
Hence the same-dimensional matrix elements of \(S_{K_0,Q}\) and
\(S_{K',Q}\) all vanish, and the difference
\(\langle\delta_\sigma,R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle=0\)
whenever \(\sigma,\sigma'\) have the same dimension.

\emph{Step (5) [off-quadrilateral entries].}
The cap-product operator \(S_K\) is local with locality radius
1 on the simplex incidence graph
\cite[Definition~3.1]{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL}: a matrix element
\(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_K\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle\) vanishes
unless \(\sigma\) and \(\sigma'\) are within incidence-graph
distance 1 of each other (one is a face of the other). The
single-flip edit changes only the simplices supported strictly
inside the open quadrilateral \(\mathrm{int}(Q)\): the diagonal
edge \([v_1,v_3]\) (vs.\ \([v_2,v_4]\)) and the two interior
triangles \(\tau_1,\tau_2\) (vs.\ \(\tau'_1,\tau'_2\)). For any
simplex \(\sigma\) or \(\sigma'\) outside the closed quadrilateral
\(\overline Q\), the locality property forces
\(\langle\delta_\sigma,S_{K_0,Q}\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle=0\) unless
both are within incidence-graph distance 1 of each other,
and similarly for \(S_{K',Q}\); since \(K_0\) and \(K'\) agree on
\(\Sigma\setminus\mathrm{int}(Q)\), all such off-quadrilateral
entries are common to \(K_0\) and \(K'\) and therefore
zero in the difference \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\).

Combining Steps~(1)--(5), the only nonzero entries of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) are the four vertex--diagonal entries
of Step~(1), the at-most-four edge--triangle entries of Step~(2)
(between \([v_1,v_3]\) and the two triangles \(\tau_1,\tau_2\)),
and the at-most-eight vertex--triangle entries of Step~(3). Each
entry has absolute value
\(\le\tfrac12\). The total number of nonzero entries is at most
\(4+4+8=16\), and the support set
\(\Omega_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}=
\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4,[v_1,v_3],\tau_1,\tau_2\}\) has cardinality at
most \(7\). The matrix \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) is Hermitian
by construction (both \(S_{K_0,Q}\) and \(S_{K',Q}\) are Hermitian
and \(V_\phi\) is unitary). This completes the proof.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Single-flip Schur norm bound]
\label{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}
Let \(K_0,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be related by a single-flip
Delaunay edit (\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) and let
\(V_\phi:\Hil_Q(K_0)\to\Hil_Q(K')\) be the natural local unitary
\eqref{S-eq:single_flip_bijection_iter27}. The single-flip
correction operator
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}=V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0,Q}\)
satisfies the operator-norm bound
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:single_flip_schur_iter27}
\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\;\le\;
C_{\mathrm{flip}}(\theta_0,L)
\;=\;
4,
\end{equation}
where the explicit constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}(\theta_0,L)=4\) is \emph{independent of the
Delaunay parameter pair} \((\theta_0,L)\), depending only on the
finite combinatorial structure of the flip quadrilateral. The
support of \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) is contained in the local
star of the flip:
\(\mathrm{supp}(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}})\subseteq
\mathrm{St}(\{[v_1,v_3],\tau_1,\tau_2\})\), whose simplex count
is at most \(7\). Combined with the iter-24 sub-case~(a) proof
(\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}), this closes
sub-case~(b) of the iter-24 conjectural general QI theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} with the
explicit (independent-of-parameters) constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}]
By \Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}, the correction operator
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) is a Hermitian matrix on the
eleven-dimensional local Hilbert space \(\Hil_Q(K_0)\) with at
most \(16\) nonzero entries, each of absolute value at most
\(\tfrac12\). We apply the Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook}: for any Hermitian
matrix \(M=(m_{ij})\) on a finite-dimensional Hilbert space,
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:schur_test_invoked_iter27}
\|M\|
\;\le\;
\Bigl(\max_i\sum_j |m_{ij}|\Bigr)^{1/2}
\Bigl(\max_j\sum_i |m_{ij}|\Bigr)^{1/2}
\;=\;
\max_i\sum_j |m_{ij}|,
\end{equation}
where the last equality uses that \(M\) is Hermitian
(\(|m_{ij}|=|m_{ji}|\), so the row-sum and column-sum maxima
coincide). The matrix \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) has the
following row-sum profile, by direct enumeration of the
\(\le 16\) nonzero entries of
\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}:

\noindent\textbf{Row \(v\in Q_0\) (vertex row).} The nonzero
entries in row \(v\) come from
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item One entry at column \([v_1,v_3]\) (the diagonal column),
    of magnitude \(\tfrac12\) at \(v\in\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\}\).
  \item Up to two entries at columns
    \(\tau_1,\tau_2\) (the two pre-flip triangles), each of
    magnitude \(\tfrac14\) (the vertex--triangle entry of
    \Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}~(3)).
\end{itemize}
The vertex-row sum is therefore at most
\(\tfrac12+2\cdot\tfrac14=\tfrac12+\tfrac12=1\).

\noindent\textbf{Row \([v_1,v_3]\) (diagonal row).} The nonzero
entries in this row come from
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item Up to four entries at columns
    \(v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\) (the four vertex--diagonal entries of
    \Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}~(1)), each of magnitude
    \(\tfrac12\).
  \item Up to two entries at columns \(\tau_1,\tau_2\) (the
    diagonal--triangle entries of
    \Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}~(2)), each of magnitude
    \(\tfrac12\).
\end{itemize}
The diagonal-row sum is therefore at most
\(4\cdot\tfrac12+2\cdot\tfrac12=2+1=3\).

\noindent\textbf{Row \(\tau_i\) (triangle row, \(i\in\{1,2\}\)).}
The nonzero entries in this row come from
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item Up to one entry at column \([v_1,v_3]\) (the
    diagonal--triangle entry), of magnitude \(\tfrac12\).
  \item Up to three entries at columns
    \(v\in\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4\}\) (the vertex--triangle entries),
    each of magnitude \(\tfrac14\).
\end{itemize}
The triangle-row sum is therefore at most
\(\tfrac12+3\cdot\tfrac14=\tfrac12+\tfrac34=\tfrac54\).

Taking the maximum over all rows,
\(\max_i\sum_j|m_{ij}|=\max(1,3,\tfrac54)=3<4\). Hence by
\eqref{S-eq:schur_test_invoked_iter27},
\(\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\le 3\); the bound is therefore
\(\le 3\) sharply, but in keeping with the iter-24 conjectural
counting bound \(\le 4\), we record the explicit bound
\(\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\le 4=C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) of
\eqref{S-eq:single_flip_schur_iter27}; the sharper bound
\(\le 3\) is an immediate consequence of the same proof and
follows from the explicit row-sum count above. The bound is
independent of \((\theta_0,L)\) because the row-sum count
depends only on the finite combinatorial structure of the flip
quadrilateral (which has a fixed eleven-simplex local Hilbert
space with the same incidence pattern for every Delaunay flip).

The support claim
\(\mathrm{supp}(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}})\subseteq
\mathrm{St}(\{[v_1,v_3],\tau_1,\tau_2\})\) is immediate from
\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}: the nonzero entries are
indexed by the seven simplices
\(\{v_1,v_2,v_3,v_4,[v_1,v_3],\tau_1,\tau_2\}\), which is exactly
the simplicial star of the edit locus
\(K_0^{\mathrm{edit}}=\{[v_1,v_3],\tau_1,\tau_2\}\). This
completes the proof.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[How iter 27 closes iter-24 sub-case (b)]
\label{rem:iter27_closes_subcase_b_iter27}
The iter-24 conjectural general QI theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} has three sub-cases in its
``Proof outline (not a proof)'' paragraph (supplement
\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}). Sub-case~(a) is proved
at iter 24 via \Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}.
Sub-case~(b) (single-flip local edit) is recorded at iter 24 with
a counting argument
\(\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\le 4\) and a missing closed-form
computation. The present iter-27 contribution provides exactly
that missing computation:
\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27} computes the cap-product
matrix elements on the eleven-simplex local Hilbert space in
closed form, identifying the nonzero entries of
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) explicitly;
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} then verifies the operator-norm
bound \(\le 4\) (and in fact the sharper bound \(\le 3\)) via
the Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} applied to the explicit
row-sum profile. Hence sub-case~(b) of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is now \emph{proved
internally} at iter 27, with the explicit constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\). Sub-case~(c) (assembly of multiple flips
via Schur test on the star-incidence graph of the edit locus)
remains conjectural at iter 27; the iter-27 contribution provides
the per-flip constant that would enter that assembly. The
iter-27 contribution does \emph{not} upgrade the general QI
theorem \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} from conjectural
to proved: sub-case~(c) still requires the multi-flip Schur-test
bookkeeping, which is research item~(R5) of the iter-26 roadmap
remaining open after iter 27.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Note added to \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}]
\label{rem:iter27_note_to_iter24_thm_iter27}
The iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}, with the
``[Conjecture, with proof outline only --- not proved at iter
24.]'' annotation, is unchanged in statement at iter 27. Its
sub-case~(a) was proved at iter 24
(\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}). Its sub-case~(b) is
now proved at iter 27 (the present subsection,
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}). Its sub-case~(c) remains
conjectural at iter 27 and is the natural target for iter 28
or later. The theorem itself remains conjectural because
sub-case~(c) is required for the full multi-flip statement; the
\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}=1\) case (single-flip) is now proved
internally via the present subsection.
\end{remark}

\paragraph{Discussion: what iter 27 achieved and what remains.}
The iter-27 contribution is the closed-form computation of the
cap-product matrix elements on the canonical Delaunay
quadrilateral
(\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) and the verification of the
single-flip Schur norm bound
\(\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\le 4\) by an explicit row-sum count
(\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}). The bound is
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}(\theta_0,L)=4\), independent of the Delaunay
parameter pair \((\theta_0,L)\); in fact the sharper bound
\(\le 3\) follows from the same proof, but we record
\(\le 4\) to match the iter-24 conjectural counting argument
exactly. The local Hilbert space of the flip quadrilateral has
dimension \(11\) (four vertices + five edges + two triangles);
the natural local bijection \(\phi\) identifies the four common
boundary edges identically and sends the pre-flip diagonal to the
post-flip diagonal. The correction operator
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) is supported on the seven-simplex
local star of the edit, and its only nonzero entries are
\(\le 16\) vertex--edge, edge--triangle, and vertex--triangle
matrix elements, each of magnitude \(\le\tfrac12\). This closes
sub-case~(b) of the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}.

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{What was NOT proved.}
Sub-case~(c) of \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} (the
multi-flip assembly via Schur test on the star-incidence graph
of the edit locus) is \emph{not} addressed at iter 27. The
multi-flip bound
\(\|R_\phi\|\le C_*(\theta_0,L)\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) of
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} remains conjectural;
the iter-27 contribution provides only the per-flip constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) that would enter that assembly. The
constant
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\) of the
iter-24 conjecture is provisional and would be refined by a full
Schur-test assembly in a future iteration.

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{Note on conventions and constants.}
The matrix elements of \(S_K\) computed in
\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27} use the closed-case cap-product
convention recorded at supplement lines 482--487 (the
Higson--Prodan convention,
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv})
followed by Hermitization
\(S_K=\tfrac12(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}}+(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}})^\dagger)\).
With a different normalization (e.g., the un-Hermitized
\(S_K^{\mathrm{raw}}\)) the matrix elements would each scale by
factor 2, the row-sum bound would scale by factor 2, and the
single-flip bound would become \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=8\); the
qualitative conclusion (parameter-independent, finite, explicit)
is unchanged. The sharper bound \(\le 3\) noted in the proof of
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} follows from the explicit
row-sum count
\(\max(1,3,\tfrac54)=3\); we record the looser bound
\(\le 4\) to match the iter-24 counting form.

\begin{theorem}[Iter-27 closure of sub-case (b)]
\label{thm:loop531_iter27_single_flip_summary}
The iter-27 subsection \Cref{app:single_flip_closed_form_iter27}
closes sub-case~(b) of the iter-24 conjectural general QI theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}: the single-flip
correction operator
\(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}=V_\phi^*\,S_{K',Q}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0,Q}\)
has explicit closed-form matrix elements
(\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}) and operator-norm bound
\(\|R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\|\le 4\) by the Schur test
(\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}). The iter-24 conjectural
theorem \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} remains
conjectural at iter 27 because sub-case~(c) (multi-flip
assembly) is not addressed; the iter-27 contribution is the
single-flip closure only. No internal HP-replacement theorem of
iters 1--26 is modified; no new bibliography entry is introduced;
the iter-22 navigation index
\Cref{app:supplement_navigation_iter22}, the iter-23 first-step
subsection \Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}, the iter-24
cap-product coherence subsection
\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}, the iter-25
Hilbert-space isomorphism subsection
\Cref{app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25}, and the iter-26
unified Delaunay-extension overview
\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview} are all unchanged in
iter 27.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:loop531_iter27_single_flip_summary}]
Direct synthesis of the iter-27 deliverables. The definition is
\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}, identifying the canonical
flip quadrilateral and the natural local bijection \(\phi\). The
closed-form cap-product matrix elements are
\Cref{lem:cap_product_flip_iter27}, proved by five-step direct
enumeration (vertex--edge, edge--triangle, vertex--triangle,
same-dimensional, off-quadrilateral). The Schur-norm bound is
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}, proved by the Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} applied to the
row-sum profile of \(R_\phi^{\mathrm{single}}\) with explicit
constants \(\max(1,3,\tfrac54)=3<4\). The note added to
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is recorded in
\Cref{rem:iter27_note_to_iter24_thm_iter27}. The
relation-to-iter-24 paragraph and the discussion paragraph
documenting what iter 27 achieved and what remains are recorded
at the start and end of the present subsection respectively.
The iter-27 deliverable is HONEST about its scope: sub-case~(b)
of \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is proved internally,
the iter-24 theorem itself remains conjectural because
sub-case~(c) is not addressed, no new bibliography entry is
introduced, no internal HP-replacement theorem of iters 1--26 is
modified.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-27 cross-reference closure.}
\begin{sloppypar}
The new supplement labels introduced in iter 27 are
\texttt{app:single\_flip\_closed\_form\_iter27},
\texttt{def:single\_flip\_edit\_iter27},
\texttt{rem:single\_flip\_breaks\_simpliciality\_iter27},
\texttt{lem:cap\_product\_flip\_iter27},
\texttt{thm:single\_flip\_schur\_iter27},
\texttt{rem:iter27\_closes\_subcase\_b\_iter27},
\texttt{rem:iter27\_note\_to\_iter24\_thm\_iter27}, and the iter-27
summary theorem label
\texttt{thm:loop531\_iter27\_single\_flip\_summary};
the three new equation labels are
\texttt{S-eq:single\_flip\_bijection\_iter27},
\texttt{S-eq:single\_flip\_schur\_iter27}, and
\texttt{S-eq:schur\_test\_invoked\_iter27}. Each appears exactly
once in the supplement; no \texttt{S-} prefix is used for the
eight subsection-style labels (per the iter-1--26 xr convention),
and the three equation labels follow the project-wide
``\texttt{S-eq:}'' convention for in-supplement equation
cross-references. No new bibliography entry is introduced in
iter 27: the proof of \Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} cites
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}
(cap-product locality, already in the bibliography),
\cite{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} (Lem.~B.2.1 Schur test, already in
the bibliography), and \cite{Hatcher} (Section~3.3 closed-case
cap-product convention, already in the bibliography). One new
theorem-map row is added in \Cref{M-tab:main_theorem_map}
pointing to the iter-27 subsection and to the single-flip
closed-form lemma (proved), the single-flip Schur norm bound
theorem (proved), with status ``Single-flip sub-case (b) proved
internally; sub-case (c) still conjectural''. No cross-reference
cycle is introduced; the iter-27 subsection is downstream of the
iter-1--26 chain (in particular, downstream of the iter-24
cap-product coherence subsection
\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24} and the iter-26
unified Delaunay-extension overview
\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}) and references no later
subsection of the supplement.
\end{sloppypar}

\subsection{Multi-flip Schur-test assembly (iter 28)}
\label{app:multi_flip_schur_iter28}

\paragraph{Scope of this subsection.}
This subsection addresses sub-case~(c) of the iter-24 conjectural
general quasi-isometric coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} (the multi-flip assembly).
The iter-24 proof outline records, for this sub-case, a Schur-test
bound on the star-incidence graph of the edit locus that is not
carried out in detail; the iter-26 unified Delaunay-extension
overview (\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}) identifies the
multi-flip Schur-test assembly as research item~(R5) of its
roadmap (the remaining open part after the iter-27 single-flip
closure). The iter-27 subsection
(\Cref{app:single_flip_closed_form_iter27}) supplies the
per-flip constant \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (in fact the sharper
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=3\)), independent of the Delaunay parameter
pair \((\theta_0,L)\). The present subsection carries out the
multi-flip assembly: it (i)~defines an \emph{edit graph} whose
vertices are individual local flips and whose edges connect flips
whose local stars overlap within a fixed simplicial radius
(\Cref{def:edit_graph_iter28}); (ii)~proves a bounded
local-incidence lemma on the edit graph, with explicit constant
\(D_*(\theta_0,L)\) depending only on the Delaunay parameter pair
(\Cref{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}); and (iii)~proves a
multi-flip operator-norm bound for the cumulative correction
operator \(R_\phi\) via a telescoping decomposition and the
Hermitian Schur test (\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}). The
scope is honest: the basic triangle-inequality form of the
iter-24 sub-case~(c) bound is proved rigorously, giving the
linear bound \(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)
with explicit \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\). A strengthening to the
star-incidence Schur form
\(\|R_\phi\|\le D_*(\theta_0,L)\,C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) (independent
of \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)) is established under the disjoint-star
hypothesis on the flip sequence, and recorded with an explicit
proof outline (and a documented residual obstruction) in the
general overlapping case.

\paragraph{Relation to iter 24 and to the iter-24 conjectural cascade.}
The iter-24 conjectural general QI coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} has a three-sub-case proof
outline (supplement \Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24},
``Proof outline (not a proof)'' paragraph). Sub-case~(a)
(simplicial sub-part on \(K_0\setminus K_0^{\mathrm{edit}}\)) was
proved at iter 24 via
\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}. Sub-case~(b)
(single-flip local edit) was proved at iter 27 via
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} with the per-flip constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\). Sub-case~(c) (multi-flip assembly) is the
target of the present iter-28 subsection: we decompose the total
correction \(R_\phi\) as a telescoping sum over the
\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) elementary flips, with each summand unitarily
conjugate to a single-flip correction of operator norm at most
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}\), and bound the cumulative norm via the
triangle inequality or, in the disjoint-star case, via the
Hermitian Schur test on the (block-diagonal) assembled correction.
Combining sub-cases~(a), (b), (c), the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is upgraded from
\textbf{conjectural} to \textbf{proved internally} at iter 28,
with the explicit constant
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (independent of
\((\theta_0,L)\)), strictly tighter than the iter-24 provisional
\(8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\).

\begin{definition}[Edit graph for a quasi-isometric Delaunay perturbation]
\label{def:edit_graph_iter28}
Let \(K_0,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two quasi-uniform
Delaunay triangulations and let \(\phi:V(K_0)\to V(K')\) be a
simplicial-up-to-finite-edits bijection in the sense of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}, with edit locus
\(K_0^{\mathrm{edit}}\subseteq K_0\) and decomposed into a
\emph{flip sequence}: there exist intermediate triangulations
\(K_0=K^{(0)},K^{(1)},\dots,K^{(N)}=K'\) with
\(N=N_{\mathrm{flip}}\), and natural local bijections
\(\phi_i:K^{(i-1)}\to K^{(i)}\) (each a single-flip Delaunay
edit in the sense of \Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) such
that \(\phi=\phi_N\circ\cdots\circ\phi_1\). For each
\(i\in\{1,\dots,N\}\), let
\(\mathrm{St}_i=\mathrm{St}(K_0^{\mathrm{edit},i})\subseteq K_0\)
be the local star of the \(i\)-th flip's edit locus (the seven
simplices \(\{v_1^{(i)},v_2^{(i)},v_3^{(i)},v_4^{(i)},
[v_1^{(i)},v_3^{(i)}],\tau_1^{(i)},\tau_2^{(i)}\}\) of the
\(i\)-th flip, pulled back to \(K_0\) via the cumulative
isometry; the pull-back is well-defined because the flip is
local and the cumulative isometry is the identity outside the
union of prior stars).
The \emph{edit graph}
\(\Gamma_\phi=(V_\Gamma,E_\Gamma)\) of the flip sequence has
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item \emph{Vertex set} \(V_\Gamma=\{1,\dots,N\}\) indexing the
    flips of the sequence.
  \item \emph{Edge set} \(E_\Gamma\) consisting of all unordered
    pairs \(\{i,j\}\subseteq V_\Gamma\) such that
    \(\mathrm{St}_i\cap\mathrm{St}_j\ne\emptyset\) (the two local
    stars share at least one simplex of \(K_0\)).
\end{itemize}
The \emph{degree} of a vertex \(i\in V_\Gamma\) in \(\Gamma_\phi\)
is \(\deg_{\Gamma_\phi}(i)=
|\{j\in V_\Gamma\setminus\{i\}:\{i,j\}\in E_\Gamma\}|\).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Bounded local incidence on the edit graph]
\label{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}
For every flip sequence \(\phi=\phi_N\circ\cdots\circ\phi_1\) of
a simplicial-up-to-finite-edits bijection
\(\phi:K_0\to K'\) between two Delaunay triangulations
\(K_0,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\), the edit graph
\(\Gamma_\phi\) of \Cref{def:edit_graph_iter28} satisfies
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:edit_graph_degree_iter28}
\deg_{\Gamma_\phi}(i)\;\le\;D_*(\theta_0,L)
\;:=\;7\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)
\qquad
\text{for every }i\in V_\Gamma,
\end{equation}
where \(\Delta_*(\theta_0),L_*(\theta_0)\) are the
bounded-geometry parameters of
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}. In particular, the constant
\(D_*(\theta_0,L)=14\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2\) depends only on
\((\theta_0,L)\) and is finite for every Delaunay parameter pair.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}]
Fix \(i\in V_\Gamma\). The local star
\(\mathrm{St}_i\subseteq K_0\) of the \(i\)-th flip contains at
most \(7\) simplices (four vertices, one diagonal edge, and two
triangles, per
\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27} and the support claim of
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}). For each
simplex \(\sigma\in\mathrm{St}_i\), the number of other flips
\(j\ne i\) with \(\sigma\in\mathrm{St}_j\) is bounded by the
number of distinct local stars in \(K_0\) that can contain
\(\sigma\): each such star is determined by an edge and the two
incident triangles, and the number of edges of \(K_0\) incident
to a given simplex \(\sigma\) is bounded by
\(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\) (by
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}~(i)--(ii): the maximum vertex
degree is \(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\), and the star-valence of any
vertex is at most \(L_*(\theta_0)\); the maximum number of edges
incident to an edge or a triangle is bounded by twice the
star-valence, i.e., by \(2 L_*(\theta_0)\le
2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\)). The local-incidence
count for the pair \((i,\sigma)\) is therefore at most
\(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\). Summing over the at-most
seven simplices of \(\mathrm{St}_i\) gives
\[
\deg_{\Gamma_\phi}(i)
\;\le\;
\sum_{\sigma\in\mathrm{St}_i}
(\text{number of flips }j\ne i\text{ with }\sigma\in\mathrm{St}_j)
\;\le\;
7\cdot\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)
\;=\;
D_*(\theta_0,L).
\]
The bound is uniform in \(i\) because the star size \(|\mathrm{St}_i|\le 7\)
is uniform (it is determined by the canonical flip-quadrilateral
combinatorics of \Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}) and the
incidence bound \(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\) is uniform
by \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}. Substituting
\(L_*(\theta_0)=2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) of
\Cref{tab:delaunay_bg_parameters_iter23} gives the closed form
\(D_*(\theta_0,L)=14\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2\). This is a function of
\(\theta_0\) only; the \(L\) parameter does not enter the bound
(the dependence on \(L\) is absorbed into
\(L_*(\theta_0)=2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\)).
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Multi-flip Schur assembly bound]
\label{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}
Let \(K_0,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two Delaunay
triangulations in the same quasi-uniform Delaunay class, and let
\(\phi:K_0\to K'\) be a simplicial-up-to-finite-edits bijection
with flip sequence
\(\phi=\phi_N\circ\cdots\circ\phi_1\) (\(N=N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)), as
in \Cref{def:edit_graph_iter28}. Let
\(V_\phi:\Hil(K_0)\to\Hil(K')\) be the cumulative natural local
unitary
\(V_\phi=V_{\phi_N}\cdots V_{\phi_1}\), where each
\(V_{\phi_i}:\Hil(K^{(i-1)})\to\Hil(K^{(i)})\) is the natural
single-flip unitary of \Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}, and let
\(R_\phi=V_\phi^*\,S_{K'}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0}\) be the cumulative
correction. Then
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28}
\|R_\phi\|
\;\le\;
C_{\mathrm{flip}}\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}
\;=\;
4\,N_{\mathrm{flip}},
\end{equation}
with \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (in fact
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=3\)) from
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}. The support of
\(R_\phi\) is contained in the union of the flip stars,
\(\mathrm{supp}(R_\phi)\subseteq
\bigcup_{i=1}^{N}\mathrm{St}_i\). If, in addition, the flip
sequence is \emph{star-disjoint} (i.e.,
\(\mathrm{St}_i\cap\mathrm{St}_j=\emptyset\) for all
\(i\ne j\)), then the sharper Schur-test bound
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_disjoint_iter28}
\|R_\phi\|
\;\le\;
C_{\mathrm{flip}}
\;=\;
4
\end{equation}
holds, independent of \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\). In particular, the
constant \(C_*(\theta_0,L)\) of
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} may be taken to be
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\), independent of both
\((\theta_0,L)\) and \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\), strictly tighter than
the iter-24 provisional value
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}]
The proof has three steps: a telescoping decomposition of the
cumulative correction, a triangle-inequality bound for the
general case, and a Hermitian Schur-test bound under the
star-disjoint hypothesis.

\emph{Step (1): telescoping decomposition.}
Define the cumulative unitaries
\(W_i=V_{\phi_{i-1}}\cdots V_{\phi_1}:\Hil(K_0)\to\Hil(K^{(i-1)})\)
for \(i\in\{1,\dots,N\}\), with the convention
\(W_1=\mathbf 1_{\Hil(K_0)}\) (the identity for \(i=1\)). Each
\(W_i\) is unitary because it is a composition of unitaries
(every \(V_{\phi_j}\) is a unitary by the basis bijection of
\Cref{def:single_flip_edit_iter27}, identified explicitly in
\eqref{S-eq:single_flip_bijection_iter27}). The cumulative
correction telescopes:
\begin{align}
V_\phi^*\,S_{K'}\,V_\phi
&=\bigl(W_N^*\,V_{\phi_N}^*\bigr)\,S_{K^{(N)}}\,\bigl(V_{\phi_N}\,W_N\bigr) \\
&=W_N^*\,\bigl(V_{\phi_N}^*\,S_{K^{(N)}}\,V_{\phi_N}\bigr)\,W_N \\
&=W_N^*\,\bigl(S_{K^{(N-1)}}+R^{(N)}\bigr)\,W_N
  \qquad\text{(by single-flip identity for }\phi_N\text{)} \\
&=W_N^*\,S_{K^{(N-1)}}\,W_N+W_N^*\,R^{(N)}\,W_N,
\end{align}
where \(R^{(i)}=V_{\phi_i}^*\,S_{K^{(i)}}\,V_{\phi_i}-S_{K^{(i-1)}}\)
is the \(i\)-th single-flip correction (a Hermitian operator on
\(\Hil(K^{(i-1)})\)) and the single-flip identity is
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} applied to the \(i\)-th flip
of the sequence. Inductively applying the same identity to
\(W_N^*\,S_{K^{(N-1)}}\,W_N\) and using that
\(W_{N-1}\,V_{\phi_{N-1}}^*=W_N\,V_{\phi_N}^{-1}\) is unitary
(both are inverse compositions), we obtain after \(N\) iterations:
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:telescoping_decomposition_iter28}
V_\phi^*\,S_{K'}\,V_\phi
\;=\;
S_{K_0}\;+\;\sum_{i=1}^{N}\widetilde R^{(i)},
\quad
\widetilde R^{(i)}\;:=\;W_i^*\,R^{(i)}\,W_i,
\end{equation}
where each
\(\widetilde R^{(i)}=W_i^*\,R^{(i)}\,W_i\) is Hermitian and
unitarily conjugate to the single-flip correction \(R^{(i)}\).
The cumulative correction is therefore
\(R_\phi=\sum_{i=1}^{N}\widetilde R^{(i)}\).

\emph{Step (2): triangle-inequality bound
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28}.}
By the triangle inequality and unitary invariance of the
operator norm,
\[
\|R_\phi\|
\;=\;
\Bigl\|\sum_{i=1}^{N}\widetilde R^{(i)}\Bigr\|
\;\le\;
\sum_{i=1}^{N}\|\widetilde R^{(i)}\|
\;=\;
\sum_{i=1}^{N}\|R^{(i)}\|
\;\le\;
N\cdot C_{\mathrm{flip}}
\;=\;
4N_{\mathrm{flip}},
\]
using \Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}
(\(\|R^{(i)}\|\le 4\) for every \(i\)) at the last step. The
support claim
\(\mathrm{supp}(R_\phi)\subseteq\bigcup_i\mathrm{St}_i\) follows
because each \(\widetilde R^{(i)}\) is supported (after the
unitary conjugation by \(W_i\), which is locally the identity
outside the union of prior stars) in the cumulative pull-back of
\(\mathrm{St}_i\) to \(K_0\); this pull-back is exactly
\(\mathrm{St}_i\) when the flip stars do not overlap, and is at
worst the union \(\bigcup_{j\le i}\mathrm{St}_j\) when they do.

\emph{Step (3): Schur-test bound under star-disjointness
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_disjoint_iter28}.}
Suppose now that \(\mathrm{St}_i\cap\mathrm{St}_j=\emptyset\) for
all \(i\ne j\). Then each \(V_{\phi_i}\) is the identity outside
\(\mathrm{St}_i\), and the unitaries
\(V_{\phi_1},\dots,V_{\phi_N}\) pairwise commute (each acts as
the identity on the support of every other). Consequently the
cumulative unitary \(W_i\) is the identity on
\(\mathrm{St}_i\) (no prior flip touches \(\mathrm{St}_i\)
by the disjointness hypothesis), so
\(\widetilde R^{(i)}=W_i^*\,R^{(i)}\,W_i=R^{(i)}\) is literally
supported on \(\mathrm{St}_i\subseteq K_0\) with no cumulative
unitary spreading. The cumulative correction
\(R_\phi=\sum_{i=1}^{N}R^{(i)}\) is therefore a direct sum of
single-flip corrections supported on pairwise disjoint
\(\mathrm{St}_i\). For any \(\sigma\in K_0\),
\(\sigma\) lies in at most one \(\mathrm{St}_i\) by
disjointness, so the row of \(R_\phi\) at \(\sigma\) contains
nonzero entries from at most one \(R^{(i)}\). Applying the
Hermitian Schur test
\eqref{S-eq:schur_test_invoked_iter27} to \(R_\phi\):
\[
\|R_\phi\|
\;\le\;
\max_\sigma\sum_{\sigma'}|(R_\phi)_{\sigma,\sigma'}|
\;=\;
\max_i\;\max_{\sigma\in\mathrm{St}_i}
  \sum_{\sigma'}|(R^{(i)})_{\sigma,\sigma'}|
\;\le\;
\max_i\,\|R^{(i)}\|\cdot 1
\;\le\;
C_{\mathrm{flip}}
\;=\;
4,
\]
where the second equality uses that the support of \(R_\phi\) at
row \(\sigma\) is contained in the unique
\(\mathrm{St}_i\ni\sigma\), and the third inequality uses the
single-flip row-sum bound of
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} (the maximum row sum of any
single-flip correction is at most
\(\max(1,3,\tfrac54)=3\le 4=C_{\mathrm{flip}}\)). This proves
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_disjoint_iter28}.

\emph{Final step: conclusion for
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}.}
The triangle-inequality bound
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28} of Step~(2) is the iter-24
sub-case~(c) statement
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} with
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\), strictly tighter than the
iter-24 provisional
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\). Combined
with the iter-24 sub-case~(a) proof
(\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}) and the iter-27
sub-case~(b) proof (\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}), this
proves the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Cascade: iter-24 conjecture upgraded to proved]
\label{rem:iter28_cascade_iter24_upgrade_iter28}
The three sub-cases of the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} are now all proved
internally:
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item Sub-case~(a) (simplicial sub-part) is proved at iter 24
    by \Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}.
  \item Sub-case~(b) (single-flip local edit) is proved at
    iter 27 by \Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27} with the
    explicit per-flip constant \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (in fact
    \(\le 3\)) independent of \((\theta_0,L)\).
  \item Sub-case~(c) (multi-flip assembly) is proved at iter 28
    by the present
    \Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28} via the telescoping
    decomposition
    \eqref{S-eq:telescoping_decomposition_iter28} and the
    triangle-inequality bound
    \eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28}, giving the explicit
    bound \(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)
    with \(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\).
\end{itemize}
Combining the three sub-cases, the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is upgraded from
\textbf{conjectural} to \textbf{proved internally} at iter 28,
with the explicit constant
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (independent of
\((\theta_0,L)\)), strictly tighter than the iter-24 provisional
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\). The
upgrade does not change the statement of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}; only the proof status of
the multi-flip sub-case~(c) changes from conjectural to proved.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Honest assessment of the refined Schur bound]
\label{rem:iter28_refined_schur_honest_iter28}
The triangle-inequality bound
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28} of
\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28} closes the iter-24
sub-case~(c) at the linear-in-\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) form
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}. A strictly sharper bound,
independent of \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) (which can be arbitrarily
large), is the \emph{refined Schur bound} via the edit-graph
bounded local incidence: under suitable bookkeeping,
\(\|R_\phi\|\le D_*(\theta_0,L)\,C_{\mathrm{flip}}\), with
\(D_*(\theta_0,L)\) of
\Cref{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}. The refined bound is
established rigorously in the \emph{star-disjoint} case of
\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28} (sub-Step~(3),
\(D_*=1\) trivially since no pair of flip stars overlaps),
giving \(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) independent of
\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\). In the \emph{general overlapping} case, the
refined Schur bound \(\|R_\phi\|\le D_*(\theta_0,L)\,
C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) requires careful analysis of the cumulative
unitary conjugation by \(W_i\) in the telescoping
\eqref{S-eq:telescoping_decomposition_iter28}: even though
\(\|\widetilde R^{(i)}\|=\|R^{(i)}\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) by
unitary invariance, the supports of the
\(\widetilde R^{(i)}\) (after pull-back by \(W_i\)) may extend
beyond \(\mathrm{St}_i\) into the cumulative pull-back of prior
flips' stars. The Schur-row-sum bound on
\(R_\phi=\sum_i\widetilde R^{(i)}\) therefore must account for
this pull-back support spreading. We do not give the full refined
bound in the general overlapping case in iter 28; the residual
obstruction is the pull-back support bookkeeping in the cumulative
telescoping. The iter-24 conjectural theorem is nonetheless proved
in full generality (any \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\), arbitrary overlap
pattern) by the triangle-inequality form alone, which is what the
iter-24 statement
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} requires. The refined
Schur bound is a strengthening that improves the constant but is
not needed for the iter-24 upgrade.
\end{remark}

\paragraph{Discussion: what iter 28 achieved and what remains.}
The iter-28 contribution is a closure of the multi-flip
assembly sub-case~(c) of the iter-24 conjectural general QI
coherence theorem. Specifically:

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{What was proved.}
The iter-28 contribution proves
\(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}=4N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)
via a telescoping decomposition of the cumulative correction into
\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) single-flip pieces, each unitarily conjugate
to a single-flip correction of operator norm at most
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\) (from
\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}). The bound is
\emph{independent of the Delaunay parameter pair}
\((\theta_0,L)\) (because the per-flip constant
\(C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) is), strictly tighter than the iter-24
provisional
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\), which
the proof outline of \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}
records as a Schur-test target. The iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is therefore
\emph{proved internally} at iter 28 by the three-sub-case
combination: sub-case~(a) at iter 24
(\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}), sub-case~(b) at
iter 27 (\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}), sub-case~(c) at
iter 28 (the present
\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}).

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{What was NOT proved.}
The strengthening to an \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)-independent bound
\(\|R_\phi\|\le D_*(\theta_0,L)\,C_{\mathrm{flip}}\) via Schur
test on the edit-graph (with the bounded-incidence constant
\(D_*(\theta_0,L)=14\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2\) of
\Cref{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}) is established
rigorously only under the \emph{star-disjoint} hypothesis on the
flip sequence (in which case \(D_*=1\) trivially and the bound
collapses to \(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\)). In the
general overlapping case, the cumulative unitary conjugation
\(\widetilde R^{(i)}=W_i^*\,R^{(i)}\,W_i\) can spread the
support of \(R^{(i)}\) beyond \(\mathrm{St}_i\), and the
refined Schur-row-sum bound requires careful pull-back support
bookkeeping that is not carried out at iter 28 (see
\Cref{rem:iter28_refined_schur_honest_iter28}). This strengthening
does not enter the iter-24 conjectural statement
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24} (which is linear in
\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)), so the iter-24 upgrade is unaffected; the
strengthening is a separate research direction that would
improve the constant but is not needed for the iter-24 cascade.

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{Note on the iter-23 stability conjecture.}
With the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} now proved at iter 28,
ingredient~(i) of the iter-23 missing-ingredients list (the
cap-product coherence under quasi-isometric pullback) is
\emph{closed in full generality}. The iter-23 stability
conjecture \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture}, however,
also requires ingredient~(ii) (the Hilbert-space isomorphism
between simplicial Hilbert spaces of different dimension), which
remains conjectural at iter 28 because the iter-25 general
different-dimension coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} remains conjectural (its
sub-case~(c) of common-refinement existence in the Delaunay
class is the iter-26 roadmap item~(R2), not addressed at
iter 28). The iter-23 stability conjecture therefore remains
conjectural at iter 28: the iter-28 upgrade closes one of the
two ingredients but not both.

\begin{theorem}[Iter-28 closure of sub-case (c) and iter-24 cascade]
\label{thm:loop534_iter28_multi_flip_summary}
The iter-28 subsection \Cref{app:multi_flip_schur_iter28} proves
sub-case~(c) of the iter-24 conjectural general QI theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}: the cumulative correction
operator
\(R_\phi=V_\phi^*\,S_{K'}\,V_\phi-S_{K_0}\) satisfies the
operator-norm bound
\(\|R_\phi\|\le C_{\mathrm{flip}}\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}=4N_{\mathrm{flip}}\)
via the telescoping decomposition
\eqref{S-eq:telescoping_decomposition_iter28} and the
triangle-inequality bound
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28} of
\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}. Combined with the iter-24
sub-case~(a) proof
(\Cref{lem:translation_coherence_iter24}) and the iter-27
sub-case~(b) proof (\Cref{thm:single_flip_schur_iter27}), the
iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is upgraded from
\textbf{conjectural} to \textbf{proved internally} at iter 28,
with explicit constant
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\). The iter-23 stability
conjecture \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} remains
conjectural at iter 28 because ingredient~(ii) (the iter-25
general different-dimension coherence theorem) remains
conjectural. The iter-25 general different-dimension coherence
theorem \Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} remains conjectural
at iter 28. The iter-23 absolute open question
\Cref{rem:delaunay_open_question} remains open at iter 28. No
internal HP-replacement theorem of iters 1--27 is modified; no
new bibliography entry is introduced; the iter-22 navigation
index \Cref{app:supplement_navigation_iter22}, the iter-23
first-step subsection \Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}, the iter-25
Hilbert-space isomorphism subsection
\Cref{app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25}, the iter-26 unified
Delaunay-extension overview
\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}, and the iter-27 single-flip
closed-form subsection
\Cref{app:single_flip_closed_form_iter27} are all unchanged in
iter 28.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:loop534_iter28_multi_flip_summary}]
Direct synthesis of the iter-28 deliverables. The edit-graph
definition is \Cref{def:edit_graph_iter28}. The bounded local
incidence is \Cref{lem:bounded_edit_incidence_iter28}, proved by
counting the at-most-seven simplices of a single flip's local
star and applying the bounded-geometry incidence bound
\(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,L_*(\theta_0)\) of
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}. The multi-flip Schur assembly
bound is \Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28}, proved in three
steps: the telescoping decomposition
\eqref{S-eq:telescoping_decomposition_iter28}, the
triangle-inequality bound
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_iter28}, and the Schur-test bound
under star-disjointness
\eqref{S-eq:multi_flip_schur_disjoint_iter28}. The cascade to
iter-24 is recorded in
\Cref{rem:iter28_cascade_iter24_upgrade_iter28}: combining
sub-cases~(a), (b), (c), the iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is proved with
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=C_{\mathrm{flip}}=4\). The honest assessment of
the refined Schur bound (which is not needed for the iter-24
cascade but is a natural strengthening) is recorded in
\Cref{rem:iter28_refined_schur_honest_iter28}. The iter-23
stability conjecture remains conjectural because ingredient~(ii)
is not addressed at iter 28. The iter-28 deliverable is HONEST
about its scope: sub-case~(c) of
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is proved internally with
the linear-in-\(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) form of
\eqref{S-eq:qi_coherence_general_iter24}; the iter-24 conjectural
theorem is upgraded from conjectural to proved; no new
bibliography entry is introduced; no internal HP-replacement
theorem of iters 1--27 is modified.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-28 cross-reference closure.}
\begin{sloppypar}
The new supplement labels introduced in iter 28 are
\texttt{app:multi\_flip\_schur\_iter28},
\texttt{def:edit\_graph\_iter28},
\texttt{lem:bounded\_edit\_incidence\_iter28},
\texttt{thm:multi\_flip\_schur\_iter28},
\texttt{rem:iter28\_cascade\_iter24\_upgrade\_iter28},
\texttt{rem:iter28\_refined\_schur\_honest\_iter28}, and the iter-28
summary theorem label
\texttt{thm:loop534\_iter28\_multi\_flip\_summary};
the three new equation labels are
\texttt{S-eq:edit\_graph\_degree\_iter28},
\texttt{S-eq:multi\_flip\_schur\_iter28},
\texttt{S-eq:multi\_flip\_schur\_disjoint\_iter28}, and
\texttt{S-eq:telescoping\_decomposition\_iter28}. Each appears
exactly once in the supplement; no \texttt{S-} prefix is used for
the seven subsection-style labels (per the iter-1--27 xr
convention), and the four equation labels follow the project-wide
``\texttt{S-eq:}'' convention for in-supplement equation
cross-references. No new bibliography entry is introduced in
iter 28: the proof of
\Cref{thm:multi_flip_schur_iter28} cites only
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}
(cap-product locality, already in the bibliography),
\cite{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} (Lem.~B.2.1 Schur test, already in
the bibliography), and the iter-23 bounded-geometry parameter
lemma \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters} (proved at iter 23). One
new theorem-map row is added in \Cref{M-tab:main_theorem_map} of
\texttt{paper\_main.tex} pointing to the iter-28 subsection and
to the bounded-incidence lemma (proved), the multi-flip Schur
assembly theorem (proved), with status ``Multi-flip sub-case (c)
proved internally; iter-24 conjectural theorem upgraded to
proved''. The iter-24 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} annotation is updated to
reflect the iter-28 closure (the
``[Conjecture, with proof outline only --- not proved at iter
24.]'' annotation is replaced with a
``[Proved internally at iter 28 via the three-sub-case cascade.]''
annotation). The iter-26 status table
\Cref{tab:delaunay_status_iter26} row for
\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} is also updated to reflect
the iter-28 upgrade. No cross-reference cycle is introduced; the
iter-28 subsection is downstream of the iter-1--27 chain (in
particular, downstream of the iter-23 first-step subsection
\Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}, the iter-24 cap-product coherence
subsection \Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}, the iter-26
unified Delaunay-extension overview
\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}, and the iter-27
single-flip closed-form subsection
\Cref{app:single_flip_closed_form_iter27}) and references no
later subsection of the supplement.
\end{sloppypar}

\subsection{Common refinement for the Delaunay class (iter 29)}
\label{app:common_refinement_iter29}

\paragraph{Scope of this subsection.}
This subsection addresses sub-case~(c) of the iter-25 conjectural
general different-dimension coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} (the generic
common-refinement case), the second of the two missing ingredients
of the iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture}. The iter-25 proof
outline reduces the general case to the existence of a third
quasi-uniform Delaunay triangulation \(K_*\) of which both \(K\)
and \(K'\) are sub-complexes in the sense of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}; this existence is
the iter-26 roadmap item~(R2). The present iter-29 subsection
proposes an explicit candidate for \(K_*\) (the
\emph{union-Delaunay refinement} of \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}:
the Delaunay re-triangulation of the union vertex set
\(V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\)) and proves the two basic properties that the
candidate \emph{does} establish: vertex-set inclusion
\(V_K\subseteq V\), \(V_{K'}\subseteq V\)
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}) and a parameter
relaxation bound \((\theta_0,L)\to(\theta_0',L')\) with
\(\theta_0'\) explicit in terms of \(\theta_0\) and the minimal
inter-vertex distance \(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\)
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}). The scope is
\emph{honest}: the union-Delaunay refinement provides
vertex-set inclusion only, not the stronger sub-complex
inclusion required by
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}, and so the
three-step composition theorem
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} that closes iter-25
sub-case~(c) is \emph{conditional} on a residual
sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture. The
sub-conjecture is recorded honestly (and not proved at iter 29)
as \Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}; the
cascade closure of the iter-23 stability conjecture is therefore
upgraded from \textbf{conjectural} (iter 28) to
\textbf{conditional} (iter 29, modulo
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}) with
explicit constants in the relaxed parameter class
\((\theta_0',L')\).

\paragraph{Relation to iter 25 sub-case (c) and to the iter-23 stability cascade.}
The iter-25 conjectural general theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} has a three-sub-case proof
outline (supplement \Cref{app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25},
``Proof outline (not a proof)'' paragraph). Sub-cases~(a) and~(b)
(direct inclusion \(K\subseteq K'\) and reverse inclusion
\(K'\subseteq K\)) are proved rigorously at iter 25 via
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}. Sub-case~(c) (generic
common refinement \(K_*\) with \(K,K'\subseteq K_*\)) is
conjectural at iter 25; the iter-26 roadmap identifies it as
item~(R2). The present iter-29 subsection addresses sub-case~(c)
by providing an explicit candidate \(K_*\) (the union-Delaunay
refinement) and proving the vertex-inclusion property. The
remaining obstruction (that the union-Delaunay refinement is
\emph{not in general} a simplicial sub-complex refinement of
\(K\) or \(K'\), because a triangle of \(K\) may be subdivided
by an interior vertex of \(V_{K'}\)) is documented honestly. The
present iteration therefore does \emph{not} unconditionally close
iter-25 sub-case~(c); it provides a candidate construction whose
vertex set is correct but whose simplicial structure may differ
from \(K\) or \(K'\). The three-step composition theorem
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} is \emph{conditional} on
the residual sub-conjecture that a Delaunay-preserving
sub-complex refinement exists, which is exhibited as
plausible (e.g., via barycentric subdivision followed by
edge-flip Delaunay repair, with parameter degradation bounded
explicitly) but not proved in iter 29.

\begin{definition}[Union-Delaunay refinement]
\label{def:union_delaunay_iter29}
Let \((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma,g,K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two
quasi-uniform Delaunay triangulations of the same closed oriented
Riemannian surface \((\Sigma,g)\) with the same Delaunay parameter
pair \((\theta_0,L)\) (\Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}). Let
\(V_K=K_0\) and \(V_{K'}=K'_0\) denote the vertex sets of \(K\)
and \(K'\) respectively, regarded as finite subsets of
\(\Sigma\). The \emph{union vertex set} is
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:union_vertex_set_iter29}
V\;:=\;V_K\cup V_{K'}\;\subseteq\;\Sigma,
\qquad |V|\;\le\;|V_K|+|V_{K'}|.
\end{equation}
The \emph{union-Delaunay refinement} \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) of the pair
\((K,K')\) is the (geodesic) Delaunay triangulation of the
finite vertex set \(V\subseteq\Sigma\): the simplicial 2-complex
whose \(0\)-skeleton is \(V\), whose edges connect two vertices
\(p,q\in V\) whenever the geodesic segment \(pq\subseteq\Sigma\)
together with the empty-circumdisk condition produces a Delaunay
edge, and whose \(2\)-cells are the geodesic triangles satisfying
the empty-circumdisk property.
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Convention on the Delaunay triangulation of \(V\)]
\label{rem:delaunay_existence_iter29}
The existence and uniqueness (up to ties on cocircular
quadruples) of the Delaunay triangulation of a finite vertex set
on a closed oriented Riemannian surface of bounded
sectional curvature is the standard generalization of the
planar Delaunay triangulation via local empty-circumdisk
conditions on geodesic triangles, as used in iter 23 to define
\(\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) (\Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}
and its convention remarks). The ambient surface metric \(g\)
is held fixed; the triangulation \(K_*\) depends on \((g,V)\)
only. The sub-complex inclusion \(V\subseteq V_{K_*}\) and the
cardinality bound \(|V|\le|V_K|+|V_{K'}|\) of
\eqref{S-eq:union_vertex_set_iter29} are immediate from the
definition; the parameter pair \((\theta_0',L')\) of \(K_*\) is
the subject of
\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}.
\end{remark}

\begin{lemma}[Vertex-inclusion property of the union-Delaunay refinement]
\label{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}
Let \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two quasi-uniform
Delaunay triangulations of the same closed oriented Riemannian
surface \((\Sigma,g)\), and let \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) be the
union-Delaunay refinement of \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}.
Then:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\emph{(\roman*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item \emph{Vertex-set inclusion:}
    \(V_K\subseteq V_{K_*}\) and \(V_{K'}\subseteq V_{K_*}\)
    as subsets of \(\Sigma\).
  \item \emph{Cardinality bound:}
    \(|V_{K_*}|\le|V_K|+|V_{K'}|\), with equality iff
    \(V_K\cap V_{K'}=\emptyset\).
  \item \emph{Well-definedness and Delaunay property:}
    \(K_*\) is well-defined up to the standard cocircular-tie
    convention, and every \(2\)-simplex \(\tau\in(K_*)_2\)
    satisfies the empty-circumdisk Delaunay condition relative to
    the vertex set \(V=V_{K_*}\).
\end{enumerate}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}]
\emph{(i).} By \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}, the vertex set
of \(K_*\) is \(V_{K_*}=V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\). Inclusion of either
factor into the union is set-theoretic.

\emph{(ii).} The cardinality bound is a standard inclusion-exclusion
identity:
\(|V_K\cup V_{K'}|=|V_K|+|V_{K'}|-|V_K\cap V_{K'}|\le
|V_K|+|V_{K'}|\), with equality iff \(V_K\cap V_{K'}=\emptyset\).

\emph{(iii).} The Delaunay triangulation of a finite vertex set
\(V\subseteq\Sigma\) on a closed oriented Riemannian surface of
bounded sectional curvature is well-defined up to ties on
cocircular quadruples (the same convention used in iter 23 to
define \(\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) via the local
empty-circumdisk condition;
\Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}); the empty-circumdisk
Delaunay condition is local on each \(2\)-simplex and is the
defining property of \(K_*\). The convention is fixed by an
arbitrary but consistent tie-breaking rule (e.g., lexicographic
on vertex labels).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Parameter relaxation under union-Delaunay refinement]
\label{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}
Let \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) and let \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\)
be the union-Delaunay refinement of
\Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}. Let \(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\)
denote the minimal geodesic distance between vertices of \(V_K\)
and vertices of \(V_{K'}\),
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:dmin_iter29}
d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\;:=\;\min_{p\in V_K,\,q\in V_{K'},\,p\ne q}d_g(p,q).
\end{equation}
Then \(K_*\) lies in a relaxed quasi-uniform Delaunay class
\(\mathcal D(\theta_0',L')\) with parameters
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:theta_relax_iter29}
\theta_0'\;\ge\;\theta_0\cdot\min\Bigl\{1,\;\tfrac{d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})}{2L\,\overline{\ell}_{\max}(K,K')}\Bigr\},
\qquad
L'\;\le\;L\cdot\Bigl(1+\tfrac{2\,\overline{\ell}_{\max}(K,K')}{d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})}\Bigr),
\end{equation}
where \(\overline{\ell}_{\max}(K,K'):=\max(\ell_{\max}(K),\ell_{\max}(K'))\)
is the maximum edge length across the two triangulations, and
\(\theta_0',L'\) are the angle and edge-ratio parameters of the
relaxed Delaunay class
\(\mathcal D(\theta_0',L')\) of \Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}]
The union-Delaunay refinement \(K_*\) has vertices in
\(V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\); the edge lengths of \(K_*\) are bounded
above by the larger of the two: any edge of \(K_*\) with both
endpoints in \(V_K\) is shorter than the largest edge of \(K\)
that fits in the same Delaunay neighborhood (similarly for
\(V_{K'}\)). For an edge of \(K_*\) connecting a vertex in
\(V_K\setminus V_{K'}\) to a vertex in \(V_{K'}\setminus V_K\),
the edge length is bounded above by
\(\overline{\ell}_{\max}(K,K')(1+\tfrac{2\overline{\ell}_{\max}}{d_{\min}})\)
(triangle inequality), and bounded below by
\(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\). Hence the edge-ratio is at most
\(L'\le L(1+2\overline{\ell}_{\max}/d_{\min})\).
For the angle bound, a Delaunay 2-simplex of \(K_*\) with
vertices \(p\in V_K\), \(q\in V_K\) or \(V_{K'}\), and
\(r\in V_{K'}\) has its smallest angle at the vertex opposite
its longest edge; by the inscribed-angle bound on the
circumdisk, the smallest angle is bounded below by
\(\theta_0\cdot\min(1,d_{\min}/(2L\overline{\ell}_{\max}))\).
The bound is tightest when \(d_{\min}\to 0\) (the relaxed angle
\(\theta_0'\) is then proportional to \(d_{\min}\) and may be
much smaller than \(\theta_0\)). The bound is loosest when
\(d_{\min}\ge 2L\overline{\ell}_{\max}\) (the relaxed angle is
\(\theta_0'\ge\theta_0\), i.e., no degradation).

The bound \eqref{S-eq:theta_relax_iter29} is sharp in the
following sense: if \(K\) and \(K'\) share no vertices and
\(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\) is small relative to
\(\overline{\ell}_{\max}\), then \(K_*\) contains very thin
triangles with one short edge of length
\(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\) and one long edge of length
\(\overline{\ell}_{\max}\), and the minimal angle of such a
triangle is proportional to
\(d_{\min}/\overline{\ell}_{\max}\), which may be arbitrarily
small.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Honest assessment of the parameter degradation]
\label{rem:union_delaunay_param_honest_iter29}
The parameter relaxation
\eqref{S-eq:theta_relax_iter29} is \emph{not uniform} across the
Delaunay class \(\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\): the relaxed angle
\(\theta_0'\) depends on the minimal inter-vertex distance
\(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\) between \(V_K\) and \(V_{K'}\), and may
degrade to \(0\) as \(K,K'\) approach a common limit (the
``small-perturbation'' regime). For a generic pair
\((K,K')\) with \(d_{\min}\) of the same order as the typical
edge length of \(K\) and \(K'\), the relaxation is mild:
\(\theta_0'\ge\theta_0/(2L)\) and
\(L'\le L(1+2L)\le 4L^2\), both depending only on
\((\theta_0,L)\). For a singular pair with
\(d_{\min}\to 0\), the relaxation is severe and
\(\theta_0'\to 0\); in this regime the union-Delaunay refinement
is degenerate and any analytic statement (Combes--Thomas,
Schur-test) carries constants that blow up as
\(d_{\min}\to 0\). The iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} is stated for
Gromov--Hausdorff-close triangulations
\((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma',g',K')\); in the small-perturbation
regime \(d_{\mathrm{GH}}\to 0\), the union of vertex sets
\(V_K\cup V_{K'}\) is approximately \(V_K\) (or \(V_{K'}\)),
and \(d_{\min}\) may still be small relative to
\(\overline{\ell}_{\max}\). The parameter degradation is
therefore an \emph{inherent} feature of the union-Delaunay
construction in the small-perturbation regime, not an artefact
of the proof, and the iter-23 stability constants
\(C_*(\theta_0',L')\) inherit this degradation.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Sub-complex-refinement obstruction --- honest, not proved]
\label{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}
The iter-25 inclusion-coherence lemma
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} requires \(K\subseteq K_*\)
as a \emph{simplicial sub-complex}: each simplex of \(K\) must
be \emph{literally} a simplex of \(K_*\), with the same vertices
and the same orientation
(\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}). The union-Delaunay
refinement \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) of
\Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29} provides only the
\emph{vertex-set inclusion} \(V_K\subseteq V_{K_*}\)
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}); it is \emph{not} in
general a sub-complex refinement of \(K\) (or of \(K'\)). The
obstruction is concrete: a \(2\)-simplex
\(\tau=[v_1,v_2,v_3]\in K_2\) may contain in its open interior a
vertex \(p\in V_{K'}\setminus V_K\); the Delaunay
triangulation \(K_*\) of \(V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\) then subdivides
\(\tau\) into three smaller triangles
\(\{[v_1,v_2,p],[v_2,v_3,p],[v_3,v_1,p]\}\), none of which is the
original \(\tau\); the original \(\tau\) is therefore \emph{not}
a simplex of \(K_*\).

Consequently the iter-25 inclusion-coherence lemma
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} \emph{does not directly
apply} to the pair \(K\subseteq K_*\) (resp.\ \(K'\subseteq K_*\)).
A residual \emph{sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture}
is therefore required to close the iter-25 sub-case~(c)
via the union-Delaunay construction:

\medskip
\noindent
\textbf{Sub-conjecture (sub-complex refinement of \(K_*\)).}
There exists a further refinement \(\widetilde K_*\) of the
union-Delaunay triangulation \(K_*\) such that both \(K\) and
\(K'\) are simplicial sub-complexes of \(\widetilde K_*\) in the
sense of \Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}, and
\(\widetilde K_*\in\mathcal D(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\)
lies in a (further-relaxed) quasi-uniform Delaunay class with
parameters \(\widetilde\theta_0\ge\theta_0/C\),
\(\widetilde L\le C\cdot L^2\) for an explicit constant
\(C=C(\theta_0,L,d_{\min},\overline{\ell}_{\max})\).

\medskip
\noindent
The sub-conjecture is plausible by the following construction:
take the barycentric refinement
\(K^{\mathrm{bary}}\) of \(K\) (a true simplicial refinement
with \(K\) as a sub-complex of \(K^{\mathrm{bary}}\)) and
similarly \(K'^{\mathrm{bary}}\) of \(K'\); take a common
sub-complex refinement of \(K^{\mathrm{bary}}\) and
\(K'^{\mathrm{bary}}\) by intersecting their 2-simplices on
the universal cover; finally repair to Delaunay via a finite
sequence of edge flips (each preserves the simplicial sub-complex
structure on the affected stars only). The detailed verification
that the edge-flip repair preserves the sub-complex inclusion
property is multi-week research and is not carried out at iter 29;
it is the residual obstruction. The flat-torus case
(\(\theta_0=\pi/3,L=1\)) admits a canonical sub-complex
refinement by halving each equilateral triangle along a median;
the general curved case is the open sub-conjecture.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Three-step composition via union-Delaunay refinement (conditional)]
\label{thm:three_step_composition_iter29}
\textbf{[Conditional: holds modulo
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}.]}
Let \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two quasi-uniform
Delaunay triangulations of the same closed oriented Riemannian
surface \((\Sigma,g)\), and let \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) be the
union-Delaunay refinement of \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}.
Assume the sub-complex-refinement sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}: a further
refinement \(\widetilde K_*\in\mathcal D(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\)
of \(K_*\) exists such that both \(K\) and \(K'\) are simplicial
sub-complexes of \(\widetilde K_*\). Let
\(V_K=V_{K\hookrightarrow\widetilde K_*}\) and
\(V_{K'}=V_{K'\hookrightarrow\widetilde K_*}\) be the inclusion
partial isometries of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}, and let
\(W=V_K\,V_{K'}^*:\Hil(K')\to\Hil(K)\) be the composed partial
isometry.

Then the cap-product operator satisfies the Schur-bounded
coherence identity
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:three_step_coherence_iter29}
S_K\;-\;W\,S_{K'}\,W^*\;=\;\Delta_W,
\qquad
\|\Delta_W\|\;\le\;C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
\bigl(|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K|+|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K'|\bigr),
\end{equation}
with explicit constant
\(C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
=6\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\).
Conditionally, this closes sub-case~(c) of
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} for the pair \((K,K')\) with
relaxed parameters \((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} (conditional)]
The proof is a three-step composition assembling iter-28's general
QI coherence \Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24} with
iter-25's inclusion coherence
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}.

\emph{Step (1): Inclusion coherence for \(K\subseteq\widetilde K_*\).}
By the sub-conjecture hypothesis, \(K\) is a simplicial sub-complex
of \(\widetilde K_*\) in the sense of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}. The iter-25
inclusion-coherence lemma \Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}
applied to the pair \(K\subseteq\widetilde K_*\) gives
\(V_K^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K=S_K+\Delta_V^K\) with
\(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V^K)\subseteq\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\)
and \(\|\Delta_V^K\|\le 2\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\).

\emph{Step (2): Inclusion coherence for \(K'\subseteq\widetilde K_*\).}
By the same sub-conjecture hypothesis, \(K'\) is also a simplicial
sub-complex of \(\widetilde K_*\). The iter-25 inclusion-coherence
lemma applied to the pair \(K'\subseteq\widetilde K_*\) gives
\(V_{K'}^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_{K'}=S_{K'}+\Delta_V^{K'}\) with
\(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V^{K'})\subseteq\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K'\)
and \(\|\Delta_V^{K'}\|\le 2\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\).
