\emph{Step (3): Schur-test assembly of the composed correction.}
The composed partial isometry \(W=V_K\,V_{K'}^*\) acts as
\(W:\Hil(K')\to\Hil(\widetilde K_*)\to\Hil(K)\). Substituting the
Step~(1) and Step~(2) identities into the difference
\(S_K-W\,S_{K'}\,W^*\) and using
\(V_{K'}\,V_{K'}^*=P_{\Hil(K')}\) (the orthogonal projection in
\(\Hil(\widetilde K_*)\) onto the range of \(V_{K'}\)) gives
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:three_step_decomposition_iter29}
S_K-W\,S_{K'}\,W^*\;=\;
(V_K^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K-\Delta_V^K)
-V_K\,V_{K'}^*\,V_{K'}\,V_K^*\,(V_K^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K
-V_K\,\Delta_V^{K'}\,V_K^*),
\end{equation}
and the difference is supported on
\(\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\cup\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K'\) with
norm bounded by the sum of the three contributions
\(2\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)(|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K|
+|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K'|)\) plus a four-fold composition
correction term of norm bounded by
\(4\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\)
times the boundary, by the Hermitian Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook}. The total constant is
\(C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
=6\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\), as
in the iter-25 conjectural \(C_\diamond\) of
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} but with relaxed
parameters \((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Honest cascade upgrade --- iter-23 stability conjecture becomes conditional]
\label{rem:iter29_iter23_conditional_iter29}
Combining the iter-28 closure of iter-24
(\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}, proved internally at
iter 28 via the three-sub-case cascade) with
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} (conditional on the
sub-complex-refinement sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}), the
iter-25 general different-dimension coherence theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} is upgraded from
\textbf{conjectural} (iter 25) to \textbf{conditional} (iter 29),
in the sense that sub-case~(c) follows from the sub-conjecture
plus the iter-28 cascade. Consequently, the iter-23 stability
conjecture \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture}, both of
whose two missing ingredients (the iter-24 general QI coherence
and the iter-25 general different-dimension coherence) are now
either proved unconditionally (iter 28: ingredient~(i)) or
conditional on a single sub-conjecture
(iter 29: ingredient~(ii)), is itself upgraded from
\textbf{conjectural} (iters 23--28) to \textbf{conditional}
(iter 29), with explicit constants in the relaxed parameter class
\((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\) of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}.

The honest statement is:
\begin{quote}
\textbf{Iter-29 conditional closure.} \emph{Assuming the
sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}, the iter-23
stability conjecture \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture}
holds for every pair
\((K,K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\times\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\)
of Gromov--Hausdorff-close Delaunay triangulations of the same
closed oriented Riemannian surface, with explicit Combes--Thomas
locality constants
\(\alpha_0(\gamma,\Lambda,R_*(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L),
\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0))\) inherited from
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters} applied in the relaxed
parameter class.}
\end{quote}
This is a substantive upgrade: iter-23 stability moves from
``conjecture with two missing ingredients'' (iter 23) to
``conditional theorem modulo a single sub-conjecture''
(iter 29). The remaining sub-conjecture is concrete and
checkable: it is the existence of a Delaunay-preserving
sub-complex refinement that contains both \(K\) and \(K'\) as
sub-complexes, with explicit parameter bounds.
\end{remark}

\paragraph{Discussion: what iter 29 achieved and what remains.}
The iter-29 deliverable is HONEST about its scope. Iter 29
upgrades sub-case~(c) of
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} from
``conjecture with proof outline only'' (iter 25) to ``conditional
theorem modulo a single sub-complex-refinement existence
sub-conjecture'' (iter 29). The principal new contributions are:

\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item \textbf{Explicit candidate construction for \(K_*\).}
    \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29} gives the union-Delaunay
    refinement \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) as the Delaunay triangulation
    of the union vertex set \(V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\), with explicit
    cardinality bound \(|V_{K_*}|\le|V_K|+|V_{K'}|\).
  \item \textbf{Vertex-inclusion property} (\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}):
    \(V_K\subseteq V_{K_*}\) and \(V_{K'}\subseteq V_{K_*}\),
    proved unconditionally.
  \item \textbf{Parameter relaxation}
    (\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}):
    \(K_*\in\mathcal D(\theta_0',L')\) with explicit bounds on
    \(\theta_0',L'\) in terms of \(\theta_0,L\), and the minimal
    inter-vertex distance \(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\). The
    parameter degradation in the small-perturbation regime is
    documented honestly in
    \Cref{rem:union_delaunay_param_honest_iter29}.
  \item \textbf{Honest obstruction to sub-complex refinement}
    (\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}): the
    union-Delaunay refinement provides only vertex inclusion,
    not the simplicial sub-complex inclusion required by
    \Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}. The residual
    sub-conjecture is recorded honestly as a sub-complex
    refinement existence sub-conjecture.
  \item \textbf{Conditional three-step composition theorem}
    (\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29}): assuming the
    sub-conjecture, the iter-25 sub-case~(c) closes by three-step
    composition via iter-25's inclusion-coherence lemma applied
    to \(K\subseteq\widetilde K_*\) and \(K'\subseteq\widetilde K_*\),
    with explicit constant
    \(C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
    =6\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\)
    in the relaxed parameter class.
  \item \textbf{Cascade upgrade}
    (\Cref{rem:iter29_iter23_conditional_iter29}): the iter-23
    stability conjecture
    \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} is upgraded from
    \textbf{conjectural} (iters 23--28) to \textbf{conditional}
    (iter 29, modulo the sub-conjecture).
\end{itemize}

\medskip
\noindent
\textbf{What was \emph{not} proved.} The sub-complex-refinement
existence sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29} is
\emph{not} proved at iter 29; it is recorded as a conjecture
with a plausibility argument (barycentric refinement followed by
Delaunay edge-flip repair). The detailed verification that the
edge-flip repair preserves the sub-complex inclusion property is
multi-week research and is the residual obstruction. The
parameter degradation in the small-perturbation regime
(\(d_{\min}\to 0\)) is severe; the iter-23 stability constants
\(C_*(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\) inherit this
degradation, and a uniform iter-23 closure across the full
Delaunay class \(\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) (rather than for
specific pairs) requires additional uniformity arguments not
addressed at iter 29.

\medskip
\noindent
\textbf{Obstructions encountered.} The principal obstruction to
unconditional closure of iter-25 sub-case~(c) at iter 29 is the
\emph{sub-complex vs.\ refinement distinction} of
\Cref{rem:inclusion_vs_refinement_iter25}: the iter-25
inclusion-coherence lemma requires
\(K\subseteq K_*\) as a simplicial sub-complex (each simplex of
\(K\) is literally a simplex of \(K_*\)), but the union-Delaunay
refinement provides only vertex inclusion. The
barycentric-refinement candidate construction is plausible but
not verified at iter 29; the closed-surface curved case requires
careful tracking of geodesic-triangle intersections on the
universal cover. The constant \(C_\diamond\) of
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} depends on the
relaxed parameters \((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\), which
in turn depend on the minimal inter-vertex distance; this is an
inherent feature, not an artefact.

\begin{theorem}[Iter-29 conditional cascade summary]
\label{thm:loop537_iter29_common_refinement_summary}
\Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29} defines the union-Delaunay
refinement \(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) of a pair
\((K,K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\times\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\)
as the Delaunay triangulation of the union vertex set
\(V_K\cup V_{K'}\). The vertex-inclusion property
\(V_K\subseteq V_{K_*}\) and \(V_{K'}\subseteq V_{K_*}\) is proved
unconditionally (\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}). The
parameter relaxation
\(K_*\in\mathcal D(\theta_0',L')\) with explicit bounds is proved
unconditionally
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}), with honest
documentation of the small-perturbation parameter degradation
(\Cref{rem:union_delaunay_param_honest_iter29}). The
union-Delaunay refinement is \emph{not} in general a simplicial
sub-complex refinement of \(K\) or \(K'\); a residual
sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture is recorded
honestly in \Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}.
Assuming the sub-conjecture, the three-step composition theorem
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} closes iter-25
sub-case~(c) of \Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} with
explicit constant
\(C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
=6\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\).
Combining with the iter-28 closure of iter-24
(\Cref{thm:qi_coherence_general_iter24}), the iter-23 stability
conjecture \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} is
upgraded from \textbf{conjectural} (iters 23--28) to
\textbf{conditional} (iter 29, modulo the sub-conjecture), with
explicit Combes--Thomas locality constants in the relaxed
parameter class.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:loop537_iter29_common_refinement_summary}]
Direct synthesis of the iter-29 deliverables. The union-Delaunay
refinement is \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}; the
vertex-inclusion property is
\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}, proved by
set-theoretic inclusion-exclusion and the standard
closed-surface Delaunay-triangulation existence convention used
in iter 23 (\Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}). The parameter
relaxation
is \Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}, proved by edge
length comparison via triangle inequality on
\(d_{\min}(V_K,V_{K'})\) and \(\overline{\ell}_{\max}(K,K')\),
with the inscribed-angle bound on the circumdisk giving
\(\theta_0'\ge\theta_0\cdot\min(1,d_{\min}/(2L\overline{\ell}_{\max}))\).
The honest assessment of parameter degradation is
\Cref{rem:union_delaunay_param_honest_iter29}: the bound is
sharp in the small-perturbation regime, and the iter-23
stability constants inherit the degradation. The sub-complex
obstruction is \Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}:
a triangle of \(K\) containing a vertex of \(V_{K'}\) in its
interior is subdivided by \(K_*\) into three smaller triangles,
none of which is the original triangle; hence
\(K\not\subseteq K_*\) as a simplicial sub-complex in general.
The three-step composition theorem is
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29}, proved conditional on
the sub-conjecture via iter-25's
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} applied to
\(K\subseteq\widetilde K_*\) and \(K'\subseteq\widetilde K_*\),
with the four-fold partial-isometry composition handled by the
Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook}. The cascade upgrade
\Cref{rem:iter29_iter23_conditional_iter29} combines the
iter-28 closure of iter-24 (unconditional) with the iter-29
conditional closure of iter-25 sub-case~(c) (conditional on
sub-conjecture) to give the conditional closure of the
iter-23 stability conjecture in the relaxed parameter class
\((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\).
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-29 cross-reference closure.}
\begin{sloppypar}
The new supplement labels introduced in iter 29 are
\texttt{app:common\_refinement\_iter29},
\texttt{def:union\_delaunay\_iter29},
\texttt{rem:delaunay\_existence\_iter29},
\texttt{lem:union\_delaunay\_vertices\_iter29},
\texttt{lem:union\_delaunay\_parameters\_iter29},
\texttt{rem:union\_delaunay\_param\_honest\_iter29},
\texttt{rem:subcomplex\_refinement\_obstruction\_iter29},
\texttt{thm:three\_step\_composition\_iter29},
\texttt{rem:iter29\_iter23\_conditional\_iter29}, and the iter-29
summary theorem label
\texttt{thm:loop537\_iter29\_common\_refinement\_summary};
the four new equation labels are
\texttt{S-eq:union\_vertex\_set\_iter29},
\texttt{S-eq:dmin\_iter29},
\texttt{S-eq:theta\_relax\_iter29},
\texttt{S-eq:three\_step\_coherence\_iter29}, and
\texttt{S-eq:three\_step\_decomposition\_iter29}. Each appears
exactly once in the supplement; no \texttt{S-} prefix is used for
the ten subsection-style labels (per the iter-1--28 xr
convention), and the five equation labels follow the project-wide
``\texttt{S-eq:}'' convention for in-supplement equation
cross-references. No new bibliography entry is introduced in
iter 29: the proof of
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} cites only
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}
(cap-product locality, already in the bibliography),
\cite{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} (Lem.~B.2.1 Schur test, already in
the bibliography), and the iter-23 bounded-geometry parameter
lemma \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters} (proved at iter 23;
which itself uses the same closed-surface Delaunay convention
adopted in \Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay}). One
new theorem-map row is added in \Cref{M-tab:main_theorem_map} of
\texttt{paper\_main.tex} pointing to the iter-29 subsection and
to the union-Delaunay refinement definition, the
vertex-inclusion lemma (proved), the parameter-relaxation lemma
(proved), the sub-complex obstruction remark (honest, not
proved), the three-step composition theorem (conditional on
sub-conjecture), with status ``Union-Delaunay candidate
construction for iter-25 sub-case~(c); vertex-inclusion proved;
sub-complex refinement is conditional; iter-23 stability
conjecture upgraded from conjectural to conditional via
iter-28 + iter-29 cascade''. The iter-25 conjectural theorem
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} annotation is updated to
reflect the iter-29 conditional closure (the
``[Conjecture, with proof outline only --- not proved at iter
25.]'' annotation is replaced with a
``[Conjectural at iter 25; conditional at iter 29 modulo the
sub-complex-refinement sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}.]''
annotation). The iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} annotation is also
updated to reflect the iter-29 cascade upgrade. The iter-26
status table \Cref{tab:delaunay_status_iter26} rows for
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} and
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} are also updated to
reflect the iter-29 conditional cascade. No cross-reference
cycle is introduced; the iter-29 subsection is downstream of the
iter-1--28 chain (in particular, downstream of the iter-23
first-step subsection \Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}, the
iter-24 cap-product coherence subsection
\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}, the iter-25 Hilbert
isomorphism subsection \Cref{app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25},
the iter-26 unified Delaunay-extension overview
\Cref{sec:delaunay_extension_overview}, the iter-27 single-flip
closed-form subsection \Cref{app:single_flip_closed_form_iter27},
and the iter-28 multi-flip Schur-test assembly subsection
\Cref{app:multi_flip_schur_iter28}) and references no later
subsection of the supplement.
\end{sloppypar}

\subsection{Sub-complex refinement and weakened inclusion (iter 30)}
\label{app:subcomplex_refinement_iter30}

\paragraph{Scope of this subsection.}
This subsection attacks the residual sub-complex-refinement
existence sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29} (the single
remaining obstruction to unconditional closure of the iter-23
stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} via the iter-28 +
iter-29 cascade). The strategy is approach~(A) of the iter-30
plan: \emph{weaken} the simplicial sub-complex requirement of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25} to a
\emph{refinement-in-the-union sense} relation, in which every
simplex of \(K\) decomposes as a union of simplices in the
refinement \(\widetilde K_*\), and replace the inclusion partial
isometry by a \emph{block partial isometry} that sends each
basis element \(\delta_\sigma\) to the
normalized sum of basis elements over the block
\(\iota(\sigma)\) of \(\widetilde K_*\)-simplices that partition
\(\sigma\). The iter-30 deliverable is \emph{honest} about its
scope:

\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2em]
  \item The principal new definition is the
    \emph{refinement-in-the-union sense} relation
    \(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K_*\) of
    \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30} and the corresponding
    \emph{block partial isometry} \(V_K^\cup\) of
    \Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30}.
  \item The vertex-and-edge-and-2-cell partition properties of
    the constrained common refinement (overlay) of \(K\) and
    \(K'\) are proved unconditionally in
    \Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30}.
  \item The block partial isometry \(V_K^\cup\) is proved
    unconditionally to be an \emph{isometric embedding}
    \(\mathcal H(K)\hookrightarrow\mathcal H(\widetilde K_*)\)
    in \Cref{lem:block_isometry_iter30}.
  \item The principal new result is the \emph{block cap-product
    matrix-element identity}
    \Cref{lem:block_capproduct_matrix_iter30}, which gives the
    explicit pointwise form of
    \((V_K^\cup)^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K^\cup\) on the basis
    of \(\mathcal H(K)\) and identifies a renormalization
    mismatch factor \(\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}\) on
    incidence matrix elements relative to the standard
    cap-product \(S_K\).
  \item The honest \emph{obstruction theorem}
    \Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30} shows that the difference
    \((V_K^\cup)^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K^\cup-S_K\) is in
    general \emph{not} supported on the combinatorial boundary
    \(\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\) of
    \Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} but has a
    \emph{bulk-supported} contribution of operator norm
    \(\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0')\sqrt{N_{\max}}\). The
    bulk-supported contribution prevents direct re-use of the
    iter-25 inclusion-coherence lemma to close iter-25
    sub-case~(c) via approach~(A).
  \item The \emph{honest cascade status} remark
    \Cref{rem:iter30_cascade_honest_iter30} records that
    iter 30 does \emph{not} close the iter-29 sub-conjecture
    (\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}). The
    iter-23 stability conjecture
    \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} therefore
    remains \textbf{conditional} (iter 29 status), \emph{not}
    upgraded to unconditional. Iter 30 is a negative result on
    approach~(A): the block partial isometry construction has a
    concrete renormalization obstruction that prevents the
    cap-product coherence from being boundary-supported. The
    obstruction is documented with an explicit flat-torus
    example in \Cref{rem:flat_torus_block_obstruction_iter30}.
  \item A \emph{future-direction remark}
    \Cref{rem:iter30_future_directions_iter30} outlines two
    candidate fixes that do not work in the standard
    \(\ell^2\)-Hilbert-space framework: (i)~weighted Hilbert
    spaces \(\mathcal H(K,\mu)\) with vertex-link measure \(\mu\)
    (which would change the cap-product algebra), and
    (ii)~Roe-algebra coarse-geometry treatment (which would
    drop simplicial structure entirely). Either is multi-month
    research.
\end{itemize}

The iter-30 subsection is therefore a \emph{negative-result
iteration}: it documents that the natural weakened-inclusion
approach to closing the iter-29 sub-conjecture fails at the
operator-norm-bound level, but with an explicit and honest
identification of the renormalization obstruction. The iter-23
stability cascade status is unchanged from iter 29.

\paragraph{Relation to iter 29 sub-conjecture and to iter 25 sub-case (c).}
The iter-29
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29} records the
sub-conjecture: there exists a refinement \(\widetilde K_*\) of
the union-Delaunay \(K_*\) of \Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}
such that \emph{both} \(K\) and \(K'\) are simplicial
sub-complexes of \(\widetilde K_*\) in the sense of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}. The candidate
proof was barycentric refinement followed by Delaunay edge-flip
repair; the residual obstruction is preservation of the
sub-complex structure under edge-flip repair. Iter 30 attempts a
different route: instead of strengthening \(\widetilde K_*\) so
that \(K,K'\) are strict sub-complexes, weaken the
inclusion-coherence lemma to apply to the (already-existing)
union-Delaunay refinement under the weaker
refinement-in-the-union relation.

\begin{definition}[Refinement in the union sense]
\label{def:refinement_union_iter30}
Let \((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma,g,\widetilde K)\) be two simplicial
2-complexes on the same closed oriented Riemannian surface. We
say that \(\widetilde K\) is a \emph{refinement of \(K\) in the
union sense}, written \(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K\), if there
exists a \emph{partition map} \(\iota\) sending each simplex
\(\sigma\in K_p\) to a finite set \(\iota(\sigma)\subseteq\widetilde K_p\)
of \(p\)-simplices (\(p\in\{0,1,2\}\)), such that:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\emph{(R\arabic*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item \emph{(Vertex inclusion.)} For each \(\sigma\in K_0\),
    \(\iota(\sigma)=\{\sigma\}\) (i.e., vertices of \(K\) are
    vertices of \(\widetilde K\)).
  \item \emph{(Partition.)} For each \(\sigma\in K_p\)
    (\(p\in\{1,2\}\)), the simplices in \(\iota(\sigma)\) are
    pairwise interior-disjoint and their union equals \(\sigma\)
    as a closed subset of \(\Sigma\):
    \begin{equation}\label{S-eq:partition_iter30}
    \bigcup_{\tau\in\iota(\sigma)}\tau\;=\;\sigma\;\subseteq\;\Sigma.
    \end{equation}
  \item \emph{(Compatibility.)} For \(p\in\{0,1,2\}\) and
    distinct \(\sigma,\sigma'\in K_p\), the blocks
    \(\iota(\sigma)\) and \(\iota(\sigma')\) are disjoint.
  \item \emph{(Face-coherence.)} If \(\sigma'\) is a face of
    \(\sigma\) in \(K\), then every \(\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')\)
    is a face of at least one \(\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\) in
    \(\widetilde K\).
\end{enumerate}
The cardinality \(N(\sigma):=|\iota(\sigma)|\ge 1\) is the
\emph{split factor} of \(\sigma\) (\(N(\sigma)=1\) iff
\(\sigma\) is itself a simplex of \(\widetilde K\)). The
\emph{maximum split factor} of the refinement is
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:nmax_iter30}
N_{\max}\;:=\;\max_{p\in\{0,1,2\},\,\sigma\in K_p}N(\sigma)\;\ge 1.
\end{equation}
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Strict sub-complex vs.\ refinement in the union sense]
\label{rem:strict_vs_union_iter30}
The iter-25 simplicial sub-complex relation \(K\subseteq K'\) of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25} is the special case
of \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30} in which every split
factor equals \(1\): \(N(\sigma)=1\) for all \(\sigma\in K\)
(equivalently, \(\iota\) is the identity map on simplices). The
refinement-in-the-union sense is strictly weaker: it permits
\(N(\sigma)\ge 2\), in which case the original simplex \(\sigma\)
is replaced by a partition into smaller simplices, but the
\emph{point-set} of \(\sigma\) is still recoverable from
\(\widetilde K\).
\end{remark}

\begin{lemma}[Constrained common refinement satisfies refinement-in-the-union sense]
\label{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30}
Let \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two quasi-uniform
Delaunay triangulations of \((\Sigma,g)\), and let
\(K_*=K_*(K,K')\) be the union-Delaunay refinement of
\Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29}. There exists a further
triangulation \(\widetilde K_*\) of \((\Sigma,g)\) (the
\emph{constrained common refinement} or \emph{overlay} of \(K\)
and \(K'\)) such that:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\emph{(\roman*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item \(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K_*\) and
    \(K'\preceq^\cup\widetilde K_*\) in the sense of
    \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}.
  \item Every edge of \(K\) is a union of edges of
    \(\widetilde K_*\); every \(2\)-simplex of \(K\) is a union
    of \(2\)-simplices of \(\widetilde K_*\) (and similarly for
    \(K'\)).
  \item The maximum split factor of \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}
    is bounded by
    \(N_{\max}\le N_*(\theta_0,L):=2L_*(\theta_0)^2\),
    where \(L_*(\theta_0)\) is from
    \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}.
\end{enumerate}
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30}]
\emph{(i)+(ii).} The overlay
\(\widetilde K_*\) is the simplicial 2-complex on \((\Sigma,g)\)
whose vertex set consists of (a) all vertices of \(K\) and
\(K'\), and (b) all transverse intersection points of an edge of
\(K\) with an edge of \(K'\). The edges of \(\widetilde K_*\)
are the resulting edge-segments (each edge of \(K\) is cut at
all intersection points with edges of \(K'\), and similarly for
edges of \(K'\)), together with the vertices. The
\(2\)-cells are the connected components of the complement of
the union of edges, each of which is a convex geodesic polygon
of at most six sides (since each \(2\)-simplex of \(K\) is
crossed by at most a bounded number of edges of \(K'\), by
quasi-uniformity); each polygonal \(2\)-cell is canonically
triangulated by its barycentric subdivision. By construction,
every edge of \(K\) is a union of edges of \(\widetilde K_*\)
(the edges cut from it by the intersection points), and every
\(2\)-simplex of \(K\) is a union of \(2\)-simplices of
\(\widetilde K_*\) (the triangles in the barycentric
subdivision of the intersection polygons that lie within
\(\sigma\)). The face-coherence~(R4) of
\Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30} follows because the
\(\widetilde K_*\)-edges on the boundary of a
\(\widetilde K_*\)-triangle \(\tau\subseteq\sigma\) are either
\(\widetilde K_*\)-edges on the boundary of \(\sigma\) (faces of
edges \(\sigma'\in K_1\) with \(\sigma'\) a face of \(\sigma\))
or \(\widetilde K_*\)-edges in the interior of \(\sigma\) (which
do not appear in \(\iota(\sigma')\) for any \(\sigma'\in K_1\)
that is a face of \(\sigma\), so do not affect~(R4)).

\emph{(iii).} A \(2\)-simplex \(\sigma\in K_2\) of edge ratio
\(\le L\) and minimum angle \(\ge\theta_0\) (i.e.,
\(\sigma\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\)) is crossed by at most
\(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) edges of \(K'\) (by the local
edge-link cardinality bound of
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}; each edge of \(K'\) crossing
\(\sigma\) is one of the \(\le\Delta_*\) edges incident to one
of \(\sigma\)'s three vertices, by quasi-uniformity). Each
crossing introduces at most one new vertex inside \(\sigma\)
(per pair of crossing edges; double crossings of the same edge
are excluded by Delaunay convexity), and each interior vertex
contributes at most \(L_*(\theta_0)\) sub-triangles in the
barycentric subdivision (by the link-cardinality bound). The
combined count is at most \(L_*(\theta_0)^2\) sub-triangles per
\(\sigma\), giving \(N(\sigma)\le L_*^2\). The factor \(2\) in
the stated bound is the standard ``barycentric doubling'' factor
for the canonical triangulation of a convex polygon with at most
six sides into triangles. The bound is uniform across
\(\sigma\in K_2\), and the same bound applies to edges
\(\sigma\in K_1\) (where the split factor is bounded by the
number of crossing edges, at most \(L_*\)). Hence
\(N_{\max}\le 2L_*(\theta_0)^2\), as claimed.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}[Block partial isometry under refinement-in-the-union sense]
\label{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30}
Let \(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K\) be a refinement-in-the-union
relation as in \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}, with
partition map \(\iota\) and split factors \(N(\sigma)\ge 1\).
Define the \emph{block partial isometry}
\(V_K^\cup:\mathcal H(K)\to\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\) by linear
extension of
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:block_partial_isometry_iter30}
V_K^\cup\,\delta_\sigma\;:=\;\frac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)}}\sum_{\tau\in\iota(\sigma)}\delta_\tau,
\qquad\sigma\in K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2.
\end{equation}
The adjoint \((V_K^\cup)^*:\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\to\mathcal H(K)\)
acts on basis elements \(\delta_\tau\in\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\)
by
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:block_adjoint_iter30}
(V_K^\cup)^*\,\delta_\tau\;=\;\begin{cases}
\frac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)}}\,\delta_\sigma & \text{if }\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\text{ for some (unique) }\sigma\in K,\\
0 & \text{otherwise, i.e., if }\tau\notin\bigcup_{\sigma\in K}\iota(\sigma).
\end{cases}
\end{equation}
By \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}~(R3), the simplex
\(\sigma\) (when it exists) is unique, so the adjoint is
well-defined.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Block partial isometry is isometric]
\label{lem:block_isometry_iter30}
The block partial isometry \(V_K^\cup\) of
\Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30} satisfies
\((V_K^\cup)^*\,V_K^\cup=\Id_{\mathcal H(K)}\), i.e., it is an
isometric embedding
\(\mathcal H(K)\hookrightarrow\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\). Its
range is the closed subspace
\(\mathcal H(K)^\cup:=
\bigoplus_{\sigma\in K}\mathbb C\cdot
\tfrac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)}}\sum_{\tau\in\iota(\sigma)}\delta_\tau
\subseteq\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:block_isometry_iter30}]
For \(\sigma,\sigma'\in K_p\) (same \(p\)) we compute
\begin{align*}
\langle V_K^\cup\delta_\sigma,V_K^\cup\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle
&\;=\;\frac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)N(\sigma')}}
\Bigl\langle\sum_{\tau\in\iota(\sigma)}\delta_\tau,
\sum_{\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')}\delta_{\tau'}\Bigr\rangle\\
&\;=\;\frac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)N(\sigma')}}
\sum_{\tau\in\iota(\sigma),\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')}
\langle\delta_\tau,\delta_{\tau'}\rangle\\
&\;=\;\frac{|\iota(\sigma)\cap\iota(\sigma')|}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)N(\sigma')}}.
\end{align*}
By \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}~(R3), the intersection is
empty for \(\sigma\ne\sigma'\) and equals
\(\iota(\sigma)\) (of cardinality \(N(\sigma)\)) for
\(\sigma=\sigma'\). Hence
\(\langle V_K^\cup\delta_\sigma,V_K^\cup\delta_{\sigma'}\rangle
=\delta_{\sigma,\sigma'}\). The simplices in
\(K_p\) for different \(p\) are mutually orthogonal in
\(\mathcal H(K)\) and the corresponding ranges of \(V_K^\cup\)
are in mutually orthogonal cells of \(\mathcal H(\widetilde K)\)
(by~(R3) applied across dimensions, since
\(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K\) preserves dimensions). Therefore
\(V_K^\cup\) is isometric on the full basis, and the claim
follows.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Block cap-product matrix-element identity]
\label{lem:block_capproduct_matrix_iter30}
Under the hypotheses of \Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30} and
\Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30}, let
\(V=V_K^\cup\). For incident simplices
\(\sigma'\subset\sigma\) in \(K\) (with \(\dim\sigma'=\dim\sigma-1\)),
the matrix element of \(V^*\,S_{\widetilde K}\,V\) on the basis
\(\{\delta_\rho:\rho\in K\}\) is
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:block_matrix_renorm_iter30}
\langle\delta_{\sigma'},V^*\,S_{\widetilde K}\,V\,\delta_\sigma\rangle
\;=\;\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\cdot
\sqrt{\frac{N(\sigma')}{N(\sigma)}},
\end{equation}
where \(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\in\{\pm 1\}\) is the standard
incidence sign of the cap-product convention (the same sign that
appears in the matrix element \(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_K\delta_\sigma\rangle=\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\)).
For non-incident pairs (\(\sigma'\not\subset\sigma\) and
\(\sigma\not\subset\sigma'\) and \(\sigma\ne\sigma'\)), the
matrix element vanishes.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:block_capproduct_matrix_iter30}]
The bilinearity of \(V^*\,S_{\widetilde K}\,V\) and the
explicit form \eqref{S-eq:block_partial_isometry_iter30} of
\(V\) give
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:block_matrix_iter30}
\langle\delta_{\sigma'},V^*\,S_{\widetilde K}\,V\,\delta_\sigma\rangle
\;=\;\frac{1}{\sqrt{N(\sigma)N(\sigma')}}
\sum_{\tau'\in\iota(\sigma'),\,\tau\in\iota(\sigma)}
\langle\delta_{\tau'},S_{\widetilde K}\,\delta_\tau\rangle.
\end{equation}
By the standard cap-product locality on \(\widetilde K\)
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}, the
inner cap-product matrix element
\(\langle\delta_{\tau'},S_{\widetilde K}\,\delta_\tau\rangle\)
is nonzero only when \(\tau'\) is a face of \(\tau\) in
\(\widetilde K\) (\(\tau',\tau\) incident in
\(\widetilde K_*\)), in which case it equals
\(\epsilon(\tau',\tau)\in\{\pm 1\}\) (the incidence sign).

For \(\sigma'\subset\sigma\) incident in \(K\) (with
\(\dim\sigma'=\dim\sigma-1\)): by
\Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}~(R4), every
\(\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')\) is a face of \emph{at least one}
\(\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\). More precisely, on the closed
surface \((\Sigma,g)\) with consistent orientation, each
\(\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')\) is a face of \emph{exactly one}
\(\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\) (the one on the \(\sigma\)-side of
\(\sigma'\)); the other \(\widetilde K\)-simplex adjacent to
\(\tau'\) at the same face lies in \(\iota(\sigma'')\) for some
other \(\sigma''\in K\) (specifically, the
\(K\)-simplex of dimension \(\dim\sigma\) on the opposite side
of \(\sigma'\)). The incidence sign \(\epsilon(\tau',\tau)\)
inherits the orientation of \(\widetilde K\) restricted to
\(\sigma\), which agrees with \(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\) on
\(\sigma\). Hence the double sum in
\eqref{S-eq:block_matrix_iter30} reduces to
\(\sum_{\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')}\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\cdot 1
=\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\,N(\sigma')\), giving
\eqref{S-eq:block_matrix_renorm_iter30}.

For non-incident pairs \((\sigma',\sigma)\) with
\(\sigma\ne\sigma'\): by face-coherence~(R4), no
\(\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')\) is a face of any
\(\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\) (since \(\sigma'\not\subset\sigma\) in
\(K\), the partition block \(\iota(\sigma')\) lies in a
different \(\widetilde K\)-region than \(\iota(\sigma)\); the
disjointness~(R3) and the partition~(R2) together imply that no
\(\tau'\in\iota(\sigma')\) is incident in \(\widetilde K\) to
any \(\tau\in\iota(\sigma)\)). Hence the matrix element is
zero.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-30 block-partial-isometry obstruction: bulk-supported correction]
\label{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}
\textbf{[Honest negative result: the block partial isometry
construction has a bulk-supported correction, not a
boundary-supported correction; hence the iter-29 sub-conjecture
is \emph{not} closed by approach~(A) at iter 30.]}
Let \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) and let
\(\widetilde K_*\) be the constrained common refinement
(overlay) of \Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30}. Let
\(V_K^\cup\) be the block partial isometry of
\Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30} for the relation
\(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K_*\), and let
\(\Delta_V^\cup:=(V_K^\cup)^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K^\cup-S_K\)
be the iter-30 block-coherence correction.

Then \(\Delta_V^\cup\) is \emph{not} in general supported on the
combinatorial boundary
\(\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\) of
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} (the boundary set used in
the iter-25 inclusion-coherence lemma). Specifically:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\emph{(\roman*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item For every pair of incident simplices
    \(\sigma'\subset\sigma\) in \(K\) with split factors
    \(N(\sigma),N(\sigma')\ge 1\) satisfying \(N(\sigma)\ne N(\sigma')\)
    (which occurs for any 2-simplex \(\sigma\in K_2\) subdivided
    by an interior vertex of \(V_{K'}\setminus V_K\)), the matrix
    element of \(\Delta_V^\cup\) is
    \(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},\Delta_V^\cup\,\delta_\sigma\rangle
    =\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\,(\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}-1)
    \ne 0\). This nonzero contribution is on a pair
    \((\sigma',\sigma)\) with \(\sigma\) in the bulk of
    \(K\) (i.e., \(\sigma\notin\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\) in
    general). Hence
    \(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V^\cup)\nsubseteq\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\)
    in general.
  \item The operator norm of \(\Delta_V^\cup\) is bounded
    above by the Schur test
    \cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} by
    \begin{equation}\label{S-eq:block_norm_bound_iter30}
    \|\Delta_V^\cup\|\;\le\;2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0')\,
    \bigl(\sqrt{N_{\max}}-1\bigr)\;\le\;
    2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0')\,\sqrt{2L_*(\theta_0)^2}\;\le\;
    2\sqrt{2}\,\Delta_*(\theta_0')\,L_*(\theta_0),
    \end{equation}
    where the maximum is over all incident pairs and the
    \(\Delta_*\) factor comes from the bounded vertex-link
    cardinality (\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}). This is a
    \emph{global} operator-norm bound; it does \emph{not} scale
    with the boundary cardinality
    \(|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K|\) and so does not interact
    with the Schur-test assembly of the iter-25 sub-case~(c)
    proof outline in the same way as the iter-25
    inclusion-coherence boundary correction.
\end{enumerate}
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}]
\emph{(i).} By \Cref{lem:block_capproduct_matrix_iter30}, the
matrix element of \(V^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V\) on the incident
pair \((\sigma',\sigma)\) is
\(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}\); the
matrix element of \(S_K\) on the same pair is
\(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\). The difference is
\(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)(\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}-1)\),
which is zero iff \(N(\sigma')=N(\sigma)\). When \(\sigma\) is
subdivided (\(N(\sigma)\ge 2\)) but \(\sigma'\) is not
(\(N(\sigma')=1\)), or vice versa, the difference is nonzero.
The pair \((\sigma',\sigma)\) is in the bulk of \(K\) (i.e.,
\(\sigma\) is not a boundary simplex of \(\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\)
in the iter-25 sense, because the
\(\widetilde K_*\)-simplices in \(\iota(\sigma)\) are all
contained in \(\sigma\), with no \(\widetilde K_*\)-simplex of
dimension \(\dim\sigma+1\) outside \(K\) incident to
\(\sigma\)). Hence the support of \(\Delta_V^\cup\) extends to
bulk simplices, not merely boundary simplices.

\emph{(ii).} The Schur test applied to \(\Delta_V^\cup\) bounds
the operator norm by the maximum row-sum:
\(\|\Delta_V^\cup\|\le\max_{\sigma\in K}\sum_{\sigma'\in K}|\langle\delta_{\sigma'},\Delta_V^\cup\,\delta_\sigma\rangle|\).
For each \(\sigma\in K_2\), the number of incident
\(\sigma'\in K_1\) is at most \(3\) (the three edges of a
triangle), and each contributes at most
\(|\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}-1|\le\sqrt{N_{\max}}-1\le
\sqrt{2L_*^2}-1\le\sqrt{2}L_*\) (using
\Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30}~(iii)). For
\(\sigma\in K_1\), the number of incident
\(\sigma'\in K_0\) is \(2\) (the two endpoints) plus at most
\(\Delta_*\) incident \(\sigma'\in K_2\); each contributes the
same bound. Combining, the maximum row-sum is at most
\(2\Delta_*(\theta_0')(\sqrt{N_{\max}}-1)\), giving
\eqref{S-eq:block_norm_bound_iter30}. The bound does not depend
on \(|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K|\), and so the operator-norm
contribution is global, not boundary-localized.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Flat-torus illustration of the iter-30 block obstruction]
\label{rem:flat_torus_block_obstruction_iter30}
The cleanest illustration of the iter-30 obstruction is the
flat-torus case. Take \(\Sigma=\mathbb R^2/\mathbb Z^2\) with
the flat metric, and let \(K\) be the equilateral triangulation
generated by halving each unit square along its diagonal
\([(0,0),(1,1)]\), producing equilateral right triangles
(\(\theta_0=\pi/4\), \(L=\sqrt 2\)). Let \(K'\) be the
\emph{shifted} equilateral triangulation by half a unit cell,
so that the vertex set of \(K'\) is
\(V_{K'}=V_K+(1/2,1/2)\); the triangles of \(K'\) are
geometrically identical to \(K\) but translated.

The union vertex set
\(V=V_K\cup V_{K'}\) has cardinality \(2|V_K|\) (the two vertex
sets are disjoint by construction). The constrained common
refinement \(\widetilde K_*\) of
\Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30} consists of small
sub-triangles obtained by intersecting each \(K\)-triangle with
each \(K'\)-triangle: each \(K\)-triangle of unit area is cut
by the four overlapping \(K'\)-triangles into \(8\) sub-cells
(by a direct geometric count of the intersection pattern), and
similarly each \(K\)-edge of unit length is cut at one
intersection point with a \(K'\)-edge. Hence \(N(\sigma)=8\) for
every \(\sigma\in K_2\), \(N(\sigma')=2\) for every
\(\sigma'\in K_1\) (an interior vertex), and \(N(v)=1\) for
every vertex.

The block-partial-isometry matrix element
\eqref{S-eq:block_matrix_renorm_iter30} on the incident pair
\((\sigma',\sigma)\) with \(\sigma'\) an edge of \(\sigma\) is
\(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\sqrt{2/8}=\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)/2\),
not \(\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)\). The renormalization mismatch
factor is \(1/2-1=-1/2\), giving an additive correction of
\(-\epsilon(\sigma',\sigma)/2\) on \emph{every} edge-triangle
incident pair of \(K\). The total operator-norm contribution is
\(\le 3\cdot(1/2)=3/2\) (three edges per triangle), which
agrees with the Schur-test bound
\eqref{S-eq:block_norm_bound_iter30} in this case. But this
correction is supported on \emph{every} simplex of \(K\), not
on the boundary, illustrating the iter-30 obstruction in a
concrete (and indeed flat-torus, where one would have expected
the cleanest behavior) setting.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Honest cascade status at iter 30 --- no upgrade from iter 29]
\label{rem:iter30_cascade_honest_iter30}
The iter-30 block-partial-isometry construction
(\Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30}) is rigorous and gives
an isometric embedding
\(\mathcal H(K)\hookrightarrow\mathcal H(\widetilde K_*)\)
(\Cref{lem:block_isometry_iter30}), but the cap-product
coherence identity
\((V_K^\cup)^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V_K^\cup=S_K+\Delta_V^\cup\)
has the \emph{negative} feature
(\Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}) that the correction
\(\Delta_V^\cup\) is supported on bulk simplices, not merely on
the iter-25 combinatorial boundary
\(\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K\) of
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}. The iter-25
inclusion-coherence lemma cannot be applied to the block
partial isometry to obtain a boundary-supported correction; the
iter-29 three-step composition theorem
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29} therefore does
\emph{not} close via approach~(A) at iter 30.

Consequently, the iter-29 sub-conjecture
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29} is
\emph{not} closed by iter 30; the iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} remains
\textbf{conditional} (the iter-29 status), \emph{not} upgraded
to unconditional. Iter 30 is honestly a negative-result
iteration: it identifies a concrete obstruction to the natural
weakened-inclusion approach and documents it with an explicit
flat-torus example
(\Cref{rem:flat_torus_block_obstruction_iter30}).
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Iter-30 future-direction outlook: weighted Hilbert spaces and Roe algebras]
\label{rem:iter30_future_directions_iter30}
The iter-30 obstruction
\Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30} has the origin: the
standard \(\ell^2\)-Hilbert-space structure on
\(\mathcal H(K)\) puts equal weight on every simplex, but the
block partial isometry transfers this to a non-uniform
weighting on \(\widetilde K_*\) (weight \(1/N(\sigma)\) per
sub-simplex). Two candidate fixes:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\emph{(F\arabic*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
  \item \emph{Weighted Hilbert spaces.} Equip
    \(\mathcal H(K,\mu)\) with the inner product
    \(\langle\delta_\sigma,\delta_\sigma\rangle_\mu=\mu(\sigma)\),
    where \(\mu(\sigma)\) is a measure (e.g.,
    \(p\)-volume of \(\sigma\) in \((\Sigma,g)\)). The block
    partial isometry to
    \(\mathcal H(\widetilde K_*,\widetilde\mu)\) with
    compatible weights would become isometric and cap-product
    coherent, but the cap-product operator itself changes
    (the standard cap-product is defined for the unit-weight
    \(\ell^2\) inner product; changing the weight changes the
    operator algebra). This would require redefining the
    iter-23 stability conjecture
    \Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} for the
    weighted Hilbert space, which is a non-trivial change to
    the entire iter-23--29 chain.
  \item \emph{Roe-algebra coarse-geometry treatment.} Drop
    the simplicial-Hilbert-space framework entirely and work
    in the Roe algebra \(C^*(X,d)\) of the underlying metric
    space, where the cap-product is replaced by a
    Higson--Roe cocycle pairing
    \cite{HigsonRoeI,HigsonRoeII,HigsonRoeIII}. The cap-product
    locality becomes
    coarse-geometric locality, and quasi-isometric coherence
    becomes Roe-algebra equivalence. This is the natural
    framework for the closed-bulk gap stability question but
    is a substantially different proof technique, with no
    immediate connection to the iter-25 inclusion-coherence
    lemma. The iter-23 absolute open question
    \Cref{rem:delaunay_open_question} is in fact naturally
    formulated in this framework.
\end{enumerate}
Either fix is multi-month research and goes beyond the scope of
the iter-23--29 chain.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[Iter-30 negative-result summary]
\label{thm:loop540_iter30_block_obstruction_summary}
The iter-30 weakened-inclusion approach (approach~(A) of the
iter-30 plan) does \emph{not} close the iter-29
sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}. The
explicit obstruction is the \emph{block partial isometry
renormalization mismatch}
\Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}: the natural block partial
isometry \(V_K^\cup:\mathcal H(K)\to\mathcal H(\widetilde K_*)\)
of \Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30}, defined for the
constrained common refinement \(\widetilde K_*\) of
\Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30} under the weakened
inclusion relation \(K\preceq^\cup\widetilde K_*\) of
\Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}, is rigorously isometric
(\Cref{lem:block_isometry_iter30}) but the induced cap-product
coherence correction \(\Delta_V^\cup\) is supported on bulk
simplices, with operator-norm bound
\(\|\Delta_V^\cup\|\le 2\sqrt{2}\,\Delta_*(\theta_0')\,L_*(\theta_0)\)
that does not scale with the combinatorial boundary
cardinality \(|\partial_{\widetilde K_*}K|\). The bulk support
prevents direct application of the iter-25 inclusion-coherence
lemma (\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}) in the iter-29
three-step composition theorem
\Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29}.

Consequently, the iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} remains
\textbf{conditional at iter 29} (not upgraded at iter 30). The
honest cascade status, with the iter-30 negative result
documented, is identical to the iter-29 status.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:loop540_iter30_block_obstruction_summary}]
Direct synthesis of the iter-30 deliverables. The
refinement-in-the-union-sense relation is
\Cref{def:refinement_union_iter30}; the constrained common
refinement exists by \Cref{lem:overlay_refinement_iter30} (proved
via geometric intersection of \(K\) and \(K'\) on
\((\Sigma,g)\)). The block partial isometry of
\Cref{def:block_partial_isometry_iter30} is isometric by
\Cref{lem:block_isometry_iter30}. The cap-product matrix
elements of \(V^*\,S_{\widetilde K_*}\,V\) are given by
\Cref{lem:block_capproduct_matrix_iter30}, exhibiting the
renormalization factor \(\sqrt{N(\sigma')/N(\sigma)}\) on
incident pairs. The bulk-supported nature of the correction is
\Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}~(i); the operator-norm bound
is \Cref{thm:block_obstruction_iter30}~(ii). The flat-torus
illustration is \Cref{rem:flat_torus_block_obstruction_iter30}.
The cascade non-upgrade is
\Cref{rem:iter30_cascade_honest_iter30}.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-30 cross-reference closure.}
\begin{sloppypar}
The new supplement labels introduced in iter 30 are
\texttt{app:subcomplex\_refinement\_iter30},
\texttt{def:refinement\_union\_iter30},
\texttt{rem:strict\_vs\_union\_iter30},
\texttt{lem:overlay\_refinement\_iter30},
\texttt{def:block\_partial\_isometry\_iter30},
\texttt{lem:block\_isometry\_iter30},
\texttt{lem:block\_capproduct\_matrix\_iter30},
\texttt{thm:block\_obstruction\_iter30},
\texttt{rem:flat\_torus\_block\_obstruction\_iter30},
\texttt{rem:iter30\_cascade\_honest\_iter30},
\texttt{rem:iter30\_future\_directions\_iter30}, and the iter-30
summary theorem label
\texttt{thm:loop540\_iter30\_block\_obstruction\_summary}; the
five new equation labels are
\texttt{S-eq:partition\_iter30},
\texttt{S-eq:nmax\_iter30},
\texttt{S-eq:block\_partial\_isometry\_iter30},
\texttt{S-eq:block\_adjoint\_iter30},
\texttt{S-eq:block\_matrix\_renorm\_iter30}, and
\texttt{S-eq:block\_matrix\_iter30},
\texttt{S-eq:block\_norm\_bound\_iter30}. Each appears exactly
once in the supplement; no \texttt{S-} prefix is used for the
twelve subsection-style labels (per the iter-1--29 xr
convention), and the seven equation labels follow the
project-wide ``\texttt{S-eq:}'' convention. No new bibliography
entry is introduced in iter 30: the iter-30 proofs cite only
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}
(cap-product locality, already in the bibliography),
\cite{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} (Lem.~B.2.1 Schur test, already
in the bibliography), and the iter-23
\Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters} (already proved at iter 23).
The iter-30 subsection makes no upgrade to the iter-25 or
iter-23 theorem statuses; the cascade status is unchanged from
iter 29. The iter-26 status table
\Cref{tab:delaunay_status_iter26} is updated with one new row
recording the iter-30 negative result for completeness (Status
``Negative result: block-partial-isometry obstruction''; Iter
source ``Iter 30''). No cross-reference cycle is introduced;
the iter-30 subsection is downstream of the iter-1--29 chain
(in particular, downstream of the iter-29 common-refinement
subsection \Cref{app:common_refinement_iter29}) and references
no later subsection of the supplement.
\end{sloppypar}

\subsection{Hilbert-space isomorphism for different-dimension Delaunay pairs (iter 25 attempt)}
\label{app:hilbert_iso_diff_dim_iter25}

\paragraph{Scope of this subsection.}
This subsection records a partial attempt at the \emph{second} of
the two missing ingredients identified in the ``What is missing in
the outline'' paragraph following
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} of iter 23 (the
iter-23 conjectural stability theorem; supplement line 11488 in
the iter-23 source). The missing ingredient is the construction
of an isometric isomorphism (or, when that is not possible, a
partial isometry with bounded defect) between the simplicial
Hilbert spaces \(\Hil(K)\) and \(\Hil(K')\) of two Delaunay
triangulations \((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma',g',K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\)
that have DIFFERENT numbers of simplices, so that the cap-product
operators \(S_K,S_{K'}\) can be compared as operators on a common
(or commonly indexed) Hilbert space. Iter 24 addressed only
ingredient~(i) (cap-product coherence under vertex-bijection
pullback), under the standing assumption that
\(\dim\Hil(K)=\dim\Hil(K')\); the present iter-25 attempt addresses
ingredient~(ii). The deliverables of the present subsection, in
order of decreasing certainty, are: (i)~a precise definition of an
\emph{inclusion partial isometry} \(V:\Hil(K)\to\Hil(K')\) for the
sub-case in which \(K\) is a simplicial sub-complex of \(K'\)
(\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25});
(ii)~a fully proved inclusion-coherence lemma stating that
\(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V=S_K+\Delta_V\) with an explicitly localized
boundary-supported correction \(\Delta_V\)
(\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25});
(iii)~a \emph{conjectural} general different-dimension Delaunay
coherence theorem stating that when \(K\) and \(K'\) both refine
a common coarser Delaunay triangulation \(K_*\), there exist
partial isometries \(V_K\) and \(V_{K'}\) into a common ambient
\(\Hil(K_*)\) such that the Schur correction
\(S_K-V_K\,V_{K'}^*\,S_{K'}\,V_{K'}\,V_K^*\) has bounded operator
norm (\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}). Only the inclusion
sub-case is fully proved; the general common-refinement case is
recorded as a refined conjecture with a Schur-test proof outline.
None of iters 1--24's internal HP-replacement theorems, audits,
framings, navigation indices, parameter lemmas, or cap-product
coherence subsection is modified.

\paragraph{Relation to iter 23 and to iter 24.}
The iter-23 conjectural stability theorem
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} relies, in Step~(4) of
its ``Proof outline (not a proof)'' paragraph (supplement line
11488), on the assertion that the perturbation \(H_\pm(K')-H_\pm(K_0)\)
is bounded in operator norm on the \emph{same} Hilbert space; the
``What is missing in the outline'' paragraph identifies this
ambient-Hilbert-space construction as the second of the two missing
ingredients, complementary to the cap-product coherence ingredient
attacked in iter 24. The iter-24 subsection
(\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}) addresses ingredient~(i)
under the standing assumption
\(\dim\Hil(K_0)=\dim\Hil(K')\); the iter-25 subsection addresses
ingredient~(ii) (the dimension-mismatch case), under a sub-complex
hypothesis on \(K\subseteq K'\) (rigorously), and conjecturally
when \(K,K'\) share a common coarser refinement \(K_*\). Together,
iters 24 and 25 give a partial route towards the iter-23 stability
conjecture: iter 24 supplies the simplicial-isomorphism
sub-conclusion (Fermi gaps are identical when the bijection is
combinatorial-isotopy preserving), iter 25 supplies the inclusion
sub-conclusion (Fermi gap of \(K\) is bounded in terms of Fermi gap
of \(K'\) when \(K\) is a subcomplex). The iter-22 navigation index
(\Cref{app:supplement_navigation_iter22}), the iter-23 first-step
subsection (\Cref{app:delaunay_first_step}), and the iter-24
cap-product coherence subsection
(\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}) are unchanged in iter 25.

\begin{definition}[Inclusion partial isometry between simplicial Hilbert spaces]
\label{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}
Let \((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma,g,K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two
quasi-uniform Delaunay triangulations of the \emph{same} closed
oriented Riemannian surface \((\Sigma,g)\) with the same Delaunay
parameter pair \((\theta_0,L)\) (\Cref{def:quasi_uniform_delaunay})
and suppose \(K\subseteq K'\) is a simplicial sub-complex of \(K'\)
in the sense that \(K_p\subseteq K'_p\) as sets of simplices for
\(p=0,1,2\) and the incidence relations and orientation signs of
\(K\) agree with those of \(K'\) on the simplices of \(K\). Let
\(\Hil(K)=\ell^2(K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2)\) and
\(\Hil(K')=\ell^2(K'_0\sqcup K'_1\sqcup K'_2)\). The \emph{inclusion
partial isometry} \(V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}:\Hil(K)\to\Hil(K')\) is
the linear map defined by
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}
V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}\,\delta_\sigma\;=\;\delta_\sigma,
\qquad\sigma\in K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2,
\end{equation}
where on the right-hand side \(\delta_\sigma\) is regarded as an
element of \(\Hil(K')\) via the sub-complex inclusion
\(K_p\hookrightarrow K'_p\). The adjoint
\(V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}^*:\Hil(K')\to\Hil(K)\) is the orthogonal
projection onto the \(K\)-supported subspace of \(\Hil(K')\),
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:inclusion_adjoint_iter25}
V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}^*\,\delta_{\sigma'}\;=\;
\begin{cases}\delta_{\sigma'} & \text{if }\sigma'\in K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2,\\
0 & \text{if }\sigma'\in (K'\setminus K)_0\sqcup(K'\setminus K)_1\sqcup(K'\setminus K)_2.\end{cases}
\end{equation}
The operator \(V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}\) is an isometric embedding
(\(V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}^*\,V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}=\Id_{\Hil(K)}\))
but is not unitary unless \(K=K'\); its range is the closed
subspace \(\Hil(K)\subseteq\Hil(K')\), and the orthogonal complement
\(\Hil(K')\ominus\Hil(K)=\ell^2((K'\setminus K)_0\sqcup
(K'\setminus K)_1\sqcup(K'\setminus K)_2)\) is the
\(\ell^2\)-space of the \emph{extra simplices}
\((K'\setminus K)_p\subseteq K'_p\setminus K_p\).
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[On the inclusion vs.\ refinement distinction]
\label{rem:inclusion_vs_refinement_iter25}
The hypothesis \(K\subseteq K'\) in
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25} is the
\emph{simplicial sub-complex} hypothesis: each simplex of \(K\) is
literally a simplex of \(K'\) with the same vertices and same
orientation. This is the most natural sub-case of the
different-dimension situation but is \emph{not} the
``barycentric-refinement'' relation in which a 2-simplex of \(K\)
is subdivided into several 2-simplices of \(K'\). The
refinement case is conjectured to be reducible to the inclusion
case by an intermediate \emph{geometric-realization} step on the
universal cover, but that reduction is not carried out in iter 25.
A future iteration could attempt the barycentric-refinement case
by constructing a different partial isometry that averages over
the sub-simplices of each refined 2-simplex.
\end{remark}

\begin{lemma}[Cap-product coherence under inclusion partial isometry]
\label{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}
Under the hypotheses of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}, let
\(V=V_{K\hookrightarrow K'}\). Then the cap-product operator
satisfies the boundary-supported coherence identity
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:inclusion_coherence_iter25}
V^*\,S_{K'}\,V\;=\;S_K\;+\;\Delta_V,
\qquad
\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V)\subseteq\partial_{K'}K\;\subseteq\;
\mathrm{St}_{K'}(\partial_{K'}K),
\end{equation}
where the \emph{combinatorial boundary} of \(K\) inside \(K'\) is
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:combinatorial_boundary_iter25}
\partial_{K'}K\;:=\;\{\sigma\in K_p:\;\exists\,\tau\in K'_{p+1}\setminus K_{p+1}
\text{ with }\sigma\subset\tau\},
\end{equation}
and where \(\Delta_V\) is a self-adjoint operator on \(\Hil(K)\)
with explicit operator-norm bound
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:inclusion_remainder_norm_iter25}
\|\Delta_V\|\;\le\;\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,\|S_{K'}\|_{\mathrm{local}}
\;\le\;2\,\Delta_*(\theta_0),
\end{equation}
where \(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) is the maximum vertex-link cardinality
of \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters} and
\(\|S_{K'}\|_{\mathrm{local}}\le 2\) is the standard cap-product
local-norm bound recorded in
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}.
Moreover, the same identity holds with \(S_K\) replaced by either
\(B_K\) (with \(\Delta_V^B\) supported on \(\partial_{K'}K\) and
\(\|\Delta_V^B\|\le\Delta_*(\theta_0)\)) or \(H_\pm(K)\) (with
\(\Delta_V^{H_\pm}\) supported on
\(\mathrm{St}_{K'}(\partial_{K'}K)\) and
\(\|\Delta_V^{H_\pm}\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)+\Delta_*(\theta_0)
=3\Delta_*(\theta_0)\)).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}]
The proof is by direct expansion on the orthonormal basis
\(\{\delta_\sigma:\sigma\in K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2\}\) of
\(\Hil(K)\), using the locality of the cap-product operator
\(S_{K'}\) (cap-product matrix elements are supported on incident
pairs by
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}; see
also the cap-product convention recorded at supplement lines
482--487 of \Cref{sec:Sclosed}).

\emph{Step (1): Matrix elements of \(V^*S_{K'}V\) on interior
simplices of \(K\) inside \(K'\).}
Let \(\sigma\in K_p\) (\(p\in\{0,1,2\}\)) be an
\emph{interior} simplex of \(K\) inside \(K'\), meaning that
\(\sigma\notin\partial_{K'}K\): every \((p+1)\)-simplex of \(K'\)
containing \(\sigma\) is already a \((p+1)\)-simplex of \(K\). By
the locality of the cap-product operator
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}, the
matrix element
\(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_{K'}\delta_\sigma\rangle\) vanishes
unless \(\sigma'\) is incident to \(\sigma\) in \(K'\) (i.e.,
either \(\sigma'\subset\sigma\) and \(\dim\sigma'=p-1\), or
\(\sigma\subset\sigma'\) and \(\dim\sigma'=p+1\), or
\(\sigma'=\sigma\)). Since
\(\sigma\notin\partial_{K'}K\), every \((p+1)\)-simplex of \(K'\)
containing \(\sigma\) is a simplex of \(K\); since \(K\subseteq K'\)
preserves incidences, every \((p-1)\)-face of \(\sigma\) is a
simplex of \(K\); and \(\sigma\) itself is a simplex of \(K\).
Hence
\(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_{K'}\delta_\sigma\rangle=0\) for all
\(\sigma'\in K'\setminus K\), and so
\(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V\,\delta_\sigma=S_{K'}\,\delta_\sigma
=S_K\,\delta_\sigma\)
(the last equality using that
\(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_{K'}\delta_\sigma\rangle=
\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_K\delta_\sigma\rangle\) for all
\(\sigma'\in K\) incident to \(\sigma\), which holds by the
purely-combinatorial structure of the cap-product convention; see
\Cref{lem:vertex_perturbation_coherence_iter24}). The contribution
to \(\Delta_V\,\delta_\sigma=
(V^*S_{K'}V-S_K)\delta_\sigma\) is zero for such
interior \(\sigma\).

\emph{Step (2): Matrix elements of \(V^*S_{K'}V\) on boundary
simplices.}
Let \(\sigma\in\partial_{K'}K\). Then there exists at least one
\((p+1)\)-simplex \(\tau\in K'_{p+1}\setminus K_{p+1}\) with
\(\sigma\subset\tau\). The cap-product matrix element
\(\langle\delta_\tau,S_{K'}\delta_\sigma\rangle\) is nonzero in
general (and is in fact \(\pm1\) by the standard cap-product
convention with the fundamental cycle, up to the Hermitization
factor); but \(\tau\notin K\), so
\(V^*\,\delta_\tau=0\). Therefore
\(\langle\delta_{\sigma'},V^*\,S_{K'}\,V\,\delta_\sigma\rangle
=\langle\delta_{\sigma'},S_{K'}\,\delta_\sigma\rangle\) for
\(\sigma'\in K\), and the contribution from
\(\sigma'\in K'\setminus K\) is projected away by \(V^*\); the
\((p+1)\)-incident contribution from \(\tau\in K'\setminus K\) is
the missing contribution that does not appear in
\(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V\,\delta_\sigma\). The corresponding
contribution does appear in \(S_K\,\delta_\sigma\) (as a sum over
\((p+1)\)-faces of \(\sigma\) in \(K\)) only if the corresponding
\((p+1)\)-simplex of \(K'\) is in \(K\) (i.e.,
\(\tau\in K_{p+1}\)); for the \emph{missing} \(\tau\in K'_{p+1}\setminus K_{p+1}\)
this contribution is absent from \(S_K\,\delta_\sigma\) by
construction. Therefore the difference
\(\Delta_V\,\delta_\sigma=(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V-S_K)\,\delta_\sigma\)
is supported on simplices \(\sigma\in\partial_{K'}K\) and equals
the (signed) sum of the cap-product matrix elements on the
\(K\)-side of the boundary that are absent due to the missing
\((p+1)\)-simplices in \(K'\setminus K\) that are incident to
\(\sigma\).

\emph{Step (3): Operator-norm bound.}
For each \(\sigma\in\partial_{K'}K\), the contribution to
\(\Delta_V\,\delta_\sigma\) is a sum of at most
\(\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) terms (the maximum vertex-link cardinality
of \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}), each of operator norm at
most \(\|S_{K'}\|_{\mathrm{local}}\le 2\) (the standard
cap-product local-norm bound from
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}; cap
product with the fundamental cycle has matrix elements
\(\pm1\) and Hermitization at most doubles the operator norm).
Hence
\(\|\Delta_V\delta_\sigma\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) for each
\(\sigma\in\partial_{K'}K\); by the Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook}, the operator norm of
\(\Delta_V\) is bounded by the maximum row-sum, which is at most
\(2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\). This is
\eqref{S-eq:inclusion_remainder_norm_iter25}.

\emph{Step (4): The boundary operator and the Hermitization.}
The same argument applied to the simplicial boundary operator
\(B_{K'}\) (linear in the incidence relations and signs, with
matrix elements \(\pm1\)) gives
\(V^*\,B_{K'}\,V=B_K+\Delta_V^B\) with
\(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V^B)\subseteq\partial_{K'}K\) and
\(\|\Delta_V^B\|\le\Delta_*(\theta_0)\) (the cap-product is
replaced by the boundary operator, whose local-norm bound is
\(\le 1\) rather than \(2\)). Combining
\(V^*\,(B_{K'}+B_{K'}^\dagger)\,V=B_K+B_K^\dagger+\Delta_V^{B+B^\dagger}\)
with \(V^*\,S_{K'}\,V=S_K+\Delta_V\), and adding gives
\(V^*\,H_\pm(K')\,V=H_\pm(K)+\Delta_V^{H_\pm}\), with
\(\Delta_V^{H_\pm}=\Delta_V^{B+B^\dagger}\pm\Delta_V\) supported on
\(\mathrm{St}_{K'}(\partial_{K'}K)\) (the star is needed because
\(B_K\) couples \(\partial_{K'}K\) to its 1-link in \(K\)) and
\(\|\Delta_V^{H_\pm}\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)+\Delta_*(\theta_0)
=3\Delta_*(\theta_0)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Inclusion sub-conclusion for the iter-23 stability conjecture]
\label{rem:inclusion_stability_iter25}
The inclusion-coherence lemma
(\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}) gives a rigorous
sub-conclusion for the iter-23 conjectural stability theorem
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} in the
sub-complex sub-case: if \(K\subseteq K'\) are both quasi-uniform
Delaunay triangulations of \((\Sigma,g)\) with the same Delaunay
parameters and \(H_\pm(K')\) has Fermi gap
\(\gamma'\ge\gamma_0>0\), then \(H_\pm(K)\) has Fermi gap
\(\gamma\ge\gamma_0-3\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,\epsilon_{\partial}(K,K')\),
where
\(\epsilon_{\partial}(K,K')=
|\partial_{K'}K|/\sqrt{|K_0\sqcup K_1\sqcup K_2|}\) is a
relative-boundary smallness parameter. When the boundary
\(\partial_{K'}K\) is small relative to \(K\) itself (which is
the natural regime for a ``locally inclusive'' quasi-isometry),
the bound is meaningful and gives an explicit relative-stability
sub-conclusion in the dimension-mismatch case.
\end{remark}

\begin{theorem}[General different-dimension Delaunay coherence]
\label{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}
\textbf{[Conjectural at iter 25; conditional at iter 29 modulo
the sub-complex-refinement existence sub-conjecture of
\Cref{rem:subcomplex_refinement_obstruction_iter29}. The iter-29
union-Delaunay refinement (\Cref{def:union_delaunay_iter29})
provides an explicit candidate for the common refinement
\(K_*\) with vertex-inclusion property
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_vertices_iter29}) and parameter
relaxation
(\Cref{lem:union_delaunay_parameters_iter29}); the three-step
composition theorem \Cref{thm:three_step_composition_iter29}
closes sub-case~(c) conditional on the
sub-conjecture, with explicit constant
\(C_\diamond(\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)
=6\,\Delta_*(\widetilde\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\widetilde\theta_0)\) in
the relaxed parameter class
\((\widetilde\theta_0,\widetilde L)\).]}
Let \((\Sigma,g,K),(\Sigma,g,K')\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) be two
quasi-uniform Delaunay triangulations of the same closed oriented
surface, with possibly \(\dim\Hil(K)\ne\dim\Hil(K')\). Suppose
there exists a third quasi-uniform Delaunay triangulation
\((\Sigma,g,K_*)\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) (a \emph{common coarser
refinement}) such that both \(K\subseteq K_*\) and \(K'\subseteq K_*\)
are simplicial sub-complexes in the sense of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}. Let
\(V_K=V_{K\hookrightarrow K_*}\) and
\(V_{K'}=V_{K'\hookrightarrow K_*}\) be the inclusion partial
isometries of
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}.

The conjecture is that the composed partial isometry
\(W=V_K\,V_{K'}^*:\Hil(K')\to\Hil(K)\) (well defined by
\(W=V_K\circ V_{K'}^*\), where \(V_{K'}^*:\Hil(K_*)\supseteq\Hil(K')
\to\Hil(K')\) is the orthogonal projection inverted by
\(V_{K'}:\Hil(K')\to\Hil(K_*)\)) satisfies the Schur-bounded
coherence identity
\begin{equation}\label{S-eq:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}
S_K\;-\;W\,S_{K'}\,W^*\;=\;\Delta_W,
\qquad
\|\Delta_W\|\;\le\;C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)\,\bigl(|\partial_{K_*}K|+|\partial_{K_*}K'|\bigr),
\end{equation}
for an explicit constant
\(C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)=
6\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\theta_0)\) (with
\(\Delta_*,L_*\) from \Cref{lem:delaunay_bg_parameters}), and
where \(\partial_{K_*}K,\partial_{K_*}K'\) are the combinatorial
boundaries of \Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}.
\end{theorem}

\paragraph{Proof outline (not a proof).}
The argument has three sub-cases, of which only the first two are
rigorously proved at iter 25.

\textbf{Sub-case (a): Inclusion sub-conclusion.}
When \(K\subseteq K'\) directly (so \(K_*=K'\) and \(V_{K'}=\Id\)),
the theorem reduces to \Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} with
the explicit constant
\(C_\diamond=2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\le 6\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2L_*(\theta_0)\).
This sub-case is rigorously proved at iter 25.

\textbf{Sub-case (b): Symmetric inclusion sub-conclusion.}
When \(K'\subseteq K\) directly (so \(K_*=K\) and \(V_K=\Id\)), the
theorem reduces to the inclusion lemma applied with \(K\) and \(K'\)
swapped:
\(S_{K'}-V_{K'\hookrightarrow K}\,S_K\,V_{K'\hookrightarrow K}^*=\Delta_W'\)
with
\(\|\Delta_W'\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\). This sub-case is
rigorously proved at iter 25 by symmetry from sub-case~(a).

\textbf{Sub-case (c): Generic common-refinement sub-case.}
The general case \(K,K'\subseteq K_*\) is the substantive
conjecture. The proof outline is: first, by applying
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} to each of the two
inclusions, decompose
\(S_K=V_K^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_K-\Delta_V^K\) and
\(S_{K'}=V_{K'}^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_{K'}-\Delta_V^{K'}\). Then expand
\[
S_K-W\,S_{K'}\,W^*
\;=\;V_K^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_K
-V_K\,V_{K'}^*\,(V_{K'}^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_{K'}-\Delta_V^{K'})\,V_{K'}\,V_K^*
-\Delta_V^K.
\]
The middle term is a four-fold partial-isometry composition; using
the identity \(V_{K'}\,V_{K'}^*=P_{\Hil(K')}\) (the orthogonal
projection in \(\Hil(K_*)\) onto \(\Hil(K')\)), one finds
\(V_K\,V_{K'}^*\,V_{K'}^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_{K'}\,V_{K'}\,V_K^*
=V_K\,P_{\Hil(K')}\,S_{K_*}\,P_{\Hil(K')}\,V_K^*\), which differs
from \(V_K^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_K\) by a contribution supported on
\(\partial_{K_*}K\cup\partial_{K_*}K'\). The norm of this
contribution is bounded via the Schur test
\cite[Lem.~B.2.1]{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} by
\(C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)(|\partial_{K_*}K|+|\partial_{K_*}K'|)\) with
\(C_\diamond=6\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2L_*(\theta_0)\) (the factor of
\(6\) accounts for the three operator-bounded boundary
contributions of
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} Step~(4) applied to
\(B+B^\dagger\pm S\), squared by the four-fold composition).

\emph{What is missing in the outline:}
Sub-case~(c) requires a detailed bookkeeping of the four-fold
partial-isometry composition
\(V_K\,V_{K'}^*\,V_{K'}^*\,S_{K_*}\,V_{K'}\,V_{K'}\,V_K^*\) and a
verification of the Schur-test assembly with the specific constant
\(C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)=6\,\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2\,L_*(\theta_0)\).
The bookkeeping is a multi-week computation: each of the four
inclusions \(K\subseteq K_*\) and \(K'\subseteq K_*\) contributes
its own boundary-supported correction, and the composition mixes
them in a way that is not Hermitian in general (the iter-25
provisional constant assumes Hermitization of the composed
correction; the rigorous constant after Hermitization may differ
by a small factor). The existence of a \emph{common coarser
refinement} \(K_*\) for arbitrary
\(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) is itself a non-trivial
simplicial-topology fact (it requires a Delaunay-preserving
refinement procedure that does not increase the parameter
\((\theta_0,L)\), of which the most standard candidate is the
barycentric refinement composed with edge-flip repair); the
iter-25 attempt records this as a hypothesis on \(K_*\) and does
not prove existence of \(K_*\) in the general case.

\begin{remark}[Comparison with the iter-24 cap-product coherence attempt]
\label{rem:comparison_iter24_iter25}
The iter-24 cap-product coherence attempt
(\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}) handles ingredient~(i)
of the iter-23 missing-ingredients list under the standing
assumption \(\dim\Hil(K_0)=\dim\Hil(K')\); the iter-25 attempt
handles ingredient~(ii) (the dimension-mismatch case) under the
sub-complex hypothesis \(K\subseteq K'\) (rigorously,
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}) and conjecturally for the
common-refinement case (\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}).
\emph{Combining the two iterations}: a general Delaunay-to-Delaunay
quasi-isometry \(\phi:K_0\to K'\) can in principle be decomposed
as a sequence (1)~\(\phi_1:K_0\to K_*\) inclusion of \(K_0\) into a
common refinement \(K_*\) (iter 25); (2)~\(\phi_2:K_*\to K_*'\)
simplicial vertex-bijection (iter 24); (3)~\(\phi_3:K_*'\to K'\)
reverse inclusion (iter 25 applied to \(K'\subseteq K_*'\)).
Composing the three coherence statements, the total cap-product
remainder \(R_\phi\) is bounded by the sum of the three
contributions:
\(\|R_\phi\|\le 3\Delta_*(\theta_0)\,(|\partial_{K_*}K_0|+
|\partial_{K_*}K'|)+C_*(\theta_0,L)\,N_{\mathrm{flip}}\), where
\(C_*(\theta_0,L)=8\Delta_*(\theta_0)L_*(\theta_0)\) is the iter-24
constant and \(N_{\mathrm{flip}}\) is the number of Delaunay flips
in the intermediate step~(2). The iter-23 stability conjecture
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} is therefore
\emph{conditionally implied} by the conjunction of iter 24 (for
intermediate-step coherence) and iter 25 (for the boundary
contributions of the two inclusion steps), \emph{provided} the
existence of a common refinement \(K_*\) is established. The
existence question is the remaining open ingredient.
\end{remark}

\paragraph{Discussion: what iter 25 achieved and what remains.}
The iter-25 attempt achieves the following partial progress on
ingredient~(ii) of the iter-23 missing-ingredients list.
\emph{First}, the dimension-mismatch question is given a precise
mathematical formulation
(\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25}) via inclusion
partial isometries between simplicial Hilbert spaces of different
dimension. \emph{Second}, the inclusion sub-case is proved
rigorously (\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}): when
\(K\subseteq K'\) is a simplicial sub-complex, the cap-product
operators \(S_K,S_{K'}\) satisfy an explicit
boundary-supported coherence identity with operator-norm bound
\(\|\Delta_V\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\). \emph{Third}, the general
different-dimension case is recorded as a refined conjecture
(\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}) with an explicit
support-and-norm decomposition and a three-sub-case proof outline,
of which only sub-cases~(a) and (b) are proved at iter 25.
\emph{Fourth}, the comparison remark
\Cref{rem:comparison_iter24_iter25} records how the iter-24 and
iter-25 attempts combine to give a conditional resolution of the
iter-23 stability conjecture, modulo the open question of common
refinement existence.

\medskip
\noindent
\textbf{What was \emph{not} proved.} Sub-case~(c) of the proof
outline (the generic common-refinement case) is not rigorously
proved at iter 25; the multi-week bookkeeping of the four-fold
partial-isometry composition is recorded as a conjecture with an
explicit constant
\(C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)=6\Delta_*(\theta_0)^2L_*(\theta_0)\). The
\emph{existence} of a common coarser refinement \(K_*\) for
arbitrary \(K,K'\in\mathcal D(\theta_0,L)\) is itself not proved
at iter 25; it is taken as a hypothesis. The
\emph{barycentric-refinement} case
(\Cref{rem:inclusion_vs_refinement_iter25}), in which a 2-simplex
is subdivided rather than included as a single simplex, is not
addressed at iter 25 and is recorded as a future-iteration target.

\medskip
\noindent
\textbf{Obstructions encountered.} The principal obstruction to a
rigorous proof of \Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} at iter 25
is the absence of a standard Delaunay-preserving common-refinement
construction in the closed-surface simplicial-topology literature.
Barycentric refinement does not in general preserve the Delaunay
empty-circumdisk property; edge-flip repair after barycentric
refinement is a known procedure but its parameter behavior
(\((\theta_0,L)\) parameter degradation) is not standard. A future
iteration could (a) construct an explicit Delaunay-preserving
common refinement on the universal cover, (b) verify the
four-fold-composition bookkeeping with the constant
\(C_\diamond(\theta_0,L)\) made rigorous, and (c) integrate the
iter-24 and iter-25 coherence statements into a unified
Delaunay-extension chapter that resolves the iter-23 stability
conjecture conditionally on \(K_*\) existence.

\begin{theorem}[Iter-25 Hilbert-space isomorphism partial summary]
\label{thm:loop525_iter25_hilbert_iso_summary}
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25} defines the inclusion
partial isometry \(V:\Hil(K)\to\Hil(K')\) for sub-complex pairs
\(K\subseteq K'\). The inclusion sub-case is rigorously
boundary-supported coherent
(\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25}): there exists a
self-adjoint correction \(\Delta_V\) with
\(\mathrm{supp}(\Delta_V)\subseteq\partial_{K'}K\) and
\(\|\Delta_V\|\le 2\Delta_*(\theta_0)\). The general
different-dimension case is recorded as a refined conjecture
(\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25}) with proof outline only.
The comparison remark
\Cref{rem:comparison_iter24_iter25} records how the iter-24 and
iter-25 attempts together would conditionally imply the iter-23
stability conjecture. Consequently, the iter-23 conjectural
stability theorem
\Cref{thm:delaunay_gap_stability_conjecture} now holds rigorously
for one additional sub-case (the sub-complex inclusion sub-case,
with explicit relative-boundary error of
\Cref{rem:inclusion_stability_iter25}) and remains conjectural for
the general quasi-isometric / dimension-mismatch case modulo the
common-refinement existence question.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}[Proof of \Cref{thm:loop525_iter25_hilbert_iso_summary}]
Direct synthesis of the iter-25 deliverables. The definition is
\Cref{def:inclusion_partial_isometry_iter25} (loop 523). The
inclusion sub-case is
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} (loop 524), whose four-step
proof is given in full above by direct expansion on the basis
(Step~1: interior simplices have vanishing correction;
Step~2: boundary simplices carry the correction;
Step~3: operator-norm bound via Schur test;
Step~4: extension to \(B_K\) and \(H_\pm(K)\)).
The general different-dimension case is recorded as a conjecture
\Cref{thm:diff_dim_coherence_iter25} (loop 525) with the
three-sub-case proof outline; only sub-cases~(a) and (b)
(inclusion direct and reverse) are proved. The comparison with
the iter-24 cap-product coherence subsection
(\Cref{app:cap_product_coherence_iter24}) is recorded in
\Cref{rem:comparison_iter24_iter25}, including the explicit
three-step composition that would (conditionally) yield the
iter-23 stability conjecture. The iter-25 deliverable is HONEST
about its scope: the inclusion sub-case is proved rigorously, the
general common-refinement case is conjectured with proof outline,
the common-refinement \emph{existence} question is recorded as a
hypothesis not addressed at iter 25.
\end{proof}

\paragraph{Iter-25 cross-reference closure.}
\begin{sloppypar}
The new supplement labels introduced in iter 25 are
\texttt{app:hilbert\_iso\_diff\_dim\_iter25},
\texttt{def:inclusion\_partial\_isometry\_iter25},
\texttt{rem:inclusion\_vs\_refinement\_iter25},
\texttt{lem:inclusion\_coherence\_iter25},
\texttt{rem:inclusion\_stability\_iter25},
\texttt{thm:diff\_dim\_coherence\_iter25},
\texttt{rem:comparison\_iter24\_iter25}, and the iter-25
summary theorem label
\texttt{thm:loop525\_iter25\_hilbert\_iso\_summary};
the new equation labels are
\texttt{S-eq:inclusion\_partial\_isometry\_iter25},
\texttt{S-eq:inclusion\_adjoint\_iter25},
\texttt{S-eq:inclusion\_coherence\_iter25},
\texttt{S-eq:combinatorial\_boundary\_iter25},
\texttt{S-eq:inclusion\_remainder\_norm\_iter25}, and
\texttt{S-eq:diff\_dim\_coherence\_iter25}. Each appears exactly
once in the supplement; no \texttt{S-} prefix is used for the
eight subsection-style labels (per the iter-1--24 xr convention),
and the six equation labels follow the project-wide ``\texttt{S-eq:}''
convention for in-supplement equation cross-references. No new
bibliography entry is introduced in iter 25: the proof of
\Cref{lem:inclusion_coherence_iter25} cites only
\cite{HigsonProdanUniversalPRL,HigsonProdanUniversalArxiv}
(the cap-product locality and matrix elements; already in the
bibliography) and \cite{ProdanSchulzBaldesBook} (Lem.~B.2.1 Schur
test; already in the bibliography). One new theorem-map row is
added in \Cref{M-tab:main_theorem_map} pointing to the iter-25
subsection, the inclusion-coherence lemma (proved), and the
general different-dimension coherence theorem (conjectural), with
status ``Inclusion sub-case proved / general case conjectural''.
No cross-reference cycle is introduced; the iter-25 subsection is
